H735.notes: Transliteration: ʼôrach; Pronunciation: o'-rakh; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H734 (אֹרַח);

H736.notes: Transliteration: ʼôrᵉchâh; Pronunciation: o-rekh-aw'; Derivation: feminine active participle of H732 (אָרַח);

H737.notes: Transliteration: ʼăruchâh; Pronunciation: ar-oo-khaw'; Derivation: feminine passive participle of H732 (אָרַח) (in the sense of appointing);

H738.notes: Transliteration: ʼărîy; Pronunciation: ar-ee'; Derivation: or (prolonged) []; from H717 (אָרָה) (in the sense of violence);

H739.notes: Transliteration: ʼărîyʼêl; Pronunciation: ar-ee-ale'; Derivation: or אֲרִאֵל; from H738 (אֲרִי) and H410 (אֵל);

H740.meaning: Ariel, a symbolical name for Jerusalem, also the name of an Israelite
H740.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrîyʼêl; Pronunciation: ar-ee-ale'; Derivation: the same as H739 (אֲרִיאֵל);

H741.notes: Transliteration: ʼăriʼêyl; Pronunciation: ar-ee-ale'; Derivation: either by transposition for H739 (אֲרִיאֵל) or, more probably, an orthographical variation for H2025 (הַרְאֵל);

H742.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrîyday; Pronunciation: ar-ee-dah'-ee; Derivation: of Persian origin;

H743.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrîydâthâʼ; Pronunciation: ar-ee-daw-thaw'; Derivation: of Persian origin;

H744.notes: Transliteration: ʼaryêh; Pronunciation: ar-yay'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H738 (אֲרִי)

H745.notes: Transliteration: ʼAryêh; Pronunciation: ar-yay'; Derivation: the same as H738 (אֲרִי); lion;

H746.meaning: Arjok, the name of two Babylonians
H746.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂryôwk; Pronunciation: ar-yoke'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H747.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrîyçay; Pronunciation: ar-ee-sah'-ee; Derivation: of Persian origin;

H748.meaning: to be (causative, make) long (literally or figuratively)
H748.kjv: defer, draw out, lengthen, (be, become, make, pro-) long, [phrase] (out-, over-) live, tarry (long).
H748.notes: Transliteration: ʼârak; Pronunciation: aw-rak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H749.notes: Transliteration: ʼărak; Pronunciation: ar-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) properly, corresponding to H748 (אָרַךְ), but used only in the sense of reaching to a given point;

H750.notes: Transliteration: ʼârêk; Pronunciation: aw-rake'; Derivation: from H748 (אָרַךְ);

H751.notes: Transliteration: ʼErek; Pronunciation: eh'-rek; Derivation: from H748 (אָרַךְ); length;

H752.notes: Transliteration: ʼârôk; Pronunciation: aw-roke'; Derivation: from H748 (אָרַךְ);

H753.notes: Transliteration: ʼôrek; Pronunciation: o'rek'; Derivation: from H748 (אָרַךְ);

H754.notes: Transliteration: ʼarkâʼ; Pronunciation: ar-kaw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or אַרְכָּה; (Aramaic), from H749 (אֲרַךְ);

H755.notes: Transliteration: ʼarkubâh; Pronunciation: ar-koo-baw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from an unused root corresponding to H7392 (רָכַב) (in the sense of bending the knee);

H756.meaning: an Arkevite (collectively) or native of Erek
H756.notes: Transliteration: ʼArkᵉvay; Pronunciation: ar-kev-ah'ee; Derivation: (Aramaic) patrial from H751 (אֶרֶךְ);

H757.meaning: an Arkite or native of Erek
H757.notes: Transliteration: ʼArkîy; Pronunciation: ar-kee'; Derivation: patrial from another place (in Palestine) of similar name with H751 (אֶרֶךְ);

H758.meaning: Aram or Syria, and its inhabitants; also the name of the son of Shem, a grandson of Nahor, and of an Israelite
H758.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrâm; Pronunciation: arawm'; Derivation: from the same as H759 (אַרְמוֹן); the highland;

H759.notes: Transliteration: ʼarmôwn; Pronunciation: ar-mone'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be elevated);

H760.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂram Tsôwbâh; Pronunciation: ar-am' tso-baw'; Derivation: from H758 (אֲרָם) and H6678 (צוֹבָא);

H761.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrammîy; Pronunciation: ar-am-mee'; Derivation: patrial from H758 (אֲרָם);

H762.kjv: in the Syrian language (tongue), in Syriac.
H762.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrâmîyth; Pronunciation: ar-aw-meeth'; Derivation: feminine of H761 (אֲרַמִּי);

H763.meaning: Aram of (the) two rivers (Euphrates and Tigris) or Mesopotamia
H763.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂram Nahărayim; Pronunciation: ar-am' nah-har-ah'-yim; Derivation: from H758 (אֲרָם) and the dual of H5104 (נָהָר);

H764.notes: Transliteration: ʼArmônîy; Pronunciation: ar-mo-nee'; Derivation: from H759 (אַרְמוֹן); palatial;

H765.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrân; Pronunciation: ar-awn'; Derivation: from H7442 (רָנַן); stridulous;

H766.notes: Transliteration: ʼôren; Pronunciation: o'-ren; Derivation: from the same as H765 (אֲרָן) (in the sense of strength);

H767.notes: Transliteration: ʼÔren; Pronunciation: o'-ren; Derivation: the same as H766 (אֹרֶן);

H768.notes: Transliteration: ʼarnebeth; Pronunciation: ar-neh'-beth; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H769.meaning: the Arnon, a river east of the Jordan, also its territory
H769.notes: Transliteration: ʼArnôwn; Pronunciation: ar-nohn'; Derivation: or אַרְנֹןlemma אַרְנן missing vowel, corrected to אַרְנֹן; from H7442 (רָנַן); a brawling stream;

H770.notes: Transliteration: ʼArnân; Pronunciation: ar-nawn'; Derivation: probably from the same as H769 (אַרְנוֹן); noisy;

H771.notes: Transliteration: ʼOrnân; Pronunciation: or-nawn'; Derivation: probably from H766 (אֹרֶן); strong;

H772.notes: Transliteration: ʼăraʻ; Pronunciation: ar-ah'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H776 (אֶרֶץ);

H773.notes: Transliteration: ʼarʻîyth; Pronunciation: arh-eeth'; Derivation: (Aramaic) feminine of H772 (אֲרַע);

H774.notes: Transliteration: ʼArpâd; Pronunciation: ar-pawd'; Derivation: from H7502 (רָפַד); spread out;

H775.meaning: Arpakshad, a son of Noah; also the region settled by him
H775.notes: Transliteration: ʼArpakshad; Pronunciation: ar-pak-shad'; Derivation: probably of foreign origin;

H776.meaning: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
H776.kjv: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
H776.notes: Transliteration: ʼerets; Pronunciation: eh'-rets; Derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

H777.notes: Transliteration: ʼartsâʼ; Pronunciation: ar-tsaw'; Derivation: from H776 (אֶרֶץ); earthiness;

H778.notes: Transliteration: ʼăraq; Pronunciation: ar-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) by transmutation for H772 (אֲרַע);

H779.notes: Transliteration: ʼârar; Pronunciation: aw-rar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H780.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂrâraṭ; Pronunciation: ar-aw-rat'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H781.notes: Transliteration: ʼâras; Pronunciation: aw-ras'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H782.notes: Transliteration: ʼăresheth; Pronunciation: ar-eh'-sheth; Derivation: from H781 (אָרַשׂ) (in the sense of desiring to possess);

H783.meaning: Artachshasta (or Artaxerxes), a title (rather than name) of several Persian kings
H783.notes: Transliteration: ʼArtachshashtâʼ; Pronunciation: ar-takh-shash-taw'; Derivation: or אַרְתַּחְשַׁשְׁתְּא; or by permutation אַרְתַּחְשַׁסְתְּא; of foreign origin;

H784.notes: Transliteration: ʼêsh; Pronunciation: aysh; Derivation: a primitive word;

H785.notes: Transliteration: ʼêsh; Pronunciation: aysh; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H784 (אֵשׁ)

H786.meaning: entity, used only adverbially, there is or are
H786.kjv: are there, none can. Compare H3426 (יֵשׁ).
H786.notes: Transliteration: ʼish; Pronunciation: eesh; Derivation: identical (in origin and formation) with H784 (אֵשׁ);

H787.notes: Transliteration: ʼôsh; Pronunciation: ohsh; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding (by transposition and abbreviation) to H803 (אֲשׁוּיָה);

H788.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshbêl; Pronunciation: ash-bale'; Derivation: probably from the same as H7640 (שֹׁבֶל); flowing;

H789.meaning: an Ashbelite (collectively) or descendant of Ashbel
H789.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshbêlîy; Pronunciation: ash-bay-lee'; Derivation: patronymic from H788 (אַשְׁבֵּל);

H790.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshbân; Pronunciation: esh-bawn'; Derivation: probably from the same as H7644 (שֶׁבְנָא); vigorous;

H791.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshbêaʻ; Pronunciation: ash-bay'-ah; Derivation: from H7650 (שָׁבַע); adjurer;

H792.meaning: Eshbaal (or Ishbosheth), a son of Saul
H792.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshbaʻal; Pronunciation: esh-bah'-al; Derivation: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H1168 (בַּעַל); man of Baal;

H793.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshed; Pronunciation: eh'-shed; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to pour;

H794.notes: Transliteration: ʼăshêdâh; Pronunciation: ash-ay-daw'; Derivation: feminine of H793 (אֶשֶׁד);

H795.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshdôwd; Pronunciation: ash-dode'; Derivation: from H7703 (שָׁדַד); ravager;

H796.meaning: an Ashdodite (often collectively) or inhabitant of Asdod
H796.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshdôwdîy; Pronunciation: ash-do-dee'; Derivation: patrial from H795 (אַשְׁדּוֹד);

H797.meaning: (only adverb) in the language of Ashdod
H797.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshdôwdîyth; Pronunciation: ash-do-deeth'; Derivation: feminine of H796 (אַשְׁדּוֹדִי);

H798.meaning: Ashdoth-Pisgah, a place east of the Jordan
H798.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshdôwth hap-Piçgâh; Pronunciation: ash-doth' hap-pisgaw'; Derivation: from the plural of H794 (אֲשֵׁדָה) and H6449 (פִּסְגָּה) with the article interposed; ravines of the Pisgah;

H799.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshdâth; Pronunciation: esh-dawth'; Derivation: from H784 (אֵשׁ) and H1881 (דָּת);

H800.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshshâh; Pronunciation: esh-shaw'; Derivation: feminine of H784 (אֵשׁ);

H801.meaning: properly, a burnt-offering; but occasionally of any sacrifice
H801.notes: Transliteration: ʼishshâh; Pronunciation: ish-shaw'; Derivation: the same as H800 (אֶשָּׁה), but used in a liturgical sense;

H802.kjv: (adulter) ess, each, every, female, [idiom] many, [phrase] none, one, [phrase] together, wife, woman. Often unexpressed in English.
H802.notes: Transliteration: ʼishshâh; Pronunciation: ish-shaw'; Derivation: feminine of H376 (אִישׁ) or H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ); irregular plural, נָשִׁים;(used in the same wide sense as H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ))

H803.notes: Transliteration: ʼăshûwyâh; Pronunciation: ash-oo-yah'; Derivation: feminine passive participle from an unused root meaning to found;

H804.meaning: Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them (i.e. Assyria), its region and its empire
H804.kjv: Asshur, Assur, Assyria, Assyrians. See H838 (אָשֻׁר).
H804.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshshûwr; Pronunciation: ash-shoor'; Derivation: or אַשֻּׁר; apparently from H833 (אָשַׁר) (in the sense of successful);

H805.meaning: an Ashurite (collectively) or inhabitant of Ashur, a district in Palestine
H805.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂshûwrîy; Pronunciation: ash-oo-ree'; Derivation: or אַשּׁוּרִי; from a patrial word of the same form as H804 (אַשּׁוּר);

H806.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshchûwr; Pronunciation: ash-khoor'; Derivation: probably from H7835 (שָׁחַר); black;

H807.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshîymâʼ; Pronunciation: ash-ee-maw'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H808.notes: Transliteration: ʼâshîysh; Pronunciation: aw-sheesh'; Derivation: from the same as H784 (אֵשׁ) (in the sense of pressing down firmly; compare H803 (אֲשׁוּיָה));

H809.meaning: something closely pressed together, i.e. a cake of raisins or other comfits
H809.notes: Transliteration: ʼăshîyshâh; Pronunciation: ash-ee-shaw'; Derivation: feminine of H808 (אָשִׁישׁ);

H810.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshek; Pronunciation: eh'-shek; Derivation: from an unused root (probably meaning to bunch together);

H811.meaning: a bunch of grapes or other fruit
H811.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshkôwl; Pronunciation: esh-kole'; Derivation: or אֶשְׁכֹּל; probably prolonged from H810 (אֶשֶׁךְ);

H812.meaning: Eshcol, the name of an Amorite, also of a valley in Palestine
H812.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshkôl; Pronunciation: esh-kole'; Derivation: the same as H811 (אֶשְׁכּוֹל);

H813.meaning: Ashkenaz, a Japhethite, also his descendants
H813.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshkᵉnaz; Pronunciation: ash-ken-az'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H814.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshkâr; Pronunciation: esh-cawr'; Derivation: for H7939 (שָׂכָר);

H815.meaning: a tamarisk tree; by extension, a grove of any kind
H815.notes: Transliteration: ʼêshel; Pronunciation: ay'-shel; Derivation: from a root of uncertain signification;

H816.meaning: to be guilty; by implication to be punished or perish
H816.kjv: [idiom] certainly, be(-come, made) desolate, destroy, [idiom] greatly, be(-come, found, hold) guilty, offend (acknowledge offence), trespass.
H816.notes: Transliteration: ʼâsham; Pronunciation: aw-sham'; Derivation: or אָשֵׁם; a primitive root;

H817.meaning: guilt; by implication, a fault; also a sin-offering
H817.notes: Transliteration: ʼâshâm; Pronunciation: aw-shawm'; Derivation: from H816 (אָשַׁם);

H818.notes: Transliteration: ʼâshêm; Pronunciation: aw-shame'; Derivation: from H816 (אָשַׁם);

H819.meaning: guiltiness, a fault, the presentation of asin-offering
H819.notes: Transliteration: ʼashmâh; Pronunciation: ash-maw'; Derivation: feminine of H817 (אָשָׁם);

H820.notes: Transliteration: ʼashmân; Pronunciation: ash-mawn'; Derivation: probably from H8081 (שֶׁמֶן);

H821.notes: Transliteration: ʼashmurâh; Pronunciation: ash-moo-raw'; Derivation: or אַשׁמוּרָה; or אַשׁמֹרֶת; (feminine) from H8104 (שָׁמַר);

H822.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshnâb; Pronunciation: esh-nawb'; Derivation: apparently from an unused root (probably meaning to leave interstices);

H823.meaning: Ashnah, the name of two places in Palestine
H823.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshnâh; Pronunciation: ash-naw'; Derivation: probably a variation for H3466 (יְשָׁנָה);

H824.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshʻân; Pronunciation: esh-awn'; Derivation: from H8172 (שָׁעַן); support;

H825.notes: Transliteration: ʼashshâph; Pronunciation: ash-shawf'; Derivation: from an unused root (probably meaning to lisp, i.e. practice enchantment);

H826.notes: Transliteration: ʼashshâph; Pronunciation: ash-shawf'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H825 (אַשָּׁף)

H827.notes: Transliteration: ʼashpâh; Pronunciation: ash-paw'; Derivation: perhaps (feminine) from the same as H825 (אַשָּׁף) (in the sense of covering);

H828.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshpᵉnaz; Pronunciation: ash-pen-az'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H829.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshpâr; Pronunciation: esh-pawr'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H830.meaning: a heap of rubbish or filth

H831.notes: Transliteration: ʼAshqᵉlôwn; Pronunciation: ash-kel-one'; Derivation: probably from H8254 (שָׁקַל) in the sense of weighing-place (i.e. mart);

H832.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshqᵉlôwnîy; Pronunciation: esh-kel-o-nee'; Derivation: patrial from H831 (אַשְׁקְלוֹן);

H833.meaning: to be straight (used in the widest sense, especially to be level, right, happy); figuratively, to go forward, be honest, proper
H833.kjv: (call, be) bless(-ed, happy), go, guide, lead, relieve.
H833.notes: Transliteration: ʼâshar; Pronunciation: aw-shar'; Derivation: or אָשֵׁר; a primitive root;

H834.meaning: who, which, what, that; also (as an adverb and a conjunction) when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
H834.kjv: [idiom] after, [idiom] alike, as (soon as), because, [idiom] every, for, [phrase] forasmuch, [phrase] from whence, [phrase] how(-soever), [idiom] if, (so) that ((thing) which, wherein), [idiom] though, [phrase] until, [phrase] whatsoever, when, where ([phrase] -as, -in, -of, -on, -soever, -with), which, whilst, [phrase] whither(-soever), who(-m, -soever, -se). As it is indeclinable, it is often accompanied by the personal pronoun expletively, used to show the connection.
H834.notes: Transliteration: ʼăsher; Pronunciation: ash-er'; Derivation: a primitive relative pronoun (of every gender and number);

H835.meaning: happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
H835.notes: Transliteration: ʼesher; Pronunciation: eh'-sher; Derivation: from H833 (אָשַׁר);

H836.meaning: happy; Asher, a son of Jacob, and the tribe descended from him, with its territory; also a place in Palestine
H836.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂshêr; Pronunciation: aw-share'; Derivation: from H833 (אָשַׁר),

H837.notes: Transliteration: ʼôsher; Pronunciation: o'-sher; Derivation: from H833 (אָשַׁר);

H838.notes: Transliteration: ʼâshur; Pronunciation: aw-shoor'; Derivation: or אַשֻּׁר; from H833 (אָשַׁר) in the sense of going;

H839.meaning: the cedar tree or some other light elastic wood
H839.notes: Transliteration: ʼăshur; Pronunciation: ash-oor'; Derivation: contracted for H8391 (תְּאַשּׁוּר);

H840.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂsarʼêl; Pronunciation: as-ar-ale'; Derivation: by orthographical variation from H833 (אָשַׁר) and H410 (אֵל); right of God;

H841.notes: Transliteration: ʼĂsarʼêlâh; Pronunciation: as-ar-ale'-aw; Derivation: from the same as H840 (אֲשַׂרְאֵל); right toward God;

H842.meaning: Asherah (or Astarte) a Phoenician goddess; also an image of the same
H842.notes: Transliteration: ʼăshêrâh; Pronunciation: ash-ay-raw'; Derivation: or אֲשֵׁירָה; from H833 (אָשַׁר); happy;

H843.meaning: an Asherite (collectively) or descendant of Asher
H843.notes: Transliteration: ʼÂshêrîy; Pronunciation: aw-shay-ree'; Derivation: patronymic from H836 (אָשֵׁר);

H844.meaning: Asriel, the name of two Israelites
H844.notes: Transliteration: ʼAsrîyʼêl; Pronunciation: as-ree-ale'; Derivation: an orthographical variation for H840 (אֲשַׂרְאֵל);

H845.meaning: an Asrielite (collectively) or descendant of Asriel
H845.notes: Transliteration: ʼAsriʼêlîy; Pronunciation: as-ree-ale-ee'; Derivation: patronymic from H844 (אַשְׂרִיאֵל);

H846.notes: Transliteration: ʼushsharnâʼ; Pronunciation: oosh-ar-naw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H833 (אָשַׁר);

H847.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshtâʼôl; Pronunciation: esh-taw-ole'; Derivation: or אֶשְׁתָּאוֹל; probably from H7592 (שָׁאַל); intreaty;

H848.meaning: an Eshtaolite (collectively) or inhabitant of Eshtaol
H848.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshtâʼulîy; Pronunciation: esh-taw-oo-lee'; Derivation: patrial from H847 (אֶשְׁתָּאֹל);

H849.notes: Transliteration: ʼeshtaddûwr; Pronunciation: esh-tad-dure'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H7712 (שְׁדַר) (in a bad sense);

H850.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshtôwn; Pronunciation: esh-tone'; Derivation: probably from the same as H7764 (שׁוּנִי); restful;

H851.meaning: Eshtemoa or Eshtemoh, a place in Palestine
H851.notes: Transliteration: ʼEshtᵉmôaʻ; Pronunciation: esh-tem-o'-ah; Derivation: or אֶשְׁתְּמוֹעַ; or אֶשְׁתְּמֹה; from H8085 (שָׁמַע) (in the sense of obedience);

H852.notes: Transliteration: ʼâth; Pronunciation: awth; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H226 (אוֹת);

H853.meaning: properly, self (but generally used to point out more definitely the object of a verb or preposition, even or namely)
H853.notes: Transliteration: ʼêth; Pronunciation: ayth; Derivation: apparent contracted from H226 (אוֹת) in the demonstrative sense of entity;

H854.meaning: properly, nearness (used only as a preposition or an adverb), near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
H854.kjv: against, among, before, by, for, from, in(-to), (out) of, with. Often with another prepositional prefix.
H854.notes: Transliteration: ʼêth; Pronunciation: ayth; Derivation: probably from H579 (אָנָה);

H855.meaning: a hoe or other digging implement
H855.notes: Transliteration: ʼêth; Pronunciation: ayth; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H856.notes: Transliteration: ʼEthbaʻal; Pronunciation: eth-bah'-al; Derivation: from H854 (אֵת) and H1168 (בַּעַל); with Baal;

H857.notes: Transliteration: ʼâthâh; Pronunciation: aw-thaw'; Derivation: or אָתָא; a primitive root (collateral to H225 (אוּת) contraction);

H987.notes: Transliteration: baṭṭuchôwth; Pronunciation: bat-too-khoth'; Derivation: feminine plural from H982 (בָּטַח);

H988.notes: Transliteration: bâṭêl; Pronunciation: baw-tale'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H989.notes: Transliteration: bᵉṭêl; Pronunciation: bet-ale'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H988 (בָּטֵל);

H990.meaning: the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything
H990.kjv: belly, body, [phrase] as they be born, [phrase] within, womb.

H991.notes: Transliteration: Beṭen; Pronunciation: beh'-ten; Derivation: the same as H990 (בֶּטֶן);

H992.meaning: (only in plural) a pistachio-nut (from its form)
H992.notes: Transliteration: bôṭen; Pronunciation: bo'-ten; Derivation: from H990 (בֶּטֶן);

H993.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉṭônîym; Pronunciation: bet-o-neem'; Derivation: probably plural from H992 (בֹּטֶן); hollows;

H994.meaning: oh that!; with leave, or if it please

H995.meaning: to separate mentally (or distinguish), i.e.(generally) understand
H995.kjv: attend, consider, be cunning, diligently, direct, discern, eloquent, feel, inform, instruct, have intelligence, know, look well to, mark, perceive, be prudent, regard, (can) skill(-full), teach, think, (cause, make to, get, give, have) understand(-ing), view, (deal) wise(-ly, man).
H995.notes: Transliteration: bîyn; Pronunciation: bene; Derivation: a primitive root;

H996.meaning: between (repeated before each noun, often with other particles); also as a conjunction, either...or
H996.kjv: among, asunder, at, between (-twixt...and), [phrase] from (the widest), [idiom] in, out of, whether (it be...or), within.

H997.meaning: between (repeated before each noun, often with other particles); also as a conjunction, either...or
H997.notes: Transliteration: bêyn; Pronunciation: bane; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H996 (בֵּין)

H998.notes: Transliteration: bîynâh; Pronunciation: bee-naw'; Derivation: from H995 (בִּין);

H999.notes: Transliteration: bîynâh; Pronunciation: bee-naw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H998 (בִּינָה)

H1000.notes: Transliteration: bêytsâh; Pronunciation: bay-tsaw'; Derivation: from the same as H948 (בּוּץ);

H1001.notes: Transliteration: bîyrâʼ; Pronunciation: bee-raw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1002 (בִּירָה);

H1002.notes: Transliteration: bîyrâh; Pronunciation: bee-raw'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H1003.notes: Transliteration: bîyrânîyth; Pronunciation: bee-raw-neeth'; Derivation: from H1002 (בִּירָה);

H1004.meaning: a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
H1004.kjv: court, daughter, door, [phrase] dungeon, family, [phrase] forth of, [idiom] great as would contain, hangings, home(born), (winter) house(-hold), inside(-ward), palace, place, [phrase] prison, [phrase] steward, [phrase] tablet, temple, web, [phrase] within(-out).
H1004.notes: Transliteration: bayith; Pronunciation: bah'-yith; Derivation: probably from H1129 (בָּנָה) abbreviated;

H1005.meaning: a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
H1005.notes: Transliteration: bayith; Pronunciation: bah-yith; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1004 (בַּיִת)

H1006.notes: Transliteration: Bayith; Pronunciation: bah'-yith; Derivation: the same as H1004 (בַּיִת);

H1007.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʼÂven; Pronunciation: bayth aw'-ven; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H205 (אָוֶן); house of vanity;

H1008.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth-ʼÊl; Pronunciation: bayth-ale'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H410 (אֵל); house of God;

H1009.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʼArbêʼl; Pronunciation: bayth ar-bale'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H695 (אֶרֶב) and H410 (אֵל); house of God's ambush;

H1010.kjv: Beth-baal-meon. Compare H1186 (בַּעַל מְעוֹן) and H1194 (בְּעֹן).
H1010.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Baʻal Mᵉʻôwn; Pronunciation: bayth bah'-al me-own'

H1011.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Birʼîy; Pronunciation: bayth bir-ee'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1254 (בָּרָא); house of a creative one;

H1012.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Bârâh; Pronunciation: bayth baw-raw'; Derivation: probably from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5679 (עֲבָרָה); house of (the) ford;

H1013.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth-Gâdêr; Pronunciation: bayth-gaw-dare'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1447 (גָּדֵר); house of (the) wall;

H1014.meaning: Beth-Gamul, a place East of the Jordan

H1015.meaning: Beth-Diblathajim, a place East of the Jordan
H1015.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Diblâthayim; Pronunciation: bayth dib-law-thah'-yim; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the dual of H1690 (דְּבֵלָה); house of (the) two figcakes;

H1016.meaning: Beth-Dagon, the name of two places in Palestine

H1018.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hâʼêtsel; Pronunciation: bayth haw-ay'-tsel; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H681 (אֵצֶל) with the article interposed; house of the side;

H1019.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hag-Gilgâl; Pronunciation: bayth hag-gil gawl'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1537 (גִּלְגָּל) with the article interposed; house of Gilgal (or rolling);

H1020.meaning: Beth-ha-Jeshimoth, a town East of the Jordan
H1020.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ha-Yshîy-môwth; Pronunciation: bayth hah-yesh-eemoth'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3451 (יְשִׁימַה) with the article interposed; house of the deserts;

H1021.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hak-Kerem; Pronunciation: bayth hak-keh'-rem; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3754 (כֶּרֶם) with the article interposed; house of the vineyard;

H1022.meaning: a Beth-lechemite, or native of Bethlechem

H1023.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ham-Merchâq; Pronunciation: bayth ham-mer-khawk'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4801 (מֶרְחָק) with the article interposed; house of the breadth;

H1024.meaning: Beth-ham-Markaboth or Beth-Markaboth, a place in Palestine
H1024.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ham-Markâbôwth; Pronunciation: bayth ham-markaw-both'

H1025.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hâ-ʻÊmeq; Pronunciation: bayth haw-Ay'-mek; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6010 (עֵמֶק) with the article interposed; house of the valley;

H1026.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hâ-ʻĂrâbâh; Pronunciation: bayth haw-ar-aw-baw; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6160 (עֲרָבָה) with the article interposed; house of the Desert;

H1027.meaning: Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan
H1027.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hâ-Râm; Pronunciation: bayth haw-rawm'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7311 (רוּם) with the article interposed; house of the height;

H1028.meaning: Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan
H1028.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hâ-Rân; Pronunciation: bayth haw-rawn'; Derivation: probably for H1027 (בֵּית הָרָם);

H1029.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hash-Shiṭṭâh; Pronunciation: bayth hash-shit-taw'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7848 (שִׁטָּה) with the article interposed; house of the acacia;

H1030.meaning: a Beth-shimshite, or inhabitant of Bethshemesh
H1030.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth hash-Shimshîy; Pronunciation: bayth hash-shim-shee'; Derivation: patrial from H1053 (בֵּית שֶׁמֶשׁ) with the article inserted;

H1031.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Choglâh; Pronunciation: bayth chog-law'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the same as H2295 (חׇגְלָה); house of a partridge;

H1033.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Kar; Pronunciation: bayth kar; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3733 (כַּר); house of pasture;

H1034.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Lᵉbâʼôwth; Pronunciation: bayth leb-aw-oth'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3833 (לָבִיא); house of lionesses;

H1035.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Lechem; Pronunciation: bayth leh'-khem; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3899 (לֶחֶם); house of bread;

H1038.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Maʻăkâh; Pronunciation: bayth mah-ak-aw'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4601 (מַעֲכָה); house of Maakah;

H1039.meaning: Beth-Nimrah, a place east of the Jordan

H1040.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʻÊden; Pronunciation: bayth ay'-den; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5730 (עֵדֶן); house of pleasure;

H1041.meaning: house of Azmaveth, a place in Palestine
H1041.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʻAzmâveth; Pronunciation: bayth az-maw'-veth; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5820 (עַזְמָוֶת);

H1042.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʻĂnôwth; Pronunciation: bayth an-oth'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a plural from H6030 (עָנָה); house of replies;

H1044.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʻÊqed; Pronunciation: bayth ay'-ked; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a derivative of H6123 (עָקַד); house of (the) binding (for sheep-shearing);

H1045.kjv: house of Ashtaroth. Compare H1203 (בְּעֶשְׁתְּרָה), H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת).
H1045.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth ʻAshtârôwth; Pronunciation: bayth ash-taw-roth'; Derivation: ; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת); house of Ashtoreths;

H1046.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Peleṭ; Pronunciation: bayth peh'-let; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6412 (פָּלִיט); house of escape;

H1047.meaning: Beth-Peor, a place East of the Jordan
H1047.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Pᵉʻôwr; Pronunciation: bayth pe-ore'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6465 (פְּעוֹר); house of Peor;

H1048.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Patstsêts; Pronunciation: bayth pats-tsates'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a derivative from H6327 (פּוּץ); house of dispersion;

H1049.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Tsûwr; Pronunciation: bayth tsoor'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6697 (צוּר); house of (the) rock;

H1051.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Râphâʼ; Pronunciation: bayth raw-faw'; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7497 (רָפָא); house of (the) giant;

H1052.meaning: Beth-Shean or Beth-Shan, a place in Palestine
H1052.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Shᵉʼân; Pronunciation: bayth she-awn'; Derivation: or בֵּית שָׁן; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7599 (שָׁאַן); house of ease;

H1053.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Shemesh; Pronunciation: bayth sheh'-mesh; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H8121 (שֶׁמֶשׁ); house of (the) sun;

H1054.notes: Transliteration: Bêyth Tappûwach; Pronunciation: bayth tap-poo'-akh; Derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H8598 (תַּפּוּחַ); house of (the) apple;

H1055.notes: Transliteration: bîythân; Pronunciation: bee-thawn'; Derivation: probably from H1004 (בַּיִת);

H1056.notes: Transliteration: Bâkâʼ; Pronunciation: baw-kaw'; Derivation: from H1058 (בָּכָה), weeping;

H1057.meaning: the weeping tree (some gum-distilling tree, perhaps the balsam)
H1057.notes: Transliteration: bâkâʼ; Pronunciation: baw-kaw'; Derivation: the same as H1056 (בָּכָא);

H1058.kjv: [idiom] at all, bewail, complain, make lamentation, [idiom] more, mourn, [idiom] sore, [idiom] with tears, weep.
H1058.notes: Transliteration: bâkâh; Pronunciation: baw-kaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1059.notes: Transliteration: bekeh; Pronunciation: beh'-keh; Derivation: from H1058 (בָּכָה);

H1060.notes: Transliteration: bᵉkôwr; Pronunciation: bek-ore'; Derivation: from H1069 (בָּכַר);

H1061.notes: Transliteration: bikkûwr; Pronunciation: bik-koor'; Derivation: from H1069 (בָּכַר);

H1062.meaning: the firstling of man or beast; abstractly primogeniture
H1062.notes: Transliteration: bᵉkôwrâh; Pronunciation: bek-o-raw'; Derivation: or (short) בְּכֹרָה; feminine of H1060 (בְּכוֹר);

H1063.notes: Transliteration: bikkûwrâh; Pronunciation: bik-koo-raw'; Derivation: feminine of H1061 (בִּכּוּר);

H1064.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉkôwrath; Pronunciation: bek-o-rath'; Derivation: feminine of H1062 (בְּכוֹרָה); primogeniture;

H1065.meaning: a weeping; by analogy, a dripping
H1065.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉkîy; Pronunciation: bek-ee'; Derivation: from H1058 (בָּכָה);

H1066.notes: Transliteration: Bôkîym; Pronunciation: bo-keem'; Derivation: plural active participle of H1058 (בָּכָה); (with the article) the weepers;

H1067.notes: Transliteration: bᵉkîyrâh; Pronunciation: bek-ee-raw'; Derivation: feminine from H1069 (בָּכַר);

H1068.notes: Transliteration: bᵉkîyth; Pronunciation: bek-eeth'; Derivation: from H1058 (בָּכָה);

H1069.kjv: make firstborn, be firstling, bring forth first child (new fruit).

H1070.notes: Transliteration: beker; Pronunciation: beh'-ker; Derivation: from H1069 (בָּכַר) (in the sense of youth);

H1071.meaning: Beker, the name of two Israelites
H1071.notes: Transliteration: Beker; Pronunciation: beh'-ker; Derivation: the same as H1070 (בֶּכֶר);

H1072.notes: Transliteration: bikrâh; Pronunciation: bik-raw'; Derivation: feminine of H1070 (בֶּכֶר);

H1073.notes: Transliteration: bakkurâh; Pronunciation: bak-koo-raw'; Derivation: by orthographical variation for H1063 (בִּכּוּרָה);

H1074.notes: Transliteration: Bôkᵉrûw; Pronunciation: bo-ker-oo'; Derivation: from H1069 (בָּכַר); first-born;

H1075.notes: Transliteration: Bikrîy; Pronunciation: bik-ree'; Derivation: from H1069 (בָּכַר); youth-ful;

H1076.meaning: a Bakrite (collectively) or descendants of Beker
H1076.notes: Transliteration: Bakrîy; Pronunciation: bak-ree'; Derivation: patronymically from H1071 (בֶכֶר);

H1077.meaning: properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually (adverb) not at all; also lest
H1077.kjv: lest, neither, no, none (that...), not (any), nothing.
H1077.notes: Transliteration: bal; Pronunciation: bal; Derivation: from H1086 (בָּלָה);

H1078.meaning: Bel, the Baal of the Babylonians
H1078.notes: Transliteration: Bêl; Pronunciation: bale; Derivation: by contraction for H1168 (בַּעַל);

H1079.meaning: properly, anxiety, i.e. (by implication) the heart (as its seat)
H1079.notes: Transliteration: bâl; Pronunciation: bawl; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H1080 (בְּלָא);

H1080.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlâʼ; Pronunciation: bel-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1086 (בָּלָה) (but used only in a mental sense);

H1081.meaning: Baladan, the name of a Babylonian prince
H1081.notes: Transliteration: Balʼădân; Pronunciation: bal-ad-awn'; Derivation: from H1078 (בֵּל) and H113 (אָדוֹן) (contracted); Bel (is his) lord;

H1082.meaning: to break off or loose (in a favorable or unfavorable sense), i.e. desist (from grief) or invade (with destruction)
H1082.notes: Transliteration: bâlag; Pronunciation: baw-lag'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1083.meaning: Bilgah, the name of two Israelites
H1083.notes: Transliteration: Bilgah; Pronunciation: bil-gaw'; Derivation: from H1082 (בָּלַג); desistance;

H1084.notes: Transliteration: Bilgay; Pronunciation: bil-gah'ee; Derivation: from H1082 (בָּלַג); desistant;

H1085.notes: Transliteration: Bildad; Pronunciation: bil-dad'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H1086.meaning: to fail; by implication to wear out, decay (causatively, consume, spend)
H1086.kjv: consume, enjoy long, become (make, wax) old, spend, waste.
H1086.notes: Transliteration: bâlâh; Pronunciation: baw-law'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1087.notes: Transliteration: bâleh; Pronunciation: baw-leh'; Derivation: from H1086 (בָּלָה);

H1088.notes: Transliteration: Bâlâh; Pronunciation: baw-law'; Derivation: feminine of H1087 (בָּלֶה); failure;

H1089.notes: Transliteration: bâlahh; Pronunciation: baw-lah'; Derivation: a primitive root (rather by transposition for H926 (בָּהַל));

H1090.meaning: Bilhah, the name of one of Jacob's concubines; also of a place in Palestine
H1090.notes: Transliteration: Bilhâh; Pronunciation: bil-haw'; Derivation: from H1089 (בָּלַהּ); timid;

H1091.notes: Transliteration: ballâhâh; Pronunciation: bal-law-haw'; Derivation: from H1089 (בָּלַהּ);

H1092.meaning: Bilhan, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite
H1092.notes: Transliteration: Bilhân; Pronunciation: bil-hawn'; Derivation: from H1089 (בָּלַהּ); timid;

H1093.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlôw; Pronunciation: bel-o'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H1086 (בָּלָה);

H1094.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlôwʼ; Pronunciation: bel-o'; Derivation: or (fully) בְּלוֹי; from H1086 (בָּלָה);

H1095.meaning: Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel
H1095.notes: Transliteration: Bêlṭᵉshaʼtstsar; Pronunciation: bale-tesh-ats-tsar'; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H1096.meaning: Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel
H1096.notes: Transliteration: Bêlṭᵉshaʼtstsar; Pronunciation: bale-tesh-ats-tsar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1095 (בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר)

H1097.meaning: properly, failure, i.e. nothing or destruction; usually (with preposition) without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
H1097.kjv: corruption, ig(norantly), for lack of, where no...is, so that no, none, not, un(awares), without.
H1097.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlîy; Pronunciation: bel-ee'; Derivation: from H1086 (בָּלָה);

H1098.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlîyl; Pronunciation: bel-eel'; Derivation: from H1101 (בָּלַל);

H1099.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlîymâh; Pronunciation: bel-ee-mah'; Derivation: from H1097 (בְּלִי) and H4100 (מָה);

H1100.meaning: without profit, worthlessness; by extension, destruction, wickedness
H1100.notes: Transliteration: bᵉlîyaʻal; Pronunciation: bel-e-yah'-al; Derivation: from H1097 (בְּלִי) and H3276 (יַעַל);(often in connection with H376 (אִישׁ), H802 (אִשָּׁה), H1121 (בֵּן), etc.)

H1101.meaning: to overflow (specifically with oil.); by implication, to mix; to fodder
H1101.kjv: anoint, confound, [idiom] fade, mingle, mix (self), give provender, temper.
H1101.notes: Transliteration: bâlal; Pronunciation: baw-lal'; Derivation: a primitive root; (also  denominatively from H1098 (בְּלִיל)) to fodder

H1102.notes: Transliteration: bâlam; Pronunciation: baw-lam'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1103.meaning: to pinch sycamore figs (a process necessary to ripen them)
H1103.notes: Transliteration: bâlaç; Pronunciation: baw-las'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1104.meaning: to make away with (specifically by swallowing); generally, to destroy
H1104.kjv: cover, destroy, devour, eat up, be at end, spend up, swallow down (up).
H1104.notes: Transliteration: bâlaʻ; Pronunciation: baw-lah'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1105.kjv: devouring, that which he hath swallowed up.
H1105.notes: Transliteration: belaʻ; Pronunciation: beh'-lah; Derivation: from H1104 (בָּלַע);

H1106.meaning: Bela, the name of a place, also of an Edomite and of two Israelites
H1106.notes: Transliteration: Belaʻ; Pronunciation: beh'-lah; Derivation: the same as H1105 (בֶּלַע);

H1107.notes: Transliteration: bilʻădêy; Pronunciation: bil-ad-ay'; Derivation: or בַּלְעֲדֵי; constructive plural from H1077 (בַּל) and H5703 (עַד), not till, i.e. (as preposition or adverb)

H1108.meaning: a Belaite (collectively) or descendants of Bela
H1108.notes: Transliteration: Balʻîy; Pronunciation: bel-ee'; Derivation: patronymically from H1106 (בֶּלַע);

H1109.meaning: Bilam, a Mesopotamian prophet; also a place in Palestine
H1109.notes: Transliteration: Bilʻâm; Pronunciation: bil-awm'; Derivation: probably from H1077 (בַּל) and H5971 (עַם); not (of the) people, i.e. foreigner;

H1110.notes: Transliteration: bâlaq; Pronunciation: baw-lak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1111.notes: Transliteration: Bâlâq; Pronunciation: baw-lawk'; Derivation: from H1110 (בָּלַק); waster;

H1113.notes: Transliteration: Bêlshaʼtstsar; Pronunciation: bale-shats-tsar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1112 (בֵּלְשַׁאצַּר)

H1114.notes: Transliteration: Bilshân; Pronunciation: bil-shawn'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H1115.meaning: properly, a failure of, i.e. (used only as a negative particle, usually with a prepositional prefix) not, except, without, unless, besides, because not, until, etc.
H1115.kjv: because un(satiable), beside, but, [phrase] continual, except, from, lest, neither, no more, none, not, nothing, save, that no, without.
H1115.notes: Transliteration: biltîy; Pronunciation: bil-tee'; Derivation: constructive feminine of H1086 (בָּלָה) (equivalent to H1097 (בְּלִי));

H1116.notes: Transliteration: bâmâh; Pronunciation: bam-maw'; Derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be high);

H1117.notes: Transliteration: Bâmâh; Pronunciation: baw-maw'; Derivation: the same as H1116 (בָּמָה);

H1118.notes: Transliteration: Bimhâl; Pronunciation: bim-hawl'; Derivation: probably from H4107 (מָהַל) with prepositional prefix; with pruning;

H1119.notes: Transliteration: bᵉmôw; Pronunciation: bem-o'; Derivation: prolongation for prepositional prefix;

H1120.meaning: Bamoth or Bamoth-Baal, a place East of the Jordan
H1120.notes: Transliteration: Bâmôwth; Pronunciation: baw-moth'; Derivation: plural of H1116 (בָּמָה); heights; or (fully) בָּמוֹת בַּעַל; from the same and H1168 (בַּעַל); heights of Baal;

H1121.meaning: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
H1121.kjv: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch, breed, [phrase] (young) bullock, [phrase] (young) calf, [idiom] came up in, child, colt, [idiom] common, [idiom] corn, daughter, [idiom] of first, [phrase] firstborn, foal, [phrase] very fruitful, [phrase] postage, [idiom] in, [phrase] kid, [phrase] lamb, ([phrase]) man, meet, [phrase] mighty, [phrase] nephew, old, ([phrase]) people, [phrase] rebel, [phrase] robber, [idiom] servant born, [idiom] soldier, son, [phrase] spark, [phrase] steward, [phrase] stranger, [idiom] surely, them of, [phrase] tumultuous one, [phrase] valiant(-est), whelp, worthy, young (one), youth.
H1121.notes: Transliteration: bên; Pronunciation: bane; Derivation: from H1129 (בָּנָה);

H1122.notes: Transliteration: Bên; Pronunciation: bane; Derivation: the same as H1121 (בֵּן);

H1123.meaning: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense
H1123.notes: Transliteration: bên; Pronunciation: bane; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1121 (בֵּן)

H1124.notes: Transliteration: bᵉnâʼ; Pronunciation: ben-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or בְּנָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to H1129 (בָּנָה);

H1125.notes: Transliteration: Ben-ʼĂbîynâdâb; Pronunciation: ben-ab-ee''-naw-dawb'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H40 (אֲבִימֶלֶךְ); (the) son of Abinadab;

H1126.meaning: Ben-Oni, the original name of Benjamin
H1126.notes: Transliteration: Ben-ʼÔwnîy; Pronunciation: ben-o-nee'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H205 (אָוֶן); son of my sorrow;

H1127.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Geber; Pronunciation: ben-gheh'-ber; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H1397 (גֶּבֶר); son of (the) hero;

H1128.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Deqer; Pronunciation: ben-deh'-ker; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and a derivative of H1856 (דָּקַר); son of piercing (or of a lance);

H1129.kjv: (begin to) build(-er), obtain children, make, repair, set (up), [idiom] surely.
H1129.notes: Transliteration: bânâh; Pronunciation: baw-naw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1130.meaning: Ben-Hadad, the name of several Syrian kings
H1130.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Hădad; Pronunciation: ben-had-ad'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H1908 (הֲדַד); son of Hadad;

H1131.notes: Transliteration: Binnûwy; Pronunciation: bin-noo'-ee; Derivation: from H1129 (בָּנָה); built up;

H1132.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Zôwchêth; Pronunciation: ben-zo-khayth'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2105 (זוֹחֵת); son of Zocheth;

H1133.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Chûwr; Pronunciation: ben-khoor'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2354 (חוּר); son of Chur;

H1134.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Chayil; Pronunciation: ben-khah'-yil; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2428 (חַיִל); son of might;

H1135.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Chânân; Pronunciation: ben-khaw-nawn'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2605 (חָנָן); son of Chanan;

H1136.notes: Transliteration: Ben-Cheçed; Pronunciation: ben-kheh'-sed; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2617 (חֵסֵד); son of kindness;

H1137.meaning: Bani, the name of five Israelites
H1137.notes: Transliteration: Bânîy; Pronunciation: baw-nee'; Derivation: from H1129 (בָּנָה); built;

H1138.notes: Transliteration: Bunnîy; Pronunciation: boon-nee'; Derivation: or (fuller) בּוּנִי; from H1129 (בָּנָה); built;

H1139.meaning: sons of lightning, Bene-berak, a place in Palestine
H1139.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉnêy-Bᵉraq; Pronunciation: ben-ay'-ber-ak'; Derivation: from the plural construction of H1121 (בֵּן) and H1300 (בָּרָק);

H1140.notes: Transliteration: binyâh; Pronunciation: bin-yaw'; Derivation: feminine from H1129 (בָּנָה);

H1141.meaning: Benajah, the name of twelve Israelites
H1141.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉnâyâh; Pronunciation: ben-aw-yaw'; Derivation: or בּנָיָהוּ; (prolonged) from H1129 (בָּנָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has built;

H1142.meaning: Bene-Jaakan, a place in the Desert
H1142.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉnêy Yaʻăqân; Pronunciation: ben-ay' yah-ak-awn'; Derivation: from the plural of H1121 (בֵּן) and H3292 (יַעֲקָן); sons of Yaakan;

H1143.meaning: a double interval, i.e. the space between two armies
H1143.notes: Transliteration: bênayim; Pronunciation: bay-nah'-yim; Derivation: dual of H996 (בֵּין);

H1144.meaning: Binjamin, youngest son of Jacob; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory
H1144.notes: Transliteration: Binyâmîyn; Pronunciation: bin-yaw-mene'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H3225 (יָמִין); son of (the) right hand;

H1145.meaning: a Benjaminite, or descendent of Benjamin

H1146.notes: Transliteration: binyân; Pronunciation: bin-yawn'; Derivation: from H1129 (בָּנָה);

H1147.notes: Transliteration: binyân; Pronunciation: bin-yawn'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1146 (בִּנְיָן)

H1148.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉnîynûw; Pronunciation: ben-ee-noo'; Derivation: probably from H1121 (בֵּן) with pron. suff.; our son;

H1149.notes: Transliteration: bᵉnaç; Pronunciation: ben-as'; Derivation: (Aramaic) of uncertain affinity;

H1150.notes: Transliteration: Binʻâʼ; Pronunciation: bin-aw'; Derivation: or בִּנְעָה; of uncertain derivation;

H1151.notes: Transliteration: Ben-ʻAmmîy; Pronunciation: ben-am-mee'; Derivation: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H5971 (עַם) with pronomial suffix; son of my people;

H1152.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉçôwdᵉyâh; Pronunciation: bes-o-deh-yaw'; Derivation: from H5475 (סוֹד) and H3050 (יָהּ) with prepositional prefix; in (the) counsel of Jehovah;

H1153.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉçay; Pronunciation: bes-ah'-ee; Derivation: from H947 (בּוּס); domineering;

H1154.notes: Transliteration: beçer; Pronunciation: beh'-ser; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to be sour;

H1155.notes: Transliteration: bôçer; Pronunciation: bo'ser; Derivation: from the same as H1154 (בֶּסֶר)

H1156.kjv: ask, desire, make (petition), pray, request, seek.
H1156.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻâʼ; Pronunciation: beh-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or בְּעָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to H1158 (בָּעָה);

H1157.meaning: in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
H1157.kjv: about, at by (means of), for, over, through, up (-on), within.
H1157.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻad; Pronunciation: beh-ad'; Derivation: from H5704 (עַד) with prepositional prefix;

H1158.meaning: to gush over, i.e. to swell; (figuratively) to desire earnestly; by implication to ask
H1158.kjv: cause, inquire, seek up, swell out.
H1158.notes: Transliteration: bâʻâh; Pronunciation: baw-aw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1159.notes: Transliteration: bâʻûw; Pronunciation: baw-oo'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H1156 (בְּעָא);

H1160.meaning: Beor, the name of the father of an Edomitish king; also of that of Balaam
H1160.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉʻôwr; Pronunciation: beh-ore'; Derivation: from H1197 (בָּעַר) (in the sense of burning); a lamp;

H1161.notes: Transliteration: biʻûwthîym; Pronunciation: be-oo-theme'; Derivation: masculine plural from H1204 (בָּעַת);

H1162.meaning: Boaz, the ancestor of David; also the name of a pillar in front of the temple
H1162.notes: Transliteration: Bôʻaz; Pronunciation: bo'-az; Derivation: from an unused root of uncertain meaning;

H1163.notes: Transliteration: bâʻaṭ; Pronunciation: baw-at'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1164.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻîy; Pronunciation: beh-ee'; Derivation: from H1158 (בָּעָה);

H1165.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻîyr; Pronunciation: beh-ere'; Derivation: from H1197 (בָּעַר) (in the sense of eating);

H1166.meaning: to be master; hence, to marry
H1166.kjv: have dominion (over), be husband, marry(-ried, [idiom] wife).
H1166.notes: Transliteration: bâʻal; Pronunciation: baw-al'; Derivation: a primitive root; also as denominative from H1167 (בַּעַל)

H1167.meaning: a master; hence, a husband, or (figuratively) owner (often used with another noun in modifications of this latter sense)
H1167.kjv: [phrase] archer, [phrase] babbler, [phrase] bird, captain, chief man, [phrase] confederate, [phrase] have to do, [phrase] dreamer, those to whom it is due, [phrase] furious, those that are given to it, great, [phrase] hairy, he that hath it, have, [phrase] horseman, husband, lord, man, [phrase] married, master, person, [phrase] sworn, they of.
H1167.notes: Transliteration: baʻal; Pronunciation: bah'-al; Derivation: from H1166 (בָּעַל);

H1168.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal; Pronunciation: bah'-al; Derivation: the same as H1167 (בַּעַל);

H1169.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻêl; Pronunciation: beh-ale'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1167 (בַּעַל)

H1170.meaning: Baal-Berith, a special deity of the Shechemites
H1170.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Bᵉrîyth; Pronunciation: bah'-al ber-eeth'; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H1285 (בְּרִית); Baal of (the) covenant;

H1171.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Gâd; Pronunciation: bah'-al gawd; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H1409 (גָּד); Baal of Fortune;

H1172.notes: Transliteration: baʻălâh; Pronunciation: bah-al-aw'; Derivation: feminine of H1167 (בַּעַל);

H1173.meaning: Baalah, the name of three places in Palestine
H1173.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălâh; Pronunciation: bah-al-aw'; Derivation: the same as H1172 (בַּעֲלָה);

H1174.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Hâmôwn; Pronunciation: bah'-al haw-mone'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H1995 (הָמוֹן); possessor of a multitude;

H1175.kjv: Bealoth, in Aloth (by mistake for a plural from H5927 (עָלָה) with prepositional prefix).
H1175.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉʻâlôwth; Pronunciation: beh-aw-loth'; Derivation: plural of H1172 (בַּעֲלָה); mistresses;

H1176.meaning: Baal-Zebub, a special deity of the Ekronites
H1176.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Zᵉbûwb; Pronunciation: bah'-al zeb-oob'; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H2070 (זְבוּב); Baal of (the) Fly;

H1177.meaning: Baal-Chanan, the name of an Edomite, also of an Israelite
H1177.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Chânân; Pronunciation: bah'-al khaw-nawn'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H2603 (חָנַן); possessor of grace;

H1178.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Châtsôwr; Pronunciation: bah'-al khaw-tsore'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and a modification of H2691 (חָצֵר); possessor of a village;

H1179.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Chermôwn; Pronunciation: bah'-al kher-mone'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H2768 (חֶרְמוֹן); possessor of Hermon;

H1180.meaning: Baali, a symbolical name for Jehovah
H1180.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălîy; Pronunciation: bah-al-ee'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) with pron. suff.; my master;

H1181.meaning: Baale-Bamoth, a place East of the Jordan
H1181.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălêy Bâmôwth; Pronunciation: bah-al-ay' baw-moth'; Derivation: from the plural of H1168 (בַּעַל) and the plural of H1116 (בָּמָה); Baals of (the) heights;

H1182.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉʻelyâdâʻ; Pronunciation: beh-el-yaw-daw'; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H3045 (יָדַע); Baal has known;

H1183.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉʻalyâh; Pronunciation: beh-al-yaw'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah (is) master;

H1184.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălêy Yᵉhûwdâh; Pronunciation: bah-al-ay' yeh-hoo-daw'; Derivation: from the plural of H1167 (בַּעַל) and H3063 (יְהוּדָה); masters of Judah;

H1185.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălîyç; Pronunciation: bah-al-ece'; Derivation: probably from a derivative of H5965 (עָלַס) with prepositional prefix; in exultation;

H1186.meaning: Baal-Meon, a place East of the Jordan
H1186.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Mᵉʻôwn; Pronunciation: bah-al meh-one'; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H4583 (מָעוֹן); Baal of (the) habitation (of) (compare H1010 (בֵּית בַּעַל מְעוֹן));

H1187.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Pᵉʻôwr; Pronunciation: bah'-al peh-ore'; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H6465 (פְּעוֹר); Baal of Peor;

H1188.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Pᵉrâtsîym; Pronunciation: bah'-al per-aw-tseem'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and the plural of H6556 (פֶּרֶץ); possessor of breaches;

H1189.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Tsᵉphôwn; Pronunciation: bah'-al tsef-one'

H1190.meaning: Baal of Shalishah, Baal-Shalishah, a place in Palestine
H1190.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Shâlishâh; Pronunciation: bah'-al shaw-lee-shaw'; Derivation: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H8031 (שָׁלִשָׁה);

H1191.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălâth; Pronunciation: bah-al-awth'; Derivation: a modification of H1172 (בַּעֲלָה); mistressship;

H1192.notes: Transliteration: Baʻălath Bᵉʼêr; Pronunciation: bah-al-ath' beh-ayr'; Derivation: from H1172 (בַּעֲלָה) and H875 (בְּאֵר); mistress of a well;

H1193.notes: Transliteration: Baʻal Tâmâr; Pronunciation: bah'-al taw-mawr'; Derivation: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H8558 (תָּמָר); possessor of (the) palm-tree;

H1194.meaning: Beon, a place East of the Jordan
H1194.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉʻôn; Pronunciation: beh-ohn'; Derivation: probably a contraction of H1010 (בֵּית בַּעַל מְעוֹן);

H1195.meaning: Banana, the name of four Israelite
H1195.notes: Transliteration: Baʻănâʼ; Pronunciation: bah-an-aw'; Derivation: the same as H1196 (בַּעֲנָה);

H1196.meaning: Baanah, the name of four Israelites
H1196.notes: Transliteration: Baʻănâh; Pronunciation: bah-an-aw'; Derivation: from a derivative of H6031 (עָנָה) with prepositional prefix; in affliction

H1197.meaning: to kindle, i.e. consume (by fire or by eating);  to be(-come) brutish
H1197.kjv: be brutish, bring (put, take) away, burn, (cause to) eat (up), feed, heat, kindle, set (on fire), waste.
H1197.notes: Transliteration: bâʻar; Pronunciation: baw-ar'; Derivation: a primitive root; also as denominative from H1198 (בַּעַר)

H1198.meaning: properly, foot (as consumed); i.e. (by exten.) of cattle brutishness; (concretely) stupid
H1198.notes: Transliteration: baʻar; Pronunciation: bah'-ar; Derivation: from H1197 (בָּעַר);

H1199.notes: Transliteration: Bâʻărâʼ; Pronunciation: bah-ar-aw'; Derivation: from H1198 (בַּעַר); brutish;

H1200.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻêrâh; Pronunciation: be-ay-raw'; Derivation: from H1197 (בָּעַר);

H1201.notes: Transliteration: Baʻshâʼ; Pronunciation: bah-shaw'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to stink; offensiveness;

H1202.notes: Transliteration: Baʻăsêyâh; Pronunciation: bah-as-ay-yaw'; Derivation: from H6213 (עָשָׂה) and H3050 (יָהּ) with a prepositional prefix; in (the) work of Jah;

H1203.meaning: Beeshterah, a place East of the Jordan
H1203.notes: Transliteration: Bᵉʻeshtᵉrâh; Pronunciation: beh-esh-ter-aw'; Derivation: from H6251 (עַשְׁתְּרָה) (as singular of H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת)) with a prepositional prefix; with Ashtoreth;

H1204.notes: Transliteration: bâʻath; Pronunciation: baw-ath'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H1205.notes: Transliteration: bᵉʻâthâh; Pronunciation: beh-aw-thaw'; Derivation: from H1204 (בָּעַת);

H1206.notes: Transliteration: bôts; Pronunciation: botse; Derivation: probably the same as H948 (בּוּץ);

H1207.notes: Transliteration: bitstsâh; Pronunciation: bits-tsaw'; Derivation: intensive from H1206 (בֹץ);

H1208.notes: Transliteration: bâtsôwr; Pronunciation: baw-tsore'; Derivation: from H1219 (בָּצַר);

H1209.meaning: Betsai, the name of two Israelites
H1209.notes: Transliteration: Bêtsay; Pronunciation: bay-tsah'-ee; Derivation: perhaps the same as H1153 (בְּסַי);

H2621.notes: Transliteration: Chôçâh; Pronunciation: kho-saw'; Derivation: from H2620 (חָסָה); hopeful;

H2622.notes: Transliteration: châçûwth; Pronunciation: khaw-sooth'; Derivation: from H2620 (חָסָה);

H2623.kjv: godly (man), good, holy (one), merciful, saint, (un-) godly.
H2623.notes: Transliteration: châçîyd; Pronunciation: khaw-seed'; Derivation: from H2616 (חָסַד);

H2624.meaning: the kind (maternal) bird, i.e. a stork
H2624.notes: Transliteration: chăçîydâh; Pronunciation: khas-ee-daw'; Derivation: feminine of H2623 (חָסִיד);

H2625.notes: Transliteration: châçîyl; Pronunciation: khaw-seel'; Derivation: from H2628 (חָסַל);

H2626.notes: Transliteration: chăçîyn; Pronunciation: khas-een'; Derivation: from H2630 (חָסַן);

H2627.notes: Transliteration: chaççîyr; Pronunciation: khas-seer'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H2637 (חָסֵר);

H2628.notes: Transliteration: châçal; Pronunciation: khaw-sal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2629.meaning: to muzzle; by analogy, to stop the nose
H2629.notes: Transliteration: châçam; Pronunciation: khaw-sam'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2630.meaning: properly, to (be) compact; by implication, to hoard
H2630.notes: Transliteration: châçan; Pronunciation: khaw-san'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2631.notes: Transliteration: chăçan; Pronunciation: khas-an'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H2630 (חָסַן);

H2632.notes: Transliteration: chêçen; Pronunciation: khay'-sen; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H2631 (חֲסַן);

H2633.notes: Transliteration: chôçen; Pronunciation: kho'-sen; Derivation: from H2630 (חָסַן);

H2634.notes: Transliteration: châçôn; Pronunciation: khaw-sone'; Derivation: from H2630 (חָסַן);

H2635.notes: Transliteration: chăçaph; Pronunciation: khas-af'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to that of H2636 (חַסְפַּס);

H2636.notes: Transliteration: chaçpaç; Pronunciation: khas-pas'; Derivation: reduplicated from an unused root meaning apparently to peel;

H2637.meaning: to lack; by implication, to fail, want, lessen
H2637.kjv: be abated, bereave, decrease, (cause to) fail, (have) lack, make lower, want.
H2637.notes: Transliteration: châçêr; Pronunciation: khaw-sare'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2638.kjv: destitute, fail, lack, have need, void, want.
H2638.notes: Transliteration: châçêr; Pronunciation: khaw-sare'; Derivation: from H2637 (חָסֵר);

H2639.notes: Transliteration: cheçer; Pronunciation: kheh'-ler; Derivation: from H2637 (חָסֵר);

H2640.notes: Transliteration: chôçer; Pronunciation: kho'-ser; Derivation: from H2637 (חָסֵר);

H2641.notes: Transliteration: Chaçrâh; Pronunciation: khas-raw'; Derivation: from H2637 (חָסֵר); want

H2642.notes: Transliteration: cheçrôwn; Pronunciation: khes-rone'; Derivation: from H2637 (חָסֵר);

H2643.notes: Transliteration: chaph; Pronunciation: khaf; Derivation: from H2653 (חׇפַף) (in the moral sense of covered from soil);

H2644.meaning: properly, to cover, i.e. (in a sinister sense) to act covertly
H2644.notes: Transliteration: châphâʼ; Pronunciation: khaw-faw'; Derivation: an orthographical variation of H2645 (חָפָה);

H2645.meaning: to cover; by implication, to veil, to encase, protect
H2645.notes: Transliteration: châphâh; Pronunciation: khaw-faw'; Derivation: a primitive root (compare H2644 (חָפָא), H2653 (חׇפַף));

H2646.notes: Transliteration: chuppâh; Pronunciation: khoop-paw'; Derivation: from H2645 (חָפָה);

H2647.notes: Transliteration: Chuppâh; Pronunciation: khoop-paw'; Derivation: the same as H2646 (חֻפָּה);

H2648.meaning: properly, to start up suddenly, i.e. (by implication) to hasten away, to fear
H2648.notes: Transliteration: châphaz; Pronunciation: khaw-faz'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2649.notes: Transliteration: chippâzôwn; Pronunciation: khip-paw-zone'; Derivation: from H2468 (חֻלְדָּה);

H2650.notes: Transliteration: Chuppîym; Pronunciation: khoop-peem'; Derivation: plural of H2646 (חֻפָּה) (compare H2349 (חוּפָם));

H2651.notes: Transliteration: chôphen; Pronunciation: kho'-fen; Derivation: from an unused root of uncertain signification;

H2652.notes: Transliteration: Chophnîy; Pronunciation: khof-nee'; Derivation: from H2651 (חֹפֶן); perhaps pugilist;

H2653.notes: Transliteration: chophaph; Pronunciation: khaw-faf'; Derivation: a primitive root (compare H2645 (חָפָה), H3182 (יָחֵף));

H2654.meaning: properly, to incline to; by implication (literally but rarely) to bend; figuratively, to be pleased with, desire
H2654.kjv: [idiom] any at all, (have, take) delight, desire, favour, like, move, be (well) pleased, have pleasure, will, would.
H2654.notes: Transliteration: châphêts; Pronunciation: khaw-fates'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2655.kjv: delight in, desire, favour, please, have pleasure, whosoever would, willing, wish.
H2655.notes: Transliteration: châphêts; Pronunciation: khaw-fates'; Derivation: from H2654 (חָפֵץ);

H2656.meaning: pleasure; hence (abstractly) desire; concretely, a valuable thing; hence (by extension) a matter (as something in mind)
H2656.kjv: acceptable, delight(-some), desire, things desired, matter, pleasant(-ure), purpose, willingly.
H2656.notes: Transliteration: chêphets; Pronunciation: khay'-fets; Derivation: from H2654 (חָפֵץ);

H2657.meaning: Cheptsi-bah, a fanciful name for Palestine
H2657.notes: Transliteration: Chephtsîy bâhh; Pronunciation: khef-tsee'baw; Derivation: from H2656 (חֵפֶץ) with suffixes; my delight (is) in her;

H2658.meaning: properly, to pry into; by implication, to delve, to explore
H2658.notes: Transliteration: châphar; Pronunciation: khaw-far'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2659.meaning: to blush; figuratively, to be ashamed, disappointed; causatively, to shame, reproach
H2659.kjv: be ashamed, be confounded, be brought to confusion (unto shame), come (be put to) shame, bring reproach.
H2659.notes: Transliteration: châphêr; Pronunciation: khaw-fare'; Derivation: a primitive root (perhaps rather the same as H2658 (חָפַר) through the idea of detection);

H2660.meaning: Chepher, a place in Palestine; also the name of three Israelites
H2660.notes: Transliteration: Chêpher; Pronunciation: khay'-fer; Derivation: from H2658 (חָפַר) or H2659 (חָפֵר); a pit of shame;

H2661.meaning: a burrower, i.e. probably a rat

H2662.meaning: a Chephrite (collectively) or descendants of Chepher
H2662.notes: Transliteration: Chephrîy; Pronunciation: khef-ree'; Derivation: patronymically from H2660 (חֵפֶר);

H2663.notes: Transliteration: Chăphârayim; Pronunciation: khaf-aw-rah'-yim; Derivation: dual of H2660 (חֵפֶר); double pit;

H2664.meaning: to seek; causatively, to conceal oneself (i.e. let be sought), or mask
H2664.kjv: change, (make) diligent (search), disquise self, hide, search (for, out).
H2664.notes: Transliteration: châphas; Pronunciation: khaw-fas'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2665.notes: Transliteration: chêphes; Pronunciation: khay'-fes; Derivation: from H2664 (חָפַשׂ);

H2666.meaning: to spread loose; figuratively, to manumit
H2666.notes: Transliteration: châphash; Pronunciation: khaw-fash'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2667.meaning: something spread loosely, i.e. a carpet
H2667.notes: Transliteration: Chôphesh; Pronunciation: kho'-fesh; Derivation: from H2666 (חָפַשׁ);

H2668.notes: Transliteration: chuphshâh; Pronunciation: khoof-shaw'; Derivation: from H2666 (חָפַשׁ);

H2669.notes: Transliteration: chophshûwth; Pronunciation: khof-shooth'; Derivation: and חׇפְשִׁית; from H2666 (חָפַשׁ); (with H1004 (בַּיִת), a hospital)

H2670.notes: Transliteration: chophshîy; Pronunciation: khof-shee'; Derivation: from H2666 (חָפַשׁ);

H2671.meaning: properly, a piercer, i.e. an arrow; by implication, a wound; figuratively, (of God) thunderbolt; the shaft of aspear
H2671.kjv: [phrase] archer, arrow, dart, shaft, staff, wound.
H2671.notes: Transliteration: chêts; Pronunciation: khayts; Derivation: from H2686 (חָצַץ); also by interchange for H6086 (עֵץ)

H2672.meaning: to cut or carve (wood, stone or other material); by implication, to hew, split, square, quarry, engrave
H2672.kjv: cut, dig, divide, grave, hew (out, -er), made, mason.
H2672.notes: Transliteration: châtsab; Pronunciation: khaw-tsab'; Derivation: or חָצֵב; a primitive root;

H2673.meaning: to cut or split in two; to halve

H2673.notes: Transliteration: châtsâh; Pronunciation: khaw-tsaw'; Derivation: a primitive root (compare H2086 (זֵד)));

H2676.notes: Transliteration: châtsôwth; Pronunciation: khaw-tsoth'; Derivation: from H2673 (חָצָה)lemma חָעָה ayin, corrected to חָצָה;

H2677.kjv: half, middle, mid(-night), midst, part, two parts.
H2677.notes: Transliteration: chêtsîy; Pronunciation: khay-tsee'; Derivation: from H2673 (חָצָה)lemma חָעָה ayin, corrected to חָצָה;

H2678.notes: Transliteration: chitstsîy; Pronunciation: khits-tsee'; Derivation: or חֵצִי; prolongation from H2671 (חֵץ);

H2679.notes: Transliteration: Chătsîy ham-Mᵉnuchôwth; Pronunciation: chat-tsee' hammen-oo-khoth'; Derivation: from H2677 (חֵצִי) and the plural of H4496 (מְנוּחָה), with the article interposed; midst of the resting-places;

H2680.meaning: a Chatsi-ham-Menachtite or descendant of Chatsi-ham-Menuchoth
H2680.notes: Transliteration: Chătsîy ham-Mᵉnachtîy; Pronunciation: khat-see' ham-menakh-tee'; Derivation: patronymically from H2679 (חֲצִי הַמְּנֻחוֹת);

H2681.notes: Transliteration: châtsîyr; Pronunciation: khaw-tseer'; Derivation: a collateral form of H2691 (חָצֵר);

H2682.notes: Transliteration: châtsîyr; Pronunciation: khaw-tseer'; Derivation: perhaps originally the same as H2681 (חָצִיר), from the greenness of a courtyard;

H2683.meaning: the bosom (as comprised between the arms)
H2683.notes: Transliteration: chêtsen; Pronunciation: khay'-tsen; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to hold firmly;

H2684.meaning: the bosom (as comprised between the arms)
H2684.notes: Transliteration: chôtsen; Pronunciation: kho'tsen; Derivation: a collateral form of H2683 (חֵצֶן), and meaning the same

H2685.meaning: properly, to shear or cut close; figuratively, to be severe
H2685.notes: Transliteration: chătsaph; Pronunciation: khats-af'; Derivation: (Aramaic) a primitive root;

H2686.meaning: properly, to chop into, pierce or sever; hence, to curtail, to distribute (into ranks);  to shoot an arrow
H2686.kjv: archer, [idiom] bands, cut off in the midst.
H2686.notes: Transliteration: châtsats; Pronunciation: khaw-tsats'; Derivation: a primitive root (compare H2673 (חָצָה)lemma חָעָה ayin, corrected to חָצָה); also as denominative from H2671 (חֵץ)

H2687.meaning: properly, something cutting; hence, gravel (as grit); an arrow
H2687.notes: Transliteration: châtsâts; Pronunciation: khaw-tsawts'; Derivation: from H2687 (חָצָץ);  also like H2671 (חֵץ)

H2688.notes: Transliteration: Chatsᵉtsôwn Tâmâr; Pronunciation: khats-ets-one' taw-mawr'; Derivation: or חַצֲצֹן תָּמָר; from H2686 (חָצַץ) and H8558 (תָּמָר); division (i.e. perhaps row) of (the) palm-tree;

H2689.meaning: a trumpet (from its sundered or quavering note)

H2691.meaning: a yard (as inclosed by a fence); also a hamlet (as similarly surrounded with walls)

H2692.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar ʼAddâr; Pronunciation: khats-ar' addawr'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H146 (אַדָּר); (the) village of Addar;

H2693.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar Gaddâh; Pronunciation: khats-ar' gad-daw'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and a feminine of H1408 (גַּד); (the) village of (female) Fortune;

H2694.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar hat-Tîykôwn; Pronunciation: khats-ar' hat-tee-kone'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H8484 (תִּיכוֹן) with the article interposed; village of the middle;

H2695.notes: Transliteration: Chetsrôw; Pronunciation: khets-ro'; Derivation: by an orthographical variation for H2696 (חֶצְרוֹן); enclosure;

H2696.meaning: Chetsron, the name of a place in Palestine; also of two Israelites
H2696.notes: Transliteration: Chetsrôwn; Pronunciation: khets-rone'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר); court-yard;

H2697.meaning: a Chetsronite or (collectively) descendants of Chetsron
H2697.notes: Transliteration: Chetsrôwnîy; Pronunciation: khets-ro-nee'; Derivation: patronymically from H2696 (חֶצְרוֹן);

H2698.notes: Transliteration: Chătsêrôwth; Pronunciation: khats-ay-roth'; Derivation: feminine plural of H2691 (חָצֵר); yards;

H2699.notes: Transliteration: Chătsêrîym; Pronunciation: khats-ay-reem'; Derivation: plural masculine of H2691 (חָצֵר); yards;

H2700.notes: Transliteration: Chătsarmâveth; Pronunciation: khats-ar-maw'-veth; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H4194 (מָוֶת); village of death;

H2701.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar Çûwçâh; Pronunciation: khats-ar'soo-saw'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H5484 (סוּסָה); village of cavalry;

H2702.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar Çûwçîym; Pronunciation: khats-ar' soo-seem'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and the plural of H5483 (סוּס); village of horses;

H2703.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar ʻÊynôwn; Pronunciation: khats-ar' ay-none'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and a derivative of H5869 (עַיִן); village of springs;

H2704.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar ʻÊynân; Pronunciation: khats-ar' ay-nawn'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and the same as H5881 (עֵינָן); village of springs;

H2705.notes: Transliteration: Chătsar Shûwʻâl; Pronunciation: khats-ar' shoo-awl'; Derivation: from H2691 (חָצֵר) and H7776 (שׁוּעָל); village of (the) fox;

H2706.meaning: an enactment; hence, an appointment (of time, space, quantity, labor or usage)
H2706.kjv: appointed, bound, commandment, convenient, custom, decree(-d), due, law, measure, [idiom] necessary, ordinance(-nary), portion, set time, statute, task.
H2706.notes: Transliteration: chôq; Pronunciation: khoke; Derivation: from H2710 (חָקַק);

H2707.meaning: to carve; by implication, to delineate; also to entrench
H2707.kjv: carved work, portrayed, set a print.
H2707.notes: Transliteration: châqâh; Pronunciation: khaw-kaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2708.meaning: an enactment; hence, an appointment (of time, space, quantity, labor or usage)
H2708.kjv: appointed, custom, manner, ordinance, site, statute.
H2708.notes: Transliteration: chuqqâh; Pronunciation: khook-kaw'; Derivation: feminine of H2706 (חֹק), and meaning substantially the same

H2709.notes: Transliteration: Chăqûwphâʼ; Pronunciation: khah-oo-faw'; Derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to bend; crooked;

H2710.meaning: properly, to hack, i.e. engrave (Judges 5:14, to be a scribe simply); by implication, to enact (laws being cut in stone or metal tablets in primitive times) or (gen.) prescribe
H2710.kjv: appoint, decree, governor, grave, lawgiver, note, pourtray, print, set.
H2710.notes: Transliteration: châqaq; Pronunciation: khaw-kak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2711.notes: Transliteration: chêqeq; Pronunciation: khay'-kek; Derivation: from H2710 (חָקַק);

H2712.meaning: Chukkok or Chukok, a place in Palestine
H2712.notes: Transliteration: Chuqqôq; Pronunciation: Khook-koke'; Derivation: or (fully) חוּקֹק; from H2710 (חָקַק); appointed;

H2713.meaning: properly, to penetrate; hence, to examine intimately
H2713.kjv: find out, (make) search (out), seek (out), sound, try.
H2713.notes: Transliteration: châqar; Pronunciation: khaw-kar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2714.notes: Transliteration: chêqer; Pronunciation: khay'-ker; Derivation: from H2713 (חָקַר);

H2715.meaning: properly, white or pure (from the cleansing or shining power of fire; hence (figuratively) noble (in rank)
H2715.notes: Transliteration: chôr; Pronunciation: khore; Derivation: or (fully) חוֹר; from H2787 (חָרַר); (compare H2751 (חֹרִי))

H2716.notes: Transliteration: chereʼ; Pronunciation: kheh'-reh; Derivation: from an unused (and vulgar) root probably meaning to evacuate the bowels;

H2717.meaning: to parch (through drought) i.e. (by analogy,) to desolate, destroy, kill
H2717.kjv: decay, (be) desolate, destroy(-er), (be) dry (up), slay, [idiom] surely, (lay, lie, make) waste.
H2717.notes: Transliteration: chârab; Pronunciation: khaw-rab'; Derivation: or חֲרֵב; a primitive root;

H2718.notes: Transliteration: chărab; Pronunciation: khar-ab'; Derivation: (Aramaic) a root corresponding to H2717 (חָרַב);

H2719.meaning: drought; also a cutting instrument (from its destructive effect), as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement
H2719.kjv: axe, dagger, knife, mattock, sword, tool.
H2719.notes: Transliteration: chereb; Pronunciation: kheh'-reb; Derivation: from H2717 (חָרַב);

H2720.notes: Transliteration: chârêb; Pronunciation: khaw-rabe'; Derivation: from H2717 (חָרַב);

H2721.kjv: desolation, drought, dry, heat, [idiom] utterly, waste.
H2721.notes: Transliteration: chôreb; Pronunciation: kho'-reb; Derivation: a collaterally form of H2719 (חֶרֶב);

H2722.meaning: Choreb, a (generic) name for the Sinaitic mountains
H2722.notes: Transliteration: Chôrêb; Pronunciation: kho-rabe'; Derivation: from H2717 (חָרַב); desolate;

H2723.meaning: properly, drought, i.e. (by implication) a desolation
H2723.notes: Transliteration: chorbâh; Pronunciation: khor-baw'; Derivation: feminine of H2721 (חֹרֶב);

H2724.notes: Transliteration: chârâbâh; Pronunciation: khaw-raw-baw'; Derivation: feminine of H2720 (חָרֵב);

H2725.notes: Transliteration: chărâbôwn; Pronunciation: khar-aw-bone'; Derivation: from H2717 (חָרַב);

H2726.meaning: Charbona or Charbonah, a eunuch of Xerxes
H2726.notes: Transliteration: Charbôwnâʼ; Pronunciation: khar-bo-naw'; Derivation: or חַרְבוֹנָה; of Persian origin;

H2727.meaning: properly, to leap suddenly, i.e. (by implication) to be dismayed
H2727.notes: Transliteration: chârag; Pronunciation: khaw-rag'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2728.meaning: the leaping insect, i.e. a locust
H2728.notes: Transliteration: chârᵉgôl; Pronunciation: khar-gole'; Derivation: from H2727 (חָרַג);

H2729.meaning: to shudder with terror; hence, to fear; also to hasten (with anxiety)
H2729.kjv: be (make) afraid, be careful, discomfit, fray (away), quake, tremble.
H2729.notes: Transliteration: chârad; Pronunciation: khaw-rad'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2730.notes: Transliteration: chârêd; Pronunciation: khaw-rade'; Derivation: from H2729 (חָרַד);

H2731.notes: Transliteration: chărâdâh; Pronunciation: khar-aw-daw'; Derivation: feminine of H2730 (חָרֵד);

H2732.meaning: Charadah, a place in the Desert
H2732.notes: Transliteration: Chărâdâh; Pronunciation: khar-aw-daw'; Derivation: the same as H2731 (חֲרָדָה);

H2733.meaning: a Charodite, or inhabitant of Charod
H2733.notes: Transliteration: Chărôdîy; Pronunciation: khar-o-dee'; Derivation: patrial from a derivative of H2729 (חָרַד) (compare H5878 (עֵין חֲרֹד));

H2734.meaning: to glow or grow warm; figuratively (usually) to blaze up, of anger, zeal, jealousy
H2734.kjv: be angry, burn, be displeased, [idiom] earnestly, fret self, grieve, be (wax) hot, be incensed, kindle, [idiom] very, be wroth. See H8474 (תַּחָרָה).
H2734.notes: Transliteration: chârâh; Pronunciation: khaw-raw'; Derivation: a primitive root (compare H2787 (חָרַר));

H2735.meaning: Chor-hag-Gidgad, a place in the Desert
H2735.notes: Transliteration: Chôr hag-Gidgâd; Pronunciation: khore hag-ghid-gawd'

H2736.notes: Transliteration: Charhăyâh; Pronunciation: khar-hah-yaw'; Derivation: ; from H2734 (חָרָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); fearing Jah;

H2737.meaning: properly, pierced, i.e. a bead of pearl, gems or jewels (as strung)

H2742.notes: Transliteration: chărûwts; Pronunciation: khaw-roots'; Derivation: or חָרֻץ; passive participle of H2782 (חָרַץ);

H2743.notes: Transliteration: Chârûwts; Pronunciation: khaw-roots'; Derivation: the same as H2742 (חֲרוּץ); earnest;

H2744.notes: Transliteration: Charchûwr; Pronunciation: khar-khoor'; Derivation: a fuller form of H2746 (חַרְחֻר); inflammation;

H2745.notes: Transliteration: Charchaç; Pronunciation: khar-khas'; Derivation: from the same as H2775 (חֶרֶס); perhaps shining;

H2746.notes: Transliteration: charchur; Pronunciation: khar-khoor'; Derivation: from H2787 (חָרַר);

H2747.meaning: a chisel or graver; also a style forwriting
H2747.notes: Transliteration: chereṭ; Pronunciation: kheh'-ret; Derivation: from a primitive root meaning to engrave;

H2748.meaning: a horoscopist (as drawing magical lines or circles)
H2748.notes: Transliteration: charṭôm; Pronunciation: khar-tome'; Derivation: from the same as H2747 (חֶרֶט);

H2749.meaning: a horoscopist (as drawing magical lines or circles)
H2749.notes: Transliteration: charṭôm; Pronunciation: khar-tome'; Derivation: (Aramaic) the same as H2748 (חַרְטֹם)

H2750.notes: Transliteration: chŏrîy; Pronunciation: khor-ee'; Derivation: from H2734 (חָרָה);

H2751.notes: Transliteration: chôrîy; Pronunciation: kho-ree'; Derivation: from the same as H2353 (חוּר);

H2752.notes: Transliteration: Chôrîy; Pronunciation: kho-ree'; Derivation: from H2356 (חוֹר); cave-dweller or troglodyte;

H2753.meaning: Chori, the name of two men
H2753.notes: Transliteration: Chôrîy; Pronunciation: kho-ree'; Derivation: or חוֹרִי; the same as H2752 (חֹרִי);

H2754.notes: Transliteration: chârîyṭ; Pronunciation: khaw-reet'; Derivation: or חָרִט; from the same as H2747 (חֶרֶט);

H2756.notes: Transliteration: Chârîyph; Pronunciation: khaw-reef'; Derivation: from H2778 (חָרַף); autumnal;

H2757.meaning: properly, incisure or (passively) incised; hence, a threshing-sledge (with sharp teeth); also a slice (as cut)
H2757.notes: Transliteration: chârîyts; Pronunciation: khaw-reets'; Derivation: or חֲרִץ; from H2782 (חָרַץ); (compare H2742 (חֲרוּץ))

H2758.notes: Transliteration: chârîysh; Pronunciation: khaw-reesh'; Derivation: from H2790 (חָרַשׁ);

H2759.meaning: quiet, i.e. sultry (as feminine noun, the sirocco or hot east wind)
H2759.notes: Transliteration: chărîyshîy; Pronunciation: khar-ee-shee'; Derivation: from H2790 (חָרַשׁ) in the sense of silence;

H2760.meaning: to braid (i.e. to entangle or snare) or catch (game) in anet
H2760.notes: Transliteration: chârak; Pronunciation: khaw-rak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2765.notes: Transliteration: Chŏrêm; Pronunciation: khor-ame'; Derivation: from H2763 (חָרַם); devoted;

H2766.notes: Transliteration: Chârim; Pronunciation: khaw-reem'; Derivation: from H2763 (חָרַם); snub-nosed;

H2767.notes: Transliteration: Chormâh; Pronunciation: khor-maw'; Derivation: from H2763 (חָרַם); devoted;

H2768.notes: Transliteration: Chermôwn; Pronunciation: kher-mone'; Derivation: from H2763 (חָרַם); abrupt;

H2769.notes: Transliteration: Chermôwnîym; Pronunciation: kher-mo-neem'; Derivation: plural of H2768 (חֶרְמוֹן);

H2770.notes: Transliteration: chermêsh; Pronunciation: kher-mashe'; Derivation: from H2763 (חָרַם);

H2771.meaning: Charan, the name of a man and also of a place
H2771.notes: Transliteration: Chârân; Pronunciation: kaw-rawn'; Derivation: from H2787 (חָרַר); parched;

H2772.meaning: a Choronite or inhabitant of Choronaim
H2772.notes: Transliteration: Chôrônîy; Pronunciation: kho-ro-nee'; Derivation: patrial from H2773 (חֹרֹנַיִם);

H2773.notes: Transliteration: Chôrônayim; Pronunciation: kho-ro-nah'-yim; Derivation: dual of a derivative from H2356 (חוֹר); double cave-town;

H2774.notes: Transliteration: Charnepher; Pronunciation: khar-neh'fer; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H2776.notes: Transliteration: Chereç; Pronunciation: kheh'-res; Derivation: the same as H2775 (חֶרֶס); shining;

H2777.meaning: a potsherd, i.e. (by implication) a pottery; the name of a gate at Jerusalem
H2777.notes: Transliteration: charçûwth; Pronunciation: khar-sooth'; Derivation: from H2775 (חֶרֶס) (apparently in the sense of a red tile used for scraping);

H2778.meaning: to pull off, i.e. (by implication) to expose (as by stripping); specifically, to betroth (as if a surrender); figuratively, to carp at, i.e. defame; to spend the winter

H2779.meaning: properly, the crop gathered, i.e. (by implication) the autumn (and winter) season; figuratively, ripeness of age
H2779.notes: Transliteration: chôreph; Pronunciation: kho'-ref; Derivation: from H2778 (חָרַף);

H2780.notes: Transliteration: Chârêph; Pronunciation: khaw-rafe'; Derivation: from H2778 (חָרַף); reproachful;

H2781.notes: Transliteration: cherpâh; Pronunciation: kher-paw'; Derivation: from H2778 (חָרַף);

H2782.meaning: properly, to point sharply, i.e. (literally) to wound; figuratively, to be alert, to decide
