H2784.notes: Transliteration: chartsubbâh; Pronunciation: khar-tsoob-baw'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H2785.meaning: a sour grape (as sharp in taste)
H2785.notes: Transliteration: chartsan; Pronunciation: kchar-tsan'; Derivation: from H2782 (חָרַץ);

H2786.notes: Transliteration: châraq; Pronunciation: khaw-rak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2787.meaning: to glow, i.e. literally (to melt, burn, dry up) or figuratively (to show or incite passion)

H2788.notes: Transliteration: chârêr; Pronunciation: khaw-rare'; Derivation: from H2787 (חָרַר);

H2791.notes: Transliteration: cheresh; Pronunciation: kheh'-resh; Derivation: from H2790 (חָרַשׁ);

H2792.notes: Transliteration: Cheresh; Pronunciation: kheh'-resh; Derivation: the same as H2791 (חֶרֶשׁ)

H2793.meaning: a forest (perhaps as furnishing the material for fabric)

H2794.notes: Transliteration: chôrêsh; Pronunciation: kho-rashe'; Derivation: active participle of H2790 (חָרַשׁ);

H2795.notes: Transliteration: chêrêsh; Pronunciation: khay-rashe'; Derivation: from H2790 (חָרַשׁ);

H2796.kjv: artificer, ([phrase]) carpenter, craftsman, engraver, maker, [phrase] mason, skilful, ([phrase]) smith, worker, workman, such as wrought.

H2797.notes: Transliteration: Charshâʼ; Pronunciation: khar-shaw'; Derivation: from H2792 (חֶרֶשׁ); magician;

H2798.meaning: mechanics, the name of a valley in Jerusalem

H2799.notes: Transliteration: chărôsheth; Pronunciation: khar-o'-sheth; Derivation: from H2790 (חָרַשׁ);

H2805.meaning: a belt or strap (as being interlaced)
H2805.notes: Transliteration: chêsheb; Pronunciation: khay'-sheb; Derivation: from H2803 (חָשַׁב);

H2807.notes: Transliteration: Chăshubâh; Pronunciation: khash-oo-baw'; Derivation: from H2803 (חָשַׁב); estimation;

H2809.meaning: Cheshbon, a place East of the Jordan

H2817.notes: Transliteration: Chăsûwphâʼ; Pronunciation: khas-oo-faw'; Derivation: or חֲשֻׂפָא; from H2834 (חָשַׂף); nakedness;

H2825.notes: Transliteration: chăshêkâh; Pronunciation: khash-ay-kaw'; Derivation: or חֲשֵׁיכָהxlit chăshêkâh corrected to chăshêykâh; from H2821 (חָשַׁךְ);

H2826.meaning: to make (intrans. be) unsteady, i.e. weak
H2826.notes: Transliteration: châshal; Pronunciation: khaw-shal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2827.notes: Transliteration: chăshal; Pronunciation: khash-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) a root corresponding to H2826 (חָשַׁל);

H2828.meaning: Chashum, the name of two or three Israelites
H2828.notes: Transliteration: Châshum; Pronunciation: khaw-shoom'; Derivation: from the same as H2831 (חַשְׁמַן); enriched;

H2829.notes: Transliteration: Cheshmôwn; Pronunciation: klesh-mone'; Derivation: the same as H2831 (חַשְׁמַן); opulent;

H2830.meaning: probably bronze or polished spectrum metal
H2830.notes: Transliteration: chashmal; Pronunciation: khash-mal'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H2831.notes: Transliteration: chashman; Pronunciation: khash-man'; Derivation: from an unused root (probably meaning firm or capacious in resources); apparently

H2832.meaning: Chasmonah, a place in the Desert
H2832.notes: Transliteration: Chashmônâh; Pronunciation: khash-mo-naw'; Derivation: feminine of H2831 (חַשְׁמַן); fertile;

H2833.meaning: perhaps a pocket (as holding the Urim and Thummim), or rich (as containing gems), used only of the gorget of the highpriest

H2838.meaning: attached, i.e. a fence-rail or rod connecting the posts or pillars

H2840.meaning: combined, i.e. the nave or hub of awheel (as holding the spokes together)

H2841.meaning: properly, a combination or gathering, i.e. of watery clouds
H2841.notes: Transliteration: chashrâh; Pronunciation: khash-raw'; Derivation: from the same as H2840 (חִשֻּׁר);

H2842.notes: Transliteration: châshash; Pronunciation: khaw-shash'; Derivation: by variation for H7179 (קַשׁ);

H2843.meaning: a Chushathite or descendant of Chushah
H2843.notes: Transliteration: Chushâthîy; Pronunciation: khoo-shaw-thee'; Derivation: patronymically from H2364 (חוּשָׁה);

H2844.meaning: concretely, crushed; also afraid; abstractly, terror
H2844.notes: Transliteration: chath; Pronunciation: khath; Derivation: from H2865 (חָתַת);

H2845.notes: Transliteration: Chêth; Pronunciation: khayth; Derivation: from H2865 (חָתַת); terror;

H2846.meaning: to lay hold of; especially to pick up fire
H2846.notes: Transliteration: châthâh; Pronunciation: khaw-thaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2847.notes: Transliteration: chittâh; Pronunciation: khit-taw'; Derivation: from H2865 (חָתַת);

H2848.notes: Transliteration: chittûwl; Pronunciation: khit-tool'; Derivation: from H2853 (חָתַל);

H2849.notes: Transliteration: chathchath; Pronunciation: khath-khath'; Derivation: from H2844 (חַת);

H2850.meaning: a Chittite, or descendant of Cheth
H2850.notes: Transliteration: Chittîy; Pronunciation: khit-tee'; Derivation: patronymically from H2845 (חֵת);

H2851.notes: Transliteration: chittîyth; Pronunciation: khit-teeth'; Derivation: from H2865 (חָתַת);

H2852.meaning: properly, to cut off, i.e. (figuratively) to decree
H2852.notes: Transliteration: châthak; Pronunciation: khaw-thak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2853.notes: Transliteration: châthal; Pronunciation: khaw-thal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2854.notes: Transliteration: chăthullâh; Pronunciation: khath-ool-law'; Derivation: from H2853 (חָתַל);

H2855.notes: Transliteration: Chethlôn; Pronunciation: kheth-lone'; Derivation: from H2853 (חָתַל); enswathed;

H2856.meaning: to close up; especially to seal
H2856.kjv: make an end, mark, seal (up), stop.
H2856.notes: Transliteration: châtham; Pronunciation: khaw-tham'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H2857.notes: Transliteration: chătham; Pronunciation: khath-am'; Derivation: (Aramaic) a root corresponding to H2856 (חָתַם);

H2858.notes: Transliteration: chôthemeth; Pronunciation: kho-the-meth; Derivation: feminine active participle of H2856 (חָתַם);

H2859.meaning: to give (a daughter) away in marriage; hence (generally) to contract affinity by marriage

H3004.notes: Transliteration: yabbâshâh; Pronunciation: yab-baw-shaw'; Derivation: from H3001 (יָבֵשׁ);

H3007.notes: Transliteration: yabbesheth; Pronunciation: yab-beh'-sheth; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3006 (יַבֶּשֶׁת);

H3013.kjv: afflict, cause grief, grieve, sorrowful, vex.

H3014.notes: Transliteration: yâgâh; Pronunciation: yaw-gaw'; Derivation: a primitive root (probably rather the same as H3013 (יָגָה) through the common idea of dissatisfaction);

H3015.notes: Transliteration: yâgôwn; Pronunciation: yaw-gohn'; Derivation: from H3013 (יָגָה);

H3016.notes: Transliteration: yâgôwr; Pronunciation: yaw-gore'; Derivation: from H3025 (יָגֹר);

H3017.notes: Transliteration: Yâgûwr; Pronunciation: yaw-goor'; Derivation: probably from H1481 (גּוּר); a lodging;

H3018.meaning: toil; hence, a work, produce, property (as the result of labor)

H3020.notes: Transliteration: Yoglîy; Pronunciation: yog-lee'; Derivation: from H1540 (גָּלָה); exiled;

H3021.meaning: properly, to gasp; hence, to be exhausted, to tire, to toil
H3021.notes: Transliteration: yâgaʻ; Pronunciation: yaw-gah'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3022.notes: Transliteration: yâgâʻ; Pronunciation: yaw-gaw'; Derivation: from H3021 (יָגַע);

H3023.notes: Transliteration: yâgêaʻ; Pronunciation: yaw-gay'-ah; Derivation: from H3021 (יָגַע);

H3024.notes: Transliteration: yᵉgiʻâh; Pronunciation: yeg-ee-aw'; Derivation: feminine of H3019 (יָגִיעַ);

H3025.notes: Transliteration: yâgôr; Pronunciation: yaw-gore'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3026.meaning: Jegar-Sahadutha, a cairn East of the Jordan
H3026.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉgar Sahădûwthâʼ; Pronunciation: yegar' sah-had-oo-thaw'

H3027.meaning: a hand (the open one (indicating power, means, direction, etc.),
H3027.kjv: ([phrase] be) able, [idiom] about, [phrase] armholes, at, axletree, because of, beside, border, [idiom] bounty, [phrase] broad, (broken-) handed, [idiom] by, charge, coast, [phrase] consecrate, [phrase] creditor, custody, debt, dominion, [idiom] enough, [phrase] fellowship, force, [idiom] from, hand(-staves, -y work), [idiom] he, himself, [idiom] in, labour, [phrase] large, ledge, (left-) handed, means, [idiom] mine, ministry, near, [idiom] of, [idiom] order, ordinance, [idiom] our, parts, pain, power, [idiom] presumptuously, service, side, sore, state, stay, draw with strength, stroke, [phrase] swear, terror, [idiom] thee, [idiom] by them, [idiom] themselves, [idiom] thine own, [idiom] thou, through, [idiom] throwing, [phrase] thumb, times, [idiom] to, [idiom] under, [idiom] us, [idiom] wait on, (way-) side, where, [phrase] wide, [idiom] with (him, me, you), work, [phrase] yield, [idiom] yourselves.

H3028.meaning: a hand (the open one (indicating power, means, direction, etc.)
H3028.notes: Transliteration: yad; Pronunciation: yad; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3027 (יָד)

H3029.notes: Transliteration: yᵉdâʼ; Pronunciation: yed-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3034 (יָדָה);

H3030.notes: Transliteration: Yidʼălâh; Pronunciation: yid-al-aw'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H3031.notes: Transliteration: Yidbâsh; Pronunciation: yid-bawsh'; Derivation: from the same as H1706 (דְּבַשׁ); perhaps honeyed;

H3039.notes: Transliteration: yᵉdîyd; Pronunciation: yed-eed'; Derivation: from the same as H1730 (דּוֹד);

H3040.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉdîydâh; Pronunciation: yed-ee-daw'; Derivation: feminine of H3039 (יְדִיד); beloved;

H3041.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉdîydᵉyâh; Pronunciation: yed-ee-deh-yaw'; Derivation: from H3039 (יְדִיד) and H3050 (יָהּ); beloved of Jah;

H3045.kjv: acknowledge, acquaintance(-ted with), advise, answer, appoint, assuredly, be aware, (un-) awares, can(-not), certainly, comprehend, consider, [idiom] could they, cunning, declare, be diligent, (can, cause to) discern, discover, endued with, familiar friend, famous, feel, can have, be (ig-) norant, instruct, kinsfolk, kinsman, (cause to let, make) know, (come to give, have, take) knowledge, have (knowledge), (be, make, make to be, make self) known, [phrase] be learned, [phrase] lie by man, mark, perceive, privy to, [idiom] prognosticator, regard, have respect, skilful, shew, can (man of) skill, be sure, of a surety, teach, (can) tell, understand, have (understanding), [idiom] will be, wist, wit, wot.

H3046.meaning: to know (properly, to ascertain by seeing); used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially (including observation, care, recognition; and causatively, instruction, designation, punishment, etc.)
H3046.notes: Transliteration: yᵉdaʻ; Pronunciation: yed-ah'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3045 (יָדַע)

H3047.notes: Transliteration: Yâdâʻ; Pronunciation: yaw-daw'; Derivation: from H3045 (יָדַע); knowing;

H3048.meaning: Jedajah, the name of two Israelites

H3049.notes: Transliteration: yiddᵉʻônîy; Pronunciation: yid-deh-o-nee'; Derivation: from H3045 (יָדַע);

H3051.meaning: to give (whether literal or figurative); generally, to put; imperatively (reflexive) come

H3052.kjv: deliver, give, lay, [phrase] prolong, pay, yield.

H3056.notes: Transliteration: Yehday; Pronunciation: yeh-dah'-ee; Derivation: perhaps from a form corresponding to H3061 (יְהוּד)lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to יְהוּד; Judaistic;

H3057.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhudîyâh; Pronunciation: yeh-hoo-dee-yaw'; Derivation: feminine of H3064 (יְהוּדִי);

H3062.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhûwdâʼîy; Pronunciation: yeh-hoo-daw-ee'; Derivation: (Aramaic) patrial from H3061 (יְהוּד)lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to יְהוּד;

H3064.meaning: a Jehudite (i.e. Judaite or Jew), or descendant of Jehudah (i.e. Judah)
H3064.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhûwdîy; Pronunciation: yeh-hoo-dee'; Derivation: patronymically from H3063 (יְהוּדָה);

H3065.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhûwdîy; Pronunciation: yeh-hoo-dee'; Derivation: the same as H3064 (יְהוּדִי);

H3066.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhûwdîyth; Pronunciation: yeh-hoo-deeth'; Derivation: feminine of H3064 (יְהוּדִי);

H3067.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhûwdîyth; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-deeth'; Derivation: the same as H3066 (יְהוּדִית); Jewess;

H3078.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwyâkîyn; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-yaw-keen'; Derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H3559 (כּוּן); Jehovah will establish;

H3079.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwyâqîym; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-yaw-keem'; Derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) abbreviated and H6965 (קוּם); Jehovah will raise;

H3080.meaning: Jehojarib, the name of two Israelites

H3081.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhûwkal; Pronunciation: yeh-hoo-kal'; Derivation: from H3201 (יָכֹל); potent;

H3082.meaning: Jehonadab, the name of an Israelite and of an Arab

H3083.meaning: Jehonathan, the name of four Israelites

H3085.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwʻaddâh; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-ad-daw'; Derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5710 (עָדָה); Jehovah-adorned;

H3086.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwʻaddîyn; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-ad-deen'; Derivation: or יְהוֹעַדָּן; from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5727 (עָדַן); Jehovahpleased;

H3087.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwtsâdâq; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-tsaw-dawk'; Derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H6663 (צָדַק); Jehovah-righted;

H3088.meaning: Jehoram, the name of a Syrian and of three Israelites

H3089.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwshebaʻ; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-sheh'-bah; Derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H7650 (שָׁבַע); Jehovah-sworn;

H3090.notes: Transliteration: Yᵉhôwshabʻath; Pronunciation: yeh-ho-shab-ath'; Derivation: a form of H3089 (יְהוֹשֶׁבַע);

H3091.kjv: Jehoshua, Jehoshuah, Joshua. Compare H1954 (הוֹשֵׁעַ), H3442 (יֵשׁוּעַ).

H3547.kjv: deck, be (do the office of a, execute the, minister in the) priest('s office).

H3548.meaning: literally one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)
H3548.kjv: chief ruler, [idiom] own, priest, prince, principal officer.
H3548.notes: Transliteration: kôhên; Pronunciation: ko-hane'; Derivation: active participle of H3547 (כָּהַן);

H3549.meaning: one officiating, a priest; also (by courtesy) an acting priest (although a layman)
H3549.notes: Transliteration: kâhên; Pronunciation: kaw-hane'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3548 (כֹּהֵן)

H3550.notes: Transliteration: kᵉhunnâh; Pronunciation: keh-hoon-naw'; Derivation: from H3547 (כָּהַן);

H3551.notes: Transliteration: kav; Pronunciation: kav; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H3854 (לַהַג) in the sense of piercing;

H3552.notes: Transliteration: Kûwb; Pronunciation: koob; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H3553.notes: Transliteration: kôwbaʻ; Pronunciation: ko'-bah; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to be high or rounded;

H3554.meaning: properly, to prick or penetrate; hence, to blister (as smarting or eating into)
H3554.notes: Transliteration: kâvâh; Pronunciation: kaw-vaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3555.notes: Transliteration: kᵉvîyâh; Pronunciation: kev-ee-yaw'; Derivation: from H3554 (כָּוָה);

H3556.meaning: a star (as round or as shining); figuratively, a prince
H3556.notes: Transliteration: kôwkâb; Pronunciation: ko-kawb'; Derivation: probably from the same as H3522 (כַּבּוֹן) (in the sense of rolling) or H3554 (כָּוָה) (in the sense of blazing);

H3557.meaning: properly, to keep in; hence, to measure; figuratively, to maintain (in various senses)
H3557.kjv: (be able to, can) abide, bear, comprehend, contain, feed, forbearing, guide, hold(-ing in), nourish(-er), be present, make provision, receive, sustain, provide sustenance (victuals).
H3557.notes: Transliteration: kûwl; Pronunciation: kool; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3558.notes: Transliteration: kûwmâz; Pronunciation: koo-mawz'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to store away;

H3559.meaning: properly, to be erect (i.e. stand perpendicular); hence (causatively) to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal (establish, fix, prepare, apply), or figurative (appoint, render sure, proper or prosperous)
H3559.kjv: certain(-ty), confirm, direct, faithfulness, fashion, fasten, firm, be fitted, be fixed, frame, be meet, ordain, order, perfect, (make) preparation, prepare (self), provide, make provision, (be, make) ready, right, set (aright, fast, forth), be stable, (e-) stablish, stand, tarry, [idiom] very deed.
H3559.notes: Transliteration: kûwn; Pronunciation: koon; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3560.notes: Transliteration: Kûwn; Pronunciation: koon; Derivation: probably from H3559 (כּוּן); established;

H3561.meaning: something prepared, i.e. a sacrificial wafer
H3561.notes: Transliteration: kavvân; Pronunciation: kav-vawn'; Derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן);

H3562.meaning: Conanjah, the name of two Israelites
H3562.notes: Transliteration: Kôwnanyâhûw; Pronunciation: ko-nan-yaw'-hoo; Derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has sustained;

H3563.meaning: a cup (as a container), often figuratively, a lot (as if a potion); also some unclean bird, probably an owl (perhaps from the cup-like cavity of its eye)
H3563.notes: Transliteration: kôwç; Pronunciation: koce; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to hold together;

H3564.meaning: a pot or furnace (as if excavated)
H3564.notes: Transliteration: kûwr; Pronunciation: koor; Derivation: from an unused root meaning properly, to dig through;

H3565.notes: Transliteration: Kôwr ʻÂshân; Pronunciation: kore aw-shawn'; Derivation: from H3564 (כּוּר) and H6227 (עָשָׁן); furnace of smoke;

H3566.notes: Transliteration: Kôwresh; Pronunciation: ko'-resh; Derivation: or (Ezra 1:1 (last time),2) כֹּרֶשׁ; from the Persians;

H3567.notes: Transliteration: Kôwresh; Pronunciation: ko'-resh; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3566 (כּוֹרֶשׁ)

H3568.meaning: Cush (or Ethiopia), the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite
H3568.notes: Transliteration: Kûwsh; Pronunciation: koosh; Derivation: probably of foreign origin;

H3569.meaning: a Cushite, or descendant of Cush
H3569.notes: Transliteration: Kûwshîy; Pronunciation: koo-shee'; Derivation: patronymically from H3568 (כּוּשׁ);

H3570.meaning: Cushi, the name of two Israelites
H3570.notes: Transliteration: Kûwshîy; Pronunciation: koo-shee'; Derivation: the same as H3569 (כּוּשִׁי);

H3571.notes: Transliteration: Kûwshîyth; Pronunciation: koo-sheeth'; Derivation: feminine of H3569 (כּוּשִׁי);

H3572.notes: Transliteration: Kûwshân; Pronunciation: koo-shawn'; Derivation: perhaps from H3568 (כּוּשׁ);

H3573.notes: Transliteration: Kûwshan Rishʻâthayim; Pronunciation: koo-shan' rish-awthah'-yim; Derivation: apparently from H3572 (כּוּשָׁן) and the dual of H7564 (רִשְׁעָה); Cushan of double wickedness;

H3574.notes: Transliteration: kôwshârâh; Pronunciation: ko-shaw-raw'; Derivation: from H3787 (כָּשֵׁר);

H3575.meaning: Cuth or Cuthah, a province of Assyria
H3575.notes: Transliteration: Kûwth; Pronunciation: kooth; Derivation: or (feminine) כּוּתָה; of foreign origin;

H3576.meaning: to lie (i.e. deceive), literally or figuratively
H3576.kjv: fail, (be found a, make a) liar, lie, lying, be in vain.
H3576.notes: Transliteration: kâzab; Pronunciation: kaw-zab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3577.kjv: deceitful, false, leasing, + liar, lie, lying.
H3577.notes: Transliteration: kâzâb; Pronunciation: kaw-zawb'; Derivation: from H3576 (כָּזַב);

H3578.notes: Transliteration: Kôzᵉbâʼ; Pronunciation: ko-zeb-aw'; Derivation: from H3576 (כָּזַב); fallacious;

H3579.notes: Transliteration: Kôzᵉbîy; Pronunciation: koz-bee'; Derivation: from H3576 (כָּזַב); false;

H3580.notes: Transliteration: Kᵉzîyb; Pronunciation: kez-eeb'; Derivation: from H3576 (כָּזַב); falsified;

H3581.meaning: vigor, literally (force, in a good or a bad sense) or figuratively (capacity, means, produce); also (from its hardiness) a large lizard
H3581.kjv: ability, able, chameleon, force, fruits, might, power(-ful), strength, substance, wealth.
H3581.notes: Transliteration: kôach; Pronunciation: ko'-akh; Derivation: or (Daniel 11:6) כּוֹחַ; from an unused root meaning to be firm;

H3582.meaning: to secrete, by act or word; hence (intensively) to destroy
H3582.notes: Transliteration: kâchad; Pronunciation: kaw-khad'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3583.notes: Transliteration: kâchal; Pronunciation: kaw-khal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3584.meaning: to be untrue, in word (to lie, feign, disown) or deed (to disappoint, fail, cringe)
H3584.kjv: deceive, deny, dissemble, fail, deal falsely, be found liars, (be-) lie, lying, submit selves.
H3584.notes: Transliteration: kâchash; Pronunciation: kaw-khash'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3585.meaning: literally a failure of flesh, i.e. emaciation; figuratively, hypocrisy
H3585.notes: Transliteration: kachash; Pronunciation: kakh'-ash; Derivation: from H3584 (כָּחַשׁ);

H3586.notes: Transliteration: kechâsh; Pronunciation: kekh-awsh'; Derivation: from H3584 (כָּחַשׁ);

H3587.notes: Transliteration: kîy; Pronunciation: kee; Derivation: from H3554 (כָּוָה);

H3588.meaning: (by implication) very widely used as a relative conjunction or adverb (as below); often largely modified by other particles annexed
H3588.kjv: and, + (forasmuch, inasmuch, where-) as, assured(-ly), + but, certainly, doubtless, + else, even, + except, for, how, (because, in, so, than) that, + nevertheless, now, rightly, seeing, since, surely, then, therefore, + (al-) though, + till, truly, + until, when, whether, while, whom, yea, yet.

H3589.notes: Transliteration: kîyd; Pronunciation: keed; Derivation: from a primitive root meaning to strike;

H3590.meaning: properly, something struck off, i.e. a spark (as struck)
H3590.notes: Transliteration: kîydôwd; Pronunciation: kee-dode'; Derivation: from the same as H3589 (כִּיד) (compare H3539 (כַּדְכֹּד));

H3591.meaning: properly, something to strike with, i.e. a dart
H3591.notes: Transliteration: kîydôwn; Pronunciation: kee-dohn'; Derivation: from the same as H3589 (כִּיד); (perhaps smaller than H2595 (חֲנִית)lemma חֲניִת second vowel, corrected to חֲנִית)

H3592.notes: Transliteration: Kîydôwn; Pronunciation: kee-dohn'; Derivation: the same as H3591 (כִּידוֹן);

H3593.notes: Transliteration: kîydôwr; Pronunciation: kee-dore'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H3594.meaning: properly, a statue, i.e. idol; but used (by euphemism) forsome heathen deity (perhaps corresponding to Priapus or Baal-peor)
H3594.notes: Transliteration: Kîyûwn; Pronunciation: kee-yoon'; Derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן);

H3595.meaning: properly, something round (as excavated or bored), i.e. a chafing-dish forcoals or a caldron forcooking; hence (from similarity of form) a washbowl; also (for the same reason) a pulpit or platform
H3595.notes: Transliteration: kîyôwr; Pronunciation: kee-yore'; Derivation: or כִּיֹּר; from the same as H3564 (כּוּר);

H3596.notes: Transliteration: kîylay; Pronunciation: kee-lah'-ee; Derivation: or כֵּלַי; from H3557 (כּוּל) in the sense of withholding;

H3597.notes: Transliteration: kêylaph; Pronunciation: kay-laf'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to clap or strike with noise;

H3598.meaning: a cluster of stars, i.e. the Pleiades
H3598.notes: Transliteration: Kîymâh; Pronunciation: kee-maw'; Derivation: from the same as H3558 (כּוּמָז);

H3599.meaning: a cup; also a bag formoney or weights
H3599.notes: Transliteration: kîyç; Pronunciation: keece; Derivation: a form for H3563 (כּוֹס);

H3600.meaning: a cooking range (consisting of two parallel stones, across which the boiler is set)
H3600.notes: Transliteration: kîyr; Pronunciation: keer; Derivation: a form for H3564 (כּוּר) (only in the dual);

H3601.meaning: literally a director, i.e. the spindle or shank of adistaff, by which it is twirled
H3601.notes: Transliteration: kîyshôwr; Pronunciation: kee-shore'; Derivation: from H3787 (כָּשֵׁר); compare H6418 (פֶּלֶךְ)

H3602.meaning: just so, referring to the previous or following context
H3602.kjv: after that (this) manner, this matter, (even) so, in such a case, thus.
H3602.notes: Transliteration: kâkâh; Pronunciation: kaw'-kaw; Derivation: from H3541 (כֹּה);

H3603.meaning: a circle, i.e. (by implication) a circumjacent tract or region, especially the Ghor or valley of the Jordan; also a (round) loaf; also a talent (or large [round] coin)
H3603.notes: Transliteration: kikkâr; Pronunciation: kik-kawr'; Derivation: from H3769 (כָּרַר);

H3604.notes: Transliteration: kikkêr; Pronunciation: kik-kare'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3603 (כִּכָּר);

H3605.meaning: properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
H3605.kjv: (in) all (manner, (ye)), altogether, any (manner), enough, every (one, place, thing), howsoever, as many as, (no-) thing, ought, whatsoever, (the) whole, whoso(-ever).
H3605.notes: Transliteration: kôl; Pronunciation: kole; Derivation: or (Jeremiah 33:8) כּוֹל; from H3634 (כָּלַל);

H3606.meaning: properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
H3606.kjv: all, any, + (forasmuch) as, + be-(for this) cause, every, + no (manner, -ne), + there (where) -fore, + though, what (where, who) -soever, (the) whole.
H3606.notes: Transliteration: kôl; Pronunciation: kole; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3605 (כֹּל)

H3607.meaning: to restrict, by act (hold back or in) or word (prohibit)
H3607.kjv: finish, forbid, keep (back), refrain, restrain, retain, shut up, be stayed, withhold.
H3607.notes: Transliteration: kâlâʼ; Pronunciation: kaw-law'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3608.notes: Transliteration: keleʼ; Pronunciation: keh'-leh; Derivation: from H3607 (כָּלָא);

H3609.notes: Transliteration: Kilʼâb; Pronunciation: kil-awb'; Derivation: apparently from H3607 (כָּלָא) and H1 (אָב); restraint of (his) father;

H3610.notes: Transliteration: kilʼayim; Pronunciation: kil-ah'-yim; Derivation: dual of H3608 (כֶּלֶא) in the original sense of separation;

H3611.meaning: a dog; hence (by euphemism) a male prostitute
H3611.notes: Transliteration: keleb; Pronunciation: keh'-leb; Derivation: from an unused root means. to yelp, or else to attack;

H3612.meaning: Caleb, the name of three Israelites
H3612.notes: Transliteration: Kâlêb; Pronunciation: kaw-labe'; Derivation: perhaps a form of H3611 (כֶּלֶב), or else from the same root in the sense of forcible;

H3613.meaning: Caleb-Ephrathah, a place in Egypt (if the text is correct)
H3613.notes: Transliteration: Kâlêb ʼEphrâthâh; Pronunciation: kaw-labe' ef-raw'-thaw; Derivation: from H3612 (כָּלֵב) and H672 (אֶפְרָת);

H3614.meaning: a Calebite or descendant of Caleb
H3614.notes: Transliteration: Kâlibbôw; Pronunciation: kaw-lib-bo'; Derivation: probably by erroneous transcription for כָּלֵבִי; patronymically from H3612 (כָּלֵב);

H3615.meaning: to end, whether intransitive (to cease, be finished, perish) or transitived (to complete, prepare, consume)
H3615.kjv: accomplish, cease, consume (away), determine, destroy (utterly), be (when... were) done, (be an) end (of), expire, (cause to) fail, faint, finish, fulfil, [idiom] fully, [idiom] have, leave (off), long, bring to pass, wholly reap, make clean riddance, spend, quite take away, waste.
H3615.notes: Transliteration: kâlâh; Pronunciation: kaw-law'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3616.notes: Transliteration: kâleh; Pronunciation: kaw-leh'; Derivation: from H3615 (כָּלָה);

H3617.meaning: a completion; adverb, completely; also destruction
H3617.kjv: altogether, (be, utterly) consume(-d), consummation(-ption), was determined, (full, utter) end, riddance.
H3617.notes: Transliteration: kâlâh; Pronunciation: kaw-law'; Derivation: from H3615 (כָּלָה);

H3618.meaning: a bride (as if perfect); hence, a son's wife
H3618.notes: Transliteration: kallâh; Pronunciation: kal-law'; Derivation: from H3634 (כָּלַל);

H3619.meaning: a bird-trap (as furnished with a clap-stick or treadle to spring it); hence, a basket (as resembling a wicker cage)
H3619.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlûwb; Pronunciation: kel-oob'; Derivation: from the same as H3611 (כֶּלֶב);

H3620.meaning: Kelub, the name of two Israelites
H3620.notes: Transliteration: Kᵉlûwb; Pronunciation: kel-oob'; Derivation: the same as H3619 (כְּלוּב)xlit kᵉlûb corrected to kᵉlûwb;

H3621.notes: Transliteration: Kᵉlûwbay; Pronunciation: kel-oo-bay'-ee; Derivation: a form of H3612 (כָּלֵב);

H3622.notes: Transliteration: Kᵉlûwhay; Pronunciation: kel-oo-hah'-ee; Derivation: from H3615 (כָּלָה); completed;

H3623.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlûwlâh; Pronunciation: kel-oo-law'; Derivation: denominative passive participle from H3618 (כַּלָּה)lemma כַּלָה missing dagesh, corrected to כַּלָּה;

H3624.notes: Transliteration: kelach; Pronunciation: keh'-lakh; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to be complete;

H3625.notes: Transliteration: Kelach; Pronunciation: keh'-lakh; Derivation: the same as H3624 (כֶּלַח);

H3626.notes: Transliteration: Kol-Chôzeh; Pronunciation: kol-kho-zeh'; Derivation: from H3605 (כֹּל) and H2374 (חֹזֶה); every seer;

H3627.meaning: something prepared, i.e. any apparatus (as an implement, utensil, dress, vessel or weapon)
H3627.kjv: armour(-bearer), artillery, bag, carriage, [phrase] furnish, furniture, instrument, jewel, that is made of, [idiom] one from another, that which pertaineth, pot, [phrase] psaltery, sack, stuff, thing, tool, vessel, ware, weapon, [phrase] whatsoever.
H3627.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlîy; Pronunciation: kel-ee'; Derivation: from H3615 (כָּלָה);

H3628.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlîyʼ; Pronunciation: kel-ee'; Derivation: or כְּלוּא; from H3627 (כְּלִי) (compare H3608 (כֶּלֶא));

H3629.meaning: a kidney (as an essential organ); figuratively, the mind (as the interior self)
H3629.notes: Transliteration: kilyâh; Pronunciation: kil-yaw'; Derivation: feminine of H3627 (כְּלִי) (only in the plural);

H3630.notes: Transliteration: Kilyôwn; Pronunciation: kil-yone'; Derivation: a form of H3631 (כִּלָּיוֹן);

H3631.notes: Transliteration: killâyôwn; Pronunciation: kil-law-yone'; Derivation: from H3615 (כָּלָה);

H3632.meaning: complete; as noun, the whole (specifically, a sacrifice entirely consumed); as adverb, fully
H3632.kjv: all, every whit, flame, perfect(-ion), utterly, whole burnt offering (sacrifice), wholly.
H3632.notes: Transliteration: kâlîyl; Pronunciation: kaw-leel'; Derivation: from H3634 (כָּלַל);

H3633.notes: Transliteration: Kalkôl; Pronunciation: kal-kole'; Derivation: from H3557 (כּוּל); sustenance;

H3634.notes: Transliteration: kâlal; Pronunciation: kaw-lal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3635.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlal; Pronunciation: kel-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3634 (כָּלַל);

H3636.notes: Transliteration: Kᵉlâl; Pronunciation: kel-awl'; Derivation: from H3634 (כָּלַל); complete;

H3637.meaning: properly, to wound; but only figuratively, to taunt or insult
H3637.kjv: be (make) ashamed, blush, be confounded, be put to confusion, hurt, reproach, (do, put to) shame.
H3637.notes: Transliteration: kâlam; Pronunciation: kaw-lawm'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3638.meaning: Kilmad, a place apparently in the Assyrian empire
H3638.notes: Transliteration: Kilmâd; Pronunciation: kil-mawd'; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H3639.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlimmâh; Pronunciation: kel-im-maw'; Derivation: from H3637 (כָּלַם);

H3640.notes: Transliteration: kᵉlimmûwth; Pronunciation: kel-im-mooth'; Derivation: from H3639 (כְּלִמָּה);

H3641.meaning: Calneh or Calno, a place in the Assyrian empire
H3641.notes: Transliteration: Kalneh; Pronunciation: kal-neh'; Derivation: or כַּלְנֵה; also כַּלְנוֹ; of foreign derivation;

H3642.notes: Transliteration: kâmahh; Pronunciation: kaw-mah; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3643.notes: Transliteration: Kimhâm; Pronunciation: kim-hawm'; Derivation: from H3642 (כָּמַהּ); pining;

H3644.meaning: a form of the prefix 'k-', but used separately  as, thus, so
H3644.kjv: according to, (such) as (it were, well as), in comparison of, like (as, to, unto), thus, when, worth.
H3644.notes: Transliteration: kᵉmôw; Pronunciation: kem-o'; Derivation: or כָּמוֹ; (compare H3651 (כֵּן));

H3645.meaning: Kemosh, the god of the Moabites
H3645.notes: Transliteration: Kᵉmôwsh; Pronunciation: kem-oshe'; Derivation: or (Jeremiah 48:7) כְּמִישׁ; from an unused root meaning to subdue; the powerful;

H3646.notes: Transliteration: kammôn; Pronunciation: kam-mone'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to store up or preserve;

H3647.meaning: to store away, i.e. (figuratively) in the memory
H3647.notes: Transliteration: kâmaç; Pronunciation: kaw-mas'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3648.meaning: properly, to intertwine or contract, i.e. (by implication) to shrivel (as with heat); figuratively, to be deeply affected with passion (love or pity)
H3648.notes: Transliteration: kâmar; Pronunciation: kaw-mar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3649.meaning: properly, an ascetic (as if shrunk with self-maceration), i.e. an idolatrous priest (only in plural)
H3649.notes: Transliteration: kâmâr; Pronunciation: kaw-mawr'; Derivation: from H3648 (כָּמַר);

H3650.meaning: obscuration (as if from shrinkage of light, i.e. an eclipse (only in plural)
H3650.notes: Transliteration: kimrîyr; Pronunciation: kim-reer'; Derivation: redupl. from H3648 (כָּמַר);

H3651.meaning: properly, set upright; hence (figuratively as adjective) just; but usually (as adverb or conjunction) rightly or so (in various applications to manner, time and relation; often with other particles)
H3651.kjv: [phrase] after that (this, -ward, -wards), as... as, [phrase] (for-) asmuch as yet, [phrase] be (for which) cause, [phrase] following, howbeit, in (the) like (manner, -wise), [idiom] the more, right, (even) so, state, straightway, such (thing), surely, [phrase] there (where) -fore, this, thus, true, well, [idiom] you.
H3651.notes: Transliteration: kên; Pronunciation: kane; Derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן);

H3652.notes: Transliteration: kên; Pronunciation: kane; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3651 (כֵּן);

H3653.meaning: a stand, i.e. pedestal or station
H3653.kjv: base, estate, foot, office, place, well.
H3653.notes: Transliteration: kên; Pronunciation: kane; Derivation: the same as H3651 (כֵּן), used as a noun;

H3655.meaning: to address by an additional name; hence, to eulogize
H3655.notes: Transliteration: kânâh; Pronunciation: kaw-naw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H3656.notes: Transliteration: Kanneh; Pronunciation: kan-neh'; Derivation: for H3641 (כַּלְנֶה);

H3657.notes: Transliteration: kannâh; Pronunciation: kaw-naw'; Derivation: from H3661 (כָּנַן);

H3658.notes: Transliteration: kinnôwr; Pronunciation: kin-nore'; Derivation: from a unused root meaning to twang;

H3659.notes: Transliteration: Konyâhûw; Pronunciation: kon-yaw'-hoo; Derivation: for H3204 (יְכׇנְיָה);

H3674.meaning: a colleague (as having the same title)
H3674.notes: Transliteration: kᵉnâth; Pronunciation: ken-awth'; Derivation: from H3655 (כָּנָה);

H3675.notes: Transliteration: kᵉnâth; Pronunciation: ken-awth'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3674 (כְּנָת)

H3677.notes: Transliteration: keçeʼ; Pronunciation: keh'-seh; Derivation: or כֶּסֶה; apparently from H3680 (כָּסָה);

H3678.meaning: properly, covered, i.e. a throne (as canopied)
H3678.notes: Transliteration: kiççêʼ; Pronunciation: kis-say'; Derivation: or כִּסֵּה; from H3680 (כָּסָה);

H3679.notes: Transliteration: Kaçday; Pronunciation: kas-dah'-ee; Derivation: for H3778 (כַּשְׂדִּי)

H3685.meaning: any notable constellation; specifically Orion (as if a burly one)

H3691.notes: Transliteration: Kiçlêv; Pronunciation: kis-lave'; Derivation: probably of foreign origin;

H3692.notes: Transliteration: Kiçlôwn; Pronunciation: kis-lone'; Derivation: from H3688 (כָּסַל); hopeful;

H3698.meaning: spelt (from its bristliness as if just shorn)

H3778.meaning: a Kasdite, or descendant of Kesed; by implication, a Chaldaean (as if so descended); also an astrologer (as if proverbial of that people

H4039.notes: Transliteration: mᵉgillâh; Pronunciation: meg-il-law'; Derivation: from H1556 (גָּלַל);

H4048.meaning: to yield up; intensively, to precipitate

H4069.notes: Transliteration: maddûwaʻ; Pronunciation: mad-doo'-ah; Derivation: or מַדֻּעַ; from H4100 (מָה) and the passive participle of H3045 (יָדַע);

H4077.notes: Transliteration: Mâday; Pronunciation: maw-dah'-ee; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H4075 (מָדַי)

H4149.meaning: Moserah or Moseroth, a place in the Desert
H4149.notes: Transliteration: Môwçêrâh; Pronunciation: mo-say-raw'; Derivation: or (plural) מֹסְרוֹת feminine of H4147 (מוֹסֵר); correction or corrections;

H4150.meaning: properly, an appointment, i.e. a fixed time or season; specifically, a festival; conventionally ayear; by implication, an assembly (as convened for a definite purpose); technically the congregation; by extension, the place of meeting; also a signal (as appointed beforehand)
H4150.kjv: appointed (sign, time), (place of, solemn) assembly, congregation, (set, solemn) feast, (appointed, due) season, solemn(-ity), synogogue, (set) time (appointed).
H4150.notes: Transliteration: môwʻêd; Pronunciation: mo-ade'; Derivation: or מֹעֵד; or (feminine) מוֹעָדָה; (2 Chronicles 8:13), from H3259 (יָעַד);

H4151.meaning: properly, an assembly  figuratively, a troop
H4151.notes: Transliteration: môwʻâd; Pronunciation: mo-awd'; Derivation: from H3259 (יָעַד); (as in H4150 (מוֹעֵד));

H4152.notes: Transliteration: mûwʻâdâh; Pronunciation: moo-aw-daw'; Derivation: from H3259 (יָעַד);

H4153.notes: Transliteration: Môwʻadyâh; Pronunciation: mo-ad-yaw'; Derivation: from H4151 (מוֹעָד) and H3050 (יָהּ); assembly of Jah;

H4154.meaning: properly, made to slip, i.e. dislocated
H4154.notes: Transliteration: mûwʻedeth; Pronunciation: moo-ay'-deth; Derivation: feminine passive participle of H4571 (מָעַד);

H4155.meaning: properly, covered, i.e. dark; abstractly, obscurity, i.e. distress
H4155.notes: Transliteration: mûwʻâph; Pronunciation: moo-awf'; Derivation: from H5774 (עוּף);

H4156.notes: Transliteration: môwʻêtsâh; Pronunciation: mo-ay-tsaw'; Derivation: from H3289 (יָעַץ);

H4157.notes: Transliteration: mûwʻâqâh; Pronunciation: moo-aw-kaw'; Derivation: from H5781 (עוּק);

H4158.meaning: Mophaath or Mephaath, a place in Palestine
H4158.notes: Transliteration: Môwphaʻath; Pronunciation: mo-fah'-ath; Derivation: (Jeremiah 48:21) or מֵיפַעַת; or מֵפַעַת; from H3313 (יָפַע); illuminative;

H4159.meaning: a miracle; by implication, a token or omen
H4159.notes: Transliteration: môwphêth; Pronunciation: mo-faith'; Derivation: or מֹפֵת; from H3302 (יָפָה) in the sense of conspicuousness;

H4160.notes: Transliteration: mûwts; Pronunciation: moots; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4161.meaning: a going forth, i.e. (the act) an egress, or (the place) an exit; hence, a source or product; specifically, dawn, the rising of the sun (the East), exportation, utterance, a gate, a fountain, a mine, a meadow (as producing grass)
H4161.kjv: brought out, bud, that which came out, east, going forth, goings out, that which (thing that) is gone out, outgoing, proceeded out, spring, vein, (water-) course (springs).
H4161.notes: Transliteration: môwtsâʼ; Pronunciation: mo-tsaw'; Derivation: or מֹצָאxlit môtsâ corrected to môtsâʼ; from H3318 (יָצָא);

H4162.meaning: Motsa, the name of two Israelites
H4162.notes: Transliteration: môwtsâʼ; Pronunciation: mo-tsaw'; Derivation: the same as H4161 (מוֹצָא);

H4163.meaning: a family descent; also a sewer
H4163.notes: Transliteration: môwtsâʼâh; Pronunciation: mo-tsaw-aw'; Derivation: feminine of H4161 (מוֹצָא); (marg.; compare H6675 (צוֹאָה))

H4164.notes: Transliteration: mûwtsaq; Pronunciation: moo-tsak'; Derivation: or מוּצָק; from H3332 (יָצַק);

H4165.meaning: properly, fusion, i.e. literally, a casting (of metal); figuratively, a mass (of clay)
H4165.notes: Transliteration: mûwtsâq; Pronunciation: moo-tsawk'; Derivation: from H5694 (עָגִיל);

H4166.meaning: properly, something poured out, i.e. a casting (of metal); by implication, a tube (as cast)
H4166.notes: Transliteration: mûwtsâqâh; Pronunciation: moo-tsaw-kaw'; Derivation: or מֻצָקָה; from H3332 (יָצַק);

H4167.notes: Transliteration: mûwq; Pronunciation: mook; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4168.meaning: a fire or fuel; abstractly, a conflagration
H4168.notes: Transliteration: môwqêd; Pronunciation: mo-kade'; Derivation: from H3344 (יָקַד);

H4169.notes: Transliteration: môwqᵉdâh; Pronunciation: mo-ked-aw'; Derivation: feminine of H4168 (מוֹקֵד);

H4170.meaning: a noose (for catching animals) (literally or figuratively); by implication, a hook (for the nose)
H4170.notes: Transliteration: môwqêsh; Pronunciation: mo-kashe'; Derivation: or מֹקֵשׁ; from H3369 (יָקֹשׁ);

H4171.meaning: to alter; by implication, to barter, to dispose of
H4171.notes: Transliteration: mûwr; Pronunciation: moor; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4172.meaning: fear; by implication, a fearful thing or deed
H4172.kjv: dread, (that ought to be) fear(-ed), terribleness, terror.
H4172.notes: Transliteration: môwrâʼ; Pronunciation: mo-raw'; Derivation: or מֹרָא; or מוֹרָהxlit môrâh corrected to môwrâh; (Psalm 9:20), from H3372 (יָרֵא);

H4173.notes: Transliteration: môwrag; Pronunciation: mo-rag'; Derivation: or מֹרַג; from an unused root meaning to triturate;

H4174.meaning: a descent; as architecture, an ornamental appendage, perhaps a festoon
H4174.kjv: going down, steep place, thin work.
H4174.notes: Transliteration: môwrâd; Pronunciation: mo-rawd'; Derivation: from H3381 (יָרַד);

H4175.meaning: an archer; also teacher or teaching; also the early rain
H4175.notes: Transliteration: môwreh; Pronunciation: mo-reh'; Derivation: from H3384 (יָרָה); (see H3138 (יוֹרֶה))

H4176.meaning: Moreh, a Canaanite; also a hill (perhaps named from him)
H4176.notes: Transliteration: Môwreh; Pronunciation: mo-reh'; Derivation: or מֹרֶה; the same as H4175 (מוֹרֶה);

H4177.notes: Transliteration: môwrâh; Pronunciation: mo-raw'; Derivation: from H4171 (מוּר) in the sense of shearing;

H4178.notes: Transliteration: môwrâṭ; Pronunciation: mo-rawt'; Derivation: from H3399 (יָרַט);

H4179.notes: Transliteration: Môwrîyâh; Pronunciation: mo-ree-yaw'; Derivation: or מֹרִיָּה; from H7200 (רָאָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); seen of Jah;

H4180.notes: Transliteration: môwrâsh; Pronunciation: mo-rawsh'; Derivation: from H3423 (יָרַשׁ);

H4181.notes: Transliteration: môwrâshâh; Pronunciation: mo-raw-shaw'; Derivation: feminine of H4180 (מוֹרָשׁ);

H4182.notes: Transliteration: Môwresheth Gath; Pronunciation: mo-reh'-sheth gath; Derivation: from H3423 (יָרַשׁ) and H1661 (גַּת); possession of Gath;

H4183.meaning: a Morashtite or inhabitant of Moresheth-Gath
H4183.notes: Transliteration: Môwrashtîy; Pronunciation: mo-rash-tee'; Derivation: patrial from H4182 (מוֹרֶשֶׁת גַּת);

H4184.notes: Transliteration: mûwsh; Pronunciation: moosh; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4185.meaning: to withdraw (both literally and figuratively, whether intransitive or transitive)

H4185.notes: Transliteration: mûwsh; Pronunciation: moosh; Derivation: a primitive root (perhaps rather the same as H4184 (מוּשׁ) through the idea of receding by contact);

H4186.meaning: a seat; figuratively, a site; abstractly, a session; by extension an abode (the place or the time); by implication, population
H4186.kjv: assembly, dwell in, dwelling(-place), wherein (that) dwelt (in), inhabited place, seat, sitting, situation, sojourning.
H4186.notes: Transliteration: môwshâb; Pronunciation: mo-shawb'; Derivation: or מֹשָׁב; from H3427 (יָשַׁב);

H4187.notes: Transliteration: Mûwshîy; Pronunciation: moo-shee'; Derivation: or מֻשִּׁי; from H4184 (מוּשׁ); sensitive;

H4188.meaning: a Mushite (collectively) or descendants of Mushi
H4188.notes: Transliteration: Mûwshîy; Pronunciation: moo-shee'; Derivation: patronymical from H4187 (מוּשִׁי);

H4189.notes: Transliteration: môwshᵉkâh; Pronunciation: mo-shek-aw'; Derivation: act participle feminine of H4900 (מָשַׁךְ);

H4190.notes: Transliteration: môwshâʻâh; Pronunciation: mo-shaw-aw'; Derivation: from H3467 (יָשַׁע);

H4191.meaning: to die (literally or figuratively); causatively, to kill
H4191.kjv: [idiom] at all, [idiom] crying, (be) dead (body, man, one), (put to, worthy of) death, destroy(-er), (cause to, be like to, must) die, kill, necro(-mancer), [idiom] must needs, slay, [idiom] surely, [idiom] very suddenly, [idiom] in (no) wise.
H4191.notes: Transliteration: mûwth; Pronunciation: mooth; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4192.meaning: 'To die for the son', probably the title of a popular song
H4192.notes: Transliteration: Mûwth; Pronunciation: mooth; Derivation: (Psalm H48 (אֲבִירָם) or מוּת לַבֵּן; from H4191 (מוּת) and H1121 (בֵּן) with the preposition and article interposed;

H4193.notes: Transliteration: môwth; Pronunciation: mohth; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H4194 (מָוֶת);

H4194.meaning: death (natural or violent); concretely, the dead, their place or state (hades); figuratively, pestilence, ruin
H4194.notes: Transliteration: mâveth; Pronunciation: maw'-veth; Derivation: from H4191 (מוּת);

H4195.notes: Transliteration: môwthâr; Pronunciation: mo-thar'; Derivation: from H3498 (יָתַר);

H4196.notes: Transliteration: mizbêach; Pronunciation: miz-bay'-akh; Derivation: from H2076 (זָבַח);

H4197.notes: Transliteration: mezeg; Pronunciation: meh'-zeg; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to mingle (water with wine);

H4198.notes: Transliteration: mâzeh; Pronunciation: maw-zeh'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to suck out;

H4199.notes: Transliteration: Mizzâh; Pronunciation: miz-zaw'; Derivation: probably from an unused root meaning to faint with fear; terror;

H4200.notes: Transliteration: mezev; Pronunciation: meh'-zev; Derivation: probably from an unused root meaning to gather in;

H4201.notes: Transliteration: mᵉzûwzâh; Pronunciation: mez-oo-zaw'; Derivation: or מְזֻזָה; from the same as H2123 (זִיז);

H4202.notes: Transliteration: mâzôwn; Pronunciation: maw-zone'; Derivation: from H2109 (זוּן);

H4203.notes: Transliteration: mâzôwn; Pronunciation: maw-zone'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H4202 (מָזוֹן)

H4204.notes: Transliteration: mâzôwr; Pronunciation: maw-zore'; Derivation: from H2114 (זוּר) in the sense of turning aside from truth;

H4205.meaning: a bandage, i.e. remedy; hence, a sore (as needing a compress)
H4205.notes: Transliteration: mâzôwr; Pronunciation: maw-zore'; Derivation: or מָזֹר; from H2115 (זוּר) in the sense of binding up;

H4206.notes: Transliteration: mâzîyach; Pronunciation: maw-zee'-akh; Derivation: or מֵזַח; from H2118 (זָחַח);

H4207.notes: Transliteration: mazlêg; Pronunciation: maz-layg'; Derivation: or (feminine) מִזְלָגָה; from an unused root meaning to draw up;

H4208.meaning: a constellation, i.e. Zodiacal sign (perhaps as affecting the weather)
H4208.notes: Transliteration: mazzâlâh; Pronunciation: maz-zaw-law'; Derivation: apparently from H5140 (נָזַל) in the sense of raining;

H4209.meaning: a plan, usually evil (machination), sometimes good (sagacity)
H4209.kjv: (wicked) device, discretion, intent, witty invention, lewdness, mischievous (device), thought, wickedly.
H4209.notes: Transliteration: mᵉzimmâh; Pronunciation: mez-im-maw'; Derivation: from H2161 (זָמַם);

H4210.meaning: properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes
H4210.notes: Transliteration: mizmôwr; Pronunciation: miz-more'; Derivation: from H2167 (זָמַר);

H4211.notes: Transliteration: mazmêrâh; Pronunciation: maz-may-raw'; Derivation: from H2168 (זָמַר);

H4212.notes: Transliteration: mᵉzammᵉrâh; Pronunciation: mez-am-mer-aw'; Derivation: from H2168 (זָמַר);

H4213.meaning: fewness; by implication, as superl. diminutiveness
H4213.notes: Transliteration: mizʻâr; Pronunciation: miz-awr'; Derivation: from the same as H2191 (זְעֵיר)xlit zeʻêyr corrected to zᵉʻêyr;

H4214.meaning: a winnowing shovel (as scattering the chaff)
H4214.notes: Transliteration: mizreh; Pronunciation: miz-reh'; Derivation: from H2219 (זָרָה);

H4215.meaning: properly, a scatterer, i.e. the north wind (as dispersing clouds; only in plural)
H4215.notes: Transliteration: mᵉzâreh; Pronunciation: mez-aw-reh'; Derivation: apparently from H2219 (זָרָה);

H4216.meaning: some noted constellation (only in the plural), perhaps collectively, the zodiac
H4216.notes: Transliteration: mazzârâh; Pronunciation: maz-zaw-raw'; Derivation: apparently from H5144 (נָזַר) in the sense of distinction;

H4217.notes: Transliteration: mizrâch; Pronunciation: miz-rawkh'; Derivation: from H2224 (זָרַח);

H4218.notes: Transliteration: mizrâʻ; Pronunciation: miz-raw'; Derivation: from H2232 (זָרַע);

H4219.notes: Transliteration: mizrâq; Pronunciation: miz-rawk'; Derivation: from H2236 (זָרַק);

H4220.notes: Transliteration: mêach; Pronunciation: may'-akh; Derivation: from H4229 (מָחָה) in the sense of greasing;

H4221.notes: Transliteration: môach; Pronunciation: mo'-akh; Derivation: from the same as H4220 (מֵחַ);

H4222.meaning: to rub or strike the hands together (in exultation)
H4222.notes: Transliteration: mâchâʼ; Pronunciation: maw-khaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4223.meaning: to strike in pieces; also to arrest; specifically to impale
H4223.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchâʼ; Pronunciation: mekh-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H4222 (מָחָא);

H4224.notes: Transliteration: machăbêʼ; Pronunciation: makh-ab-ay'; Derivation: or מַחֲבֹא; from H2244 (חָבָא);

H4225.meaning: a junction, i.e. seam or sewed piece
H4225.notes: Transliteration: machbereth; Pronunciation: makh-beh'-reth; Derivation: from H2266 (חָבַר);

H4226.meaning: a joiner, i.e. brace or cramp
H4226.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchabbᵉrâh; Pronunciation: mekh-ab-ber-aw'; Derivation: from H2266 (חָבַר);

H4227.notes: Transliteration: machăbath; Pronunciation: makh-ab-ath'; Derivation: from the same as H2281 (חָבֵת);

H4228.notes: Transliteration: machăgôreth; Pronunciation: makh-ag-o'-reth; Derivation: from H2296 (חָגַר);

H4229.meaning: properly, to stroke or rub; by implication, to erase; also to smooth (as if with oil), i.e. grease or make fat; also to touch, i.e. reach to
H4229.kjv: abolish, blot out, destroy, full of marrow, put out, reach unto, [idiom] utterly, wipe (away, out).
H4229.notes: Transliteration: mâchâh; Pronunciation: maw-khaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4230.meaning: an instrument for marking a circle, i.e. compasses
H4230.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchûwgâh; Pronunciation: mekk-oo-gaw'; Derivation: from H2328 (חוּג);

H4231.meaning: a harbor (as shut in by the shore)
H4231.notes: Transliteration: mâchôwz; Pronunciation: maw-khoze'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to enclose;

H4232.meaning: Mechujael or Mechijael, an antediluvian patriarch
H4232.notes: Transliteration: Mᵉchûwyâʼêl; Pronunciation: mekh-oo-yaw-ale'; Derivation: or מְחִיּיָאֵל; from H4229 (מָחָה) and H410 (אֵל); smitten of God;

H4233.meaning: a Machavite or inhabitant of some place named Machaveh
H4233.notes: Transliteration: Machăvîym; Pronunciation: makh-av-eem'; Derivation: apparently a patrial, but from an unknown place (in the plural only for a singular);

H4234.notes: Transliteration: mâchôwl; Pronunciation: maw-khole'; Derivation: from H2342 (חוּל);

H4235.notes: Transliteration: Mâchôwl; Pronunciation: maw-khole'; Derivation: the same as H4234 (מָחוֹל); dancing;

H4236.notes: Transliteration: machăzeh; Pronunciation: makh-az-eh'; Derivation: from H2372 (חָזָה);

H4237.notes: Transliteration: mechĕzâh; Pronunciation: mekh-ez-aw'; Derivation: from H2372 (חָזָה);

H4238.notes: Transliteration: Machăzîyʼôwth; Pronunciation: makh-az-ee-oth'; Derivation: feminine plural from H2372 (חָזָה); visions;

H4239.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchîy; Pronunciation: mekh-ee'; Derivation: from H4229 (מָחָה);

H4240.notes: Transliteration: Mᵉchîydâʼ; Pronunciation: mek-ee-daw'; Derivation: from H2330 (חוּד); junction;

H4241.meaning: preservation of life; hence, sustenance; also the live flesh, i.e. the quick
H4241.kjv: preserve life, quick, recover selves, reviving, sustenance, victuals.
H4241.notes: Transliteration: michyâh; Pronunciation: mikh-yaw'; Derivation: from H2421 (חָיָה);

H4242.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchîyr; Pronunciation: mekk-eer'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to buy;

H4243.notes: Transliteration: Mᵉchîyr; Pronunciation: mekh-eer'; Derivation: the same as H4242 (מְחִיר); price;

H4244.meaning: Machlah, the name apparently of two Israelitesses
H4244.notes: Transliteration: Machlâh; Pronunciation: makh-law'; Derivation: from H2470 (חָלָה); sickness;

H4245.notes: Transliteration: machăleh; Pronunciation: makh-al-eh'; Derivation: or (feminine) מַחֲלָה; from H2470 (חָלָה);

H4246.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchôlâh; Pronunciation: mek-o-law'; Derivation: feminine of H4234 (מָחוֹל);

H4247.notes: Transliteration: mᵉchillâh; Pronunciation: mekh-il-law'; Derivation: from H2490 (חָלַל);

H4248.notes: Transliteration: Machlôwn; Pronunciation: makh-lone'; Derivation: from H2470 (חָלָה); sick;

H4249.meaning: Machli, the name of two Israelites
H4249.notes: Transliteration: Machlîy; Pronunciation: makh-lee'; Derivation: from H2470 (חָלָה); sick;

H4250.meaning: a Machlite or (collectively) descendants of Machli
H4250.notes: Transliteration: Machlîy; Pronunciation: makh-lee'; Derivation: patronymical from H4249 (מַחְלִי);

H4251.notes: Transliteration: machluy; Pronunciation: makh-loo'-ee; Derivation: from H2470 (חָלָה);

H4252.meaning: a (sacrificial) knife (as gliding through the flesh)
H4252.notes: Transliteration: machălâph; Pronunciation: makh-al-awf'; Derivation: from H2498 (חָלַף);

H4253.meaning: a ringlet of hair (as gliding over each other)
H4253.notes: Transliteration: machlâphâh; Pronunciation: makh-law-faw'; Derivation: from H2498 (חָלַף);

H4254.kjv: changeable suit of apparel, change of raiment.
H4254.notes: Transliteration: machălâtsâh; Pronunciation: makh-al-aw-tsaw'; Derivation: from H2502 (חָלַץ);

H4255.notes: Transliteration: machlᵉqâh; Pronunciation: makh-lek-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H4256 (מַחֲלֹקֶת);

H4256.meaning: a section (of Levites, people or soldiers)
H4256.kjv: company, course, division, portion. See also H5555 (סֶלַע הַמַּחְלְקוֹת).
H4256.notes: Transliteration: machălôqeth; Pronunciation: makh-al-o'-keth; Derivation: from H2505 (חָלַק);

H4257.meaning: 'Machalath', probably the title (initial word) of a popular song
H4257.notes: Transliteration: machălath; Pronunciation: makh-al-ath'; Derivation: from H2470 (חָלָה); sickness;

H4258.meaning: Machalath, the name of an Ishmaelitess and of an Israelitess
H4258.notes: Transliteration: Machălath; Pronunciation: makh-al-ath'; Derivation: the same as H4257 (מַחֲלַת); sickness;

H4259.meaning: a Mecholathite or inhabitant of Abel-Mecholah
H4259.notes: Transliteration: Mᵉchôlâthîy; Pronunciation: mekh-o-law-thee'; Derivation: patrial from H65 (אָבֵל מְחוֹלָה);

H4260.meaning: something buttery (i.e. unctuous and pleasant), as (figuratively) flattery
H4260.notes: Transliteration: machămâʼâh; Pronunciation: makh-am-aw-aw'; Derivation: a denominative from H2529 (חֶמְאָה);

H4261.meaning: delightful; hence, a delight, i.e. object of affection or desire
H4261.notes: Transliteration: machmâd; Pronunciation: makh-mawd'; Derivation: from H2530 (חָמַד);

H4262.notes: Transliteration: machmud; Pronunciation: makh-mood'; Derivation: or מַחְמוּד; from H2530 (חָמַד);

H4263.notes: Transliteration: machmâl; Pronunciation: makh-mawl'; Derivation: from H2550 (חָמַל); (by paronomasia with H4261 (מַחְמָד)) delight

H4264.meaning: an encampment (of travellers or troops); hence, an army, whether literal (of soldiers) or figurative (of dancers, angels, cattle, locusts, stars; or even the sacred courts)
H4264.kjv: army, band, battle, camp, company, drove, host, tents.
H4264.notes: Transliteration: machăneh; Pronunciation: makh-an-eh'; Derivation: from H2583 (חָנָה);

H4265.notes: Transliteration: Machănêh-Dân; Pronunciation: makh-an-ay'-dawn; Derivation: from H4264 (מַחֲנֶה) and H1835 (דָּן); camp of Dan;

H4707.notes: Transliteration: mitspeh; Pronunciation: mits-peh'; Derivation: from H6822 (צָפָה);

H4727.notes: Transliteration: miqqâch; Pronunciation: mik-kawkh'; Derivation: from H3947 (לָקַח);

H4728.notes: Transliteration: maqqâchâh; Pronunciation: mak-kaw-khaw'; Derivation: from H3947 (לָקַח);

H4729.meaning: something to fume (incense) on i.e. a hearth place

H4734.notes: Transliteration: miqlaʻath; Pronunciation: mik-lah'-ath; Derivation: from H7049 (קָלַע);

H4737.notes: Transliteration: Miqnêyâhûw; Pronunciation: mik-nay-yaw'-hoo; Derivation: from H4735 (מִקְנֶה) and H3050 (יָהּ); possession of Jah;

H4738.notes: Transliteration: miqçâm; Pronunciation: mik-sawn'; Derivation: from H7080 (קָסַם);

H4988.notes: Transliteration: mâthâq; Pronunciation: maw-thawk'; Derivation: from H4985 (מָתַק);

H4989.meaning: Mithkah, a place in the Desert
H4989.notes: Transliteration: Mithqâh; Pronunciation: mith-kaw'; Derivation: feminine of H4987 (מֹתֶק); sweetness;

H4990.meaning: Mithredath, the name of two Persians
H4990.notes: Transliteration: Mithrᵉdâth; Pronunciation: mith-red-awth'; Derivation: of Persian origin;

H4991.notes: Transliteration: mattâth; Pronunciation: mat-tawth'; Derivation: feminine of H4976 (מַתָּן) abbreviated form;

H4992.notes: Transliteration: Mattattâh; Pronunciation: mat-tat-taw'; Derivation: for H4993 (מַתִּתְיָה); gift of Jah;

H4993.meaning: Mattithjah, the name of four Israelites
H4993.notes: Transliteration: Mattithyâh; Pronunciation: mat-tith-yaw'; Derivation: or מַתִּתְיָהוּ; from H4991 (מַתָּת) and H3050 (יָהּ); gift of Jah;

H4994.meaning: 'I pray', 'now', or 'then'; added mostly to verbs (in the Imperative or Future), or to interjections, occasionally to an adverb or conjunction
H4994.kjv: I beseech (pray) thee (you), go to, now, oh.
H4994.notes: Transliteration: nâʼ; Pronunciation: naw; Derivation: a primitive particle of incitement and entreaty, which may usually be rendered;

H4995.notes: Transliteration: nâʼ; Pronunciation: naw; Derivation: apparently from H5106 (נוּא) in the sense of harshness from refusal;

H4996.meaning: No (i.e. Thebes), the capital of Upper Egypt
H4996.notes: Transliteration: Nôʼ; Pronunciation: no; Derivation: of Egyptian origin;

H4998.meaning: properly, to be at home, i.e. (by implication) to be pleasant (or suitable), i.e. beautiful
H4998.notes: Transliteration: nâʼâh; Pronunciation: naw-aw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H4999.notes: Transliteration: nâʼâh; Pronunciation: naw-aw'; Derivation: from H4998 (נָאָה);

H5000.notes: Transliteration: nâʼveh; Pronunciation: naw-veh'; Derivation: from H4998 (נָאָה) or H5116 (נָוֶה);

H5001.meaning: properly, to whisper, i.e. (by implication) to utter as aoracle
H5001.notes: Transliteration: nâʼam; Pronunciation: naw-am'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5002.notes: Transliteration: nᵉʼum; Pronunciation: neh-oom'; Derivation: from H5001 (נָאַם);

H5003.meaning: to commit adultery; figuratively, to apostatize
H5003.kjv: adulterer(-ess), commit(-ing) adultery, woman that breaketh wedlock.
H5003.notes: Transliteration: nâʼaph; Pronunciation: naw-af'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5004.notes: Transliteration: niʼuph; Pronunciation: nee-oof'; Derivation: from H5003 (נָאַף);

H5005.notes: Transliteration: naʼăphûwph; Pronunciation: nah-af-oof'; Derivation: from H5003 (נָאַף);

H5006.kjv: abhor, (give occasion to) blaspheme, contemn, despise, flourish, [idiom] great, provoke.
H5006.notes: Transliteration: nâʼats; Pronunciation: naw-ats'; Derivation: a primitive root; in Ecclesiastes 12:5, by interchange for H5132 (נוּץ), to bloom

H5007.notes: Transliteration: nᵉʼâtsâh; Pronunciation: neh-aw-tsaw'; Derivation: or נֶאָצָה; from H5006 (נָאַץ);

H5008.notes: Transliteration: nâʼaq; Pronunciation: naw-ak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5009.notes: Transliteration: nᵉʼâqâh; Pronunciation: neh-aw-kaw'; Derivation: from H5008 (נָאַק);

H5010.notes: Transliteration: nâʼar; Pronunciation: naw-ar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5011.notes: Transliteration: Nôb; Pronunciation: nobe; Derivation: the same as H5108 (נוֹב); fruit;

H5012.meaning: to prophesy, i.e. speak (or sing) by inspiration (in prediction or simple discourse)
H5012.notes: Transliteration: nâbâʼ; Pronunciation: naw-baw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5013.meaning: to prophesy, i.e. speak (or sing) by inspiration (in prediction or simple discourse) to prophesy, i.e. speak (or sing) by inspiration (in prediction or simple discourse)
H5013.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbâʼ; Pronunciation: neb-aw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5012 (נָבָא)

H5014.meaning: to pierce; to be hollow, or (figuratively) foolish
H5014.notes: Transliteration: nâbab; Pronunciation: naw-bab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5015.meaning: Nebo, the name of a Babylonian deity, also of a mountain in Moab, and of a place in Palestine
H5015.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉbôw; Pronunciation: neb-o'; Derivation: probably of foreign derivation;

H5016.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbûwʼâh; Pronunciation: neb-oo-aw'; Derivation: from H5012 (נָבָא);

H5017.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbûwʼâh; Pronunciation: neb-oo-aw; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5016 (נְבוּאָה);

H5018.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉbûwzarʼădân; Pronunciation: neb-oo-zar-ad-awn'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H5020.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉbûwkadnetstsar; Pronunciation: neb-oo-kad-nets-tsar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5019 (נְבוּכַדְנֶאצַּר)

H5021.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉbûwshazbân; Pronunciation: neb-oo-shaz-bawn'; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H5022.notes: Transliteration: Nâbôwth; Pronunciation: naw-both'; Derivation: feminine plural from the same as H5011 (נֹב); fruits;

H5023.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbizbâh; Pronunciation: neb-iz-baw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) of uncertain derivation;

H5024.notes: Transliteration: nâbach; Pronunciation: naw-bakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5025.meaning: Nobach, the name of an Israelite, and of a place East of the Jordan
H5025.notes: Transliteration: Nôbach; Pronunciation: no'-bach; Derivation: from H5024 (נָבַח); a bark;

H5026.meaning: Nibchaz, a deity of the Avites
H5026.notes: Transliteration: Nibchaz; Pronunciation: nib-khaz'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H5027.meaning: to scan, i.e. look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care
H5027.kjv: (cause to) behold, consider, look (down), regard, have respect, see.
H5027.notes: Transliteration: nâbaṭ; Pronunciation: naw-bat'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5028.meaning: Nebat, the father of Jeroboam I
H5028.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉbâṭ; Pronunciation: neb-awt'; Derivation: from H5027 (נָבַט); regard;

H5029.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbîyʼ; Pronunciation: neb-ee'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5030 (נָבִיא);

H5030.notes: Transliteration: nâbîyʼ; Pronunciation: naw-bee'; Derivation: from H5012 (נָבָא);

H5031.meaning: a prophetess or (generally) inspired woman; by implication, a poetess; by association a prophet's wife
H5031.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbîyʼâh; Pronunciation: neb-ee-yaw'; Derivation: feminine of H5030 (נָבִיא);

H5032.meaning: Nebajoth, a son of Ismael, and the country settled by him
H5032.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉbâyôwth; Pronunciation: neb-aw-yoth'; Derivation: or נְבָיֹת; feminine plural from H5107 (נוּב); fruitfulnesses;

H5033.notes: Transliteration: nêbek; Pronunciation: nay'-bek; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to burst forth;

H5034.meaning: to wilt; generally, to fall away, fail, faint; figuratively, to be foolish or (morally) wicked; causatively, to despise, disgrace
H5034.kjv: disgrace, dishounour, lightly esteem, fade (away, -ing), fall (down, -ling, off), do foolishly, come to nought, [idiom] surely, make vile, wither.
H5034.notes: Transliteration: nâbêl; Pronunciation: naw-bale'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5035.meaning: a skin-bag for liquids (from collapsing when empty); hence, a vase (as similar in shape when full); also a lyre (as having a body of like form)
H5035.notes: Transliteration: nebel; Pronunciation: neh'-bel; Derivation: or נֵבֶל; from H5034 (נָבֵל);

H5036.notes: Transliteration: nâbâl; Pronunciation: naw-bawl'; Derivation: from H5034 (נָבֵל);

H5037.notes: Transliteration: Nâbâl; Pronunciation: naw-bawl'; Derivation: the same as H5036 (נָבָל); dolt;

H5038.meaning: a flabby thing, i.e. a carcase or carrion (human or bestial, often collectively); figuratively, an idol
H5038.kjv: (dead) body, (dead) carcase, dead of itself, which died, (beast) that (which) dieth of itself.
H5038.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbêlâh; Pronunciation: neb-ay-law'; Derivation: from H5034 (נָבֵל);

H5039.meaning: foolishness, i.e. (morally) wickedness; concretely, a crime; by extension, punishment
H5039.notes: Transliteration: nᵉbâlâh; Pronunciation: neb-aw-law'; Derivation: feminine of H5036 (נָבָל);

H5040.notes: Transliteration: nablûwth; Pronunciation: nab-looth'; Derivation: from H5036 (נָבָל);

H5041.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉballâṭ; Pronunciation: neb-al-lawt'; Derivation: apparently from H5036 (נָבָל) and H3909 (לָט); foolish secrecy;

H5042.meaning: to gush forth; figuratively, to utter (good or bad words); specifically, to emit (a foul odor)
H5042.kjv: belch out, flowing, pour out, send forth, utter (abundantly).
H5042.notes: Transliteration: nâbaʻ; Pronunciation: naw-bah'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5043.notes: Transliteration: nebrᵉshâʼ; Pronunciation: neb-reh-shaw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from an unused root meaning to shine;

H5044.notes: Transliteration: Nibshân; Pronunciation: nib-shawn'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H5045.meaning: the south (from its drought); specifically, the Negeb or southern district of Judah, occasionally, Egypt (as south to Palestine)
H5045.notes: Transliteration: negeb; Pronunciation: neh'-gheb; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to be parched;

H5046.meaning: properly, to front, i.e. stand boldly out opposite; by implication (causatively), to manifest; figuratively, to announce (always by word of mouth to one present); specifically, to expose, predict, explain, praise
H5046.kjv: bewray, [idiom] certainly, certify, declare(-ing), denounce, expound, [idiom] fully, messenger, plainly, profess, rehearse, report, shew (forth), speak, [idiom] surely, tell, utter.
H5046.notes: Transliteration: nâgad; Pronunciation: naw-gad'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5047.meaning: to flow (through the idea of clearing the way)
H5047.notes: Transliteration: nᵉgad; Pronunciation: neg-ad'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5046 (נָגַד);

H5048.meaning: a front, i.e. part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually (adverbial, especially with preposition) over against or before
H5048.kjv: about, (over) against, [idiom] aloof, [idiom] far (off), [idiom] from, over, presence, [idiom] other side, sight, [idiom] to view.
H5048.notes: Transliteration: neged; Pronunciation: neh'-ghed; Derivation: from H5046 (נָגַד);

H5049.notes: Transliteration: neged; Pronunciation: neh'-ghed; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5048 (נֶגֶד);

H5050.notes: Transliteration: nâgahh; Pronunciation: naw-gah'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5051.notes: Transliteration: nôgahh; Pronunciation: no'-gah; Derivation: from H5050 (נָגַהּ);

H5052.notes: Transliteration: Nôgahh; Pronunciation: no'-gah; Derivation: the same as H5051 (נֹגַהּ);

H5053.notes: Transliteration: nôgahh; Pronunciation: no'-gah; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5051 (נֹגַהּ);

H5054.notes: Transliteration: nᵉgôhâh; Pronunciation: neg-o-haw'; Derivation: feminine of H5051 (נֹגַהּ);

H5055.meaning: to butt with the horns; figuratively, to war against
H5055.notes: Transliteration: nâgach; Pronunciation: naw-gakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5056.notes: Transliteration: naggâch; Pronunciation: nag-gawkh'; Derivation: from H5055 (נָגַח);

H5057.meaning: a commander (as occupying the front), civil, military or religious; generally (abstractly, plural), honorable themes
H5057.kjv: captain, chief, excellent thing, (chief) governor, leader, noble, prince, (chief) ruler.
H5057.notes: Transliteration: nâgîyd; Pronunciation: naw-gheed'; Derivation: or נָגִד; from H5046 (נָגַד);

H5058.meaning: properly, instrumental music; by implication, a stringed instrument; by extension, a poem set to music; specifically, an epigram
H5058.notes: Transliteration: nᵉgîynâh; Pronunciation: neg-ee-naw'; Derivation: or נְגִינַת; (Psalm 61:title), from H5059 (נָגַן);

H5059.meaning: properly, to thrum, i.e. beat atune with the fingers; expectation. to play on astringed instrument; hence (generally), to make music
H5059.kjv: player on instruments, sing to the stringed instruments, melody, ministrel, play(-er, -ing).
H5059.notes: Transliteration: nâgan; Pronunciation: naw-gan'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5060.meaning: properly, to touch, i.e. lay the hand upon (for any purpose; euphemistically, to lie with a woman); by implication, to reach (figuratively, to arrive, acquire); violently, to strike (punish, defeat, destroy, etc.)
H5060.kjv: beat, ([idiom] be able to) bring (down), cast, come (nigh), draw near (nigh), get up, happen, join, near, plague, reach (up), smite, strike, touch.
H5060.notes: Transliteration: nâgaʻ; Pronunciation: naw-gah'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5061.meaning: a blow (figuratively, infliction); also (by implication) a spot (concretely, a leprous person or dress)
H5061.kjv: plague, sore, stricken, stripe, stroke, wound.
H5061.notes: Transliteration: negaʻ; Pronunciation: neh'-gah; Derivation: from H5060 (נָגַע);

H5062.meaning: to push, gore, defeat, stub (the toe), inflict (a disease)
H5062.kjv: beat, dash, hurt, plague, slay, smite (down), strike, stumble, [idiom] surely, put to the worse.
H5062.notes: Transliteration: nâgaph; Pronunciation: naw-gaf'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5063.meaning: a trip (of the foot); figuratively, an infliction (of disease)
H5063.notes: Transliteration: negeph; Pronunciation: neh'-ghef; Derivation: from H5062 (נָגַף);

H5064.meaning: to flow; figuratively, to stretch out; causatively, to pour out or down; figuratively, to deliver over
H5064.kjv: fall, flow away, pour down (out), run, shed, spilt, trickle down.
H5064.notes: Transliteration: nâgar; Pronunciation: naw-gar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5065.meaning: to drive (an animal, a workman, a debtor, an army); by implication, to tax, harass, tyrannize
H5065.kjv: distress, driver, exact(-or), oppress(-or), [idiom] raiser of taxes, taskmaster.
H5065.notes: Transliteration: nâgas; Pronunciation: naw-gas'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5066.meaning: to be or come (causatively, bring) near (for any purpose); euphemistically, to lie with a woman; as an enemy, to attack; religious to worship; causatively, to present; figuratively, to adduce an argument; by reversal, to stand back
H5066.kjv: (make to) approach (nigh), bring (forth, hither, near), (cause to) come (hither, near, nigh), give place, go hard (up), (be, draw, go) near (nigh), offer, overtake, present, put, stand.
H5066.notes: Transliteration: nâgash; Pronunciation: naw-gash'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5067.notes: Transliteration: nêd; Pronunciation: nade; Derivation: from H5110 (נוּד) in the sense of piling up;

H5068.meaning: to impel; hence, to volunteer (as a soldier), to present spontaneously
H5068.kjv: offer freely, be (give, make, offer self) willing(-ly).
H5068.notes: Transliteration: nâdab; Pronunciation: naw-dab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5069.notes: Transliteration: nᵉdab; Pronunciation: ned-ab'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5068 (נָדַב);

H5070.meaning: Nadab, the name of four Israelites
H5070.notes: Transliteration: Nâdâb; Pronunciation: naw-dawb'; Derivation: from H5068 (נָדַב); liberal;

H5071.meaning: properly (abstractly) spontaneity, or (adjectively) spontaneous; also (concretely) a spontaneous or (by inference, in plural) abundant gift
H5071.kjv: free(-will) offering, freely, plentiful, voluntary(-ily, offering), willing(-ly), offering).
H5071.notes: Transliteration: nᵉdâbâh; Pronunciation: ned-aw-baw'; Derivation: from H5068 (נָדַב);

H5072.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉdabyâh; Pronunciation: ned-ab-yaw'; Derivation: from H5068 (נָדַב) and H3050 (יָהּ); largess of Jah;

H5073.notes: Transliteration: nidbâk; Pronunciation: nid-bawk'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root meaning to stick;

H5074.meaning: properly, to wave to and fro (rarely to flap up and down); figuratively, to rove, flee, or (causatively) to drive away
H5074.kjv: chase (away), [idiom] could not, depart, flee ([idiom] apace, away), (re-) move, thrust away, wander (abroad, -er, -ing).
H5074.notes: Transliteration: nâdad; Pronunciation: naw-dad'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5075.notes: Transliteration: nᵉdad; Pronunciation: ned-ad'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5074 (נָדַד);

H5076.meaning: properly, tossed; abstractly, a rolling (on the bed)
H5076.notes: Transliteration: nâdud; Pronunciation: naw-dood'; Derivation: passive participle of H5074 (נָדַד);

H5077.meaning: properly, to toss; figuratively, to exclude, i.e. banish, postpone, prohibit
H5077.kjv: cast out, drive, put far away.
H5077.notes: Transliteration: nâdâh; Pronunciation: naw-daw'; Derivation: or נָדָא; (2 Kings 17:21), a primitive root;

H5078.notes: Transliteration: nêdeh; Pronunciation: nay'-deh; Derivation: from H5077 (נָדָה) in the sense of freely flinging money;

H5079.meaning: properly, rejection; by implication, impurity, especially personal (menstruation) or moral (idolatry, incest)
H5079.kjv: [idiom] far, filthiness, [idiom] flowers, menstruous (woman), put apart, [idiom] removed (woman), separation, set apart, unclean(-ness, thing, with filthiness).
H5079.notes: Transliteration: niddâh; Pronunciation: nid-daw'; Derivation: from H5074 (נָדַד);

H5080.meaning: to push off; used in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively (to expel, mislead, strike, inflict, etc.)
H5080.kjv: banish, bring, cast down (out), chase, compel, draw away, drive (away, out, quite), fetch a stroke, force, go away, outcast, thrust away (out), withdraw.
H5080.notes: Transliteration: nâdach; Pronunciation: naw-dakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5081.meaning: properly, voluntary, i.e. generous; hence, magnanimous; as noun, a grandee (sometimes a tyrant)
H5081.notes: Transliteration: nâdîyb; Pronunciation: naw-deeb'; Derivation: from H5068 (נָדַב);

H5082.notes: Transliteration: nᵉdîybâh; Pronunciation: ned-ee-baw'; Derivation: feminine of H5081 (נָדִיב);

H5083.notes: Transliteration: nâdân; Pronunciation: naw-dawn'; Derivation: probably from an unused root meaning to give;

H5084.notes: Transliteration: nâdân; Pronunciation: naw-dawn'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H5085.meaning: a sheath; figuratively, the body (as the receptacle of the soul)
H5085.notes: Transliteration: nidneh; Pronunciation: nid-neh'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from the same as H5084 (נָדָן);

H5086.kjv: drive (away, to and fro), thrust down, shaken, tossed to and fro.
H5086.notes: Transliteration: nâdaph; Pronunciation: naw-daf'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5087.meaning: to promise (pos., to do or give something to God)
H5087.notes: Transliteration: nâdar; Pronunciation: naw-dar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5088.meaning: a promise (to God); also (concretely) a thing promised
H5088.notes: Transliteration: neder; Pronunciation: neh'-der; Derivation: or נֵדֶר; from H5087 (נָדַר);

H5089.notes: Transliteration: nôahh; Pronunciation: no'-ah; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to lament;

H5090.meaning: to drive forth (a person, an animal or chariot), i.e. lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed (i.e. impel or guide oneself); also (from the panting induced by effort), to sigh
H5090.kjv: acquaint, bring (away), carry away, drive (away), lead (away, forth), (be) guide, lead (away, forth).
H5090.notes: Transliteration: nâhag; Pronunciation: naw-hag'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5091.meaning: to groan, i.e. bewail; hence (through the idea of crying aloud), to assemble (as if on proclamation)
H5091.notes: Transliteration: nâhâh; Pronunciation: naw-haw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5092.notes: Transliteration: nᵉhîy; Pronunciation: neh-hee'; Derivation: from H5091 (נָהָה);

H5093.notes: Transliteration: nihyâh; Pronunciation: nih-yaw'; Derivation: feminine of H5092 (נְהִי);

H5094.notes: Transliteration: nᵉhîyr; Pronunciation: neh-heere'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or נְהִירוּ; (Aramaic), from the same as H5105 (נְהָרָה);

H5095.meaning: properly, to run with asparkle, i.e. flow; hence (transitively), to conduct, and (by inference) to protect, sustain
H5095.notes: Transliteration: nâhal; Pronunciation: naw-hal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5096.meaning: Nahalal or Nahalol, a place in Palestine
H5096.notes: Transliteration: Nahălâl; Pronunciation: nah-hal-awl'; Derivation: or נַהֲלֹל; the same as H5097 (נַהֲלֹל);

H5097.notes: Transliteration: nahălôl; Pronunciation: nah-hal-ole'; Derivation: from H5095 (נָהַל);

H5098.notes: Transliteration: nâham; Pronunciation: naw-ham'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5099.notes: Transliteration: naham; Pronunciation: nah'-ham; Derivation: from H5098 (נָהַם);

H5100.notes: Transliteration: nᵉhâmâh; Pronunciation: neh-haw-maw'; Derivation: feminine of H5099 (נַהַם);

H5101.meaning: to bray (as an ass), scream (from hunger)
H5101.notes: Transliteration: nâhaq; Pronunciation: naw-hak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5102.meaning: to sparkle, i.e. (figuratively) be cheerful; hence (from the sheen of a running stream) to flow, i.e. (figuratively) assemble
H5102.notes: Transliteration: nâhar; Pronunciation: naw-har'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5103.notes: Transliteration: nᵉhar; Pronunciation: neh-har'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5102 (נָהַר);

H5104.meaning: a stream (including the sea; expectation the Nile, Euphrates, etc.); figuratively, prosperity
H5104.notes: Transliteration: nâhâr; Pronunciation: naw-hawr'; Derivation: from H5102 (נָהַר);

H5105.notes: Transliteration: nᵉhârâh; Pronunciation: neh-haw-raw'; Derivation: from H5102 (נָהַר) in its original sense;

H5106.meaning: to refuse, forbid, dissuade, or neutralize
H5106.kjv: break, disallow, discourage, make of none effect.
H5106.notes: Transliteration: nûwʼ; Pronunciation: noo; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5107.meaning: to germinate, i.e. (figuratively) to (causatively, make) flourish; also (of words), to utter
H5107.notes: Transliteration: nûwb; Pronunciation: noob; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5108.notes: Transliteration: nôwb; Pronunciation: nobe; Derivation: or נֵיב; from H5107 (נוּב);

H5109.notes: Transliteration: Nôwbay; Pronunciation: no-bah'ee; Derivation: from H5108 (נוֹב); fruitful;

H5110.meaning: to nod, i.e. waver; figuratively, to wander, flee, disappear; also (from shaking the head in sympathy), to console, deplore, or (from tossing the head in scorn) taunt

H5111.notes: Transliteration: nûwd; Pronunciation: nood; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5116 (נָוֶה);

H5112.notes: Transliteration: nôwd; Pronunciation: node; Derivation: (only defective נֹד ); from H5110 (נוּד);

H5114.notes: Transliteration: Nôwdâb; Pronunciation: no-dawb'; Derivation: from H5068 (נָדַב); noble;

H5115.meaning: to rest (as at home); causatively (through the implied idea of beauty ), to celebrate (with praises)
