H5116.notes: Transliteration: nâveh; Pronunciation: naw-veh'; Derivation: or (feminine) נָוָה; from H5115 (נָוָה);

H5117.meaning: to rest, i.e. settle down; used in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, intransitive, transitive and causative (to dwell, stay, let fall, place, let alone, withdraw, give comfort, etc.)
H5117.kjv: cease, be confederate, lay, let down, (be) quiet, remain, (cause to, be at, give, have, make to) rest, set down. Compare H3241 (יָנִים).
H5117.notes: Transliteration: nûwach; Pronunciation: noo'-akh; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5118.notes: Transliteration: nûwach; Pronunciation: noo'-akh; Derivation: or נוֹחַ; from H5117 (נוּחַ);

H5119.notes: Transliteration: Nôwchâh; Pronunciation: no-chaw'; Derivation: feminine of H5118 (נוּחַ); quietude;

H5121.notes: Transliteration: Nâvîyth; Pronunciation: naw-veeth'; Derivation: from H5115 (נָוָה); residence;

H5122.notes: Transliteration: nᵉvâlûw; Pronunciation: nev-aw-loo'; Derivation: (Aramaic) or נְוָלִי; (Aramaic), from an unused root probably meaning to be foul;

H5123.notes: Transliteration: nûwm; Pronunciation: noom; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5124.notes: Transliteration: nûwmâh; Pronunciation: noo-maw'; Derivation: from H5123 (נוּם);

H5125.meaning: to resprout, i.e. propagate by shoots; figuratively, to be perpetual
H5125.notes: Transliteration: nûwn; Pronunciation: noon; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5126.meaning: Nun or Non, the father of Joshua
H5126.notes: Transliteration: Nûwn; Pronunciation: noon; Derivation: or נוֹן; (1 Chronicles 7:27), from H5125 (נוּן); perpetuity,

H5127.meaning: to flit, i.e. vanish away (subside, escape; causatively, chase, impel, deliver)

H5128.meaning: to waver, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively (as subjoined)

H5130.meaning: to quiver (i.e. vibrate up and down, or rock to and fro); used in a great variety of applications (including sprinkling, beckoning, rubbing, bastinadoing, sawing, waving, etc.)

H5133.meaning: a pinion (or wing feather); often (collectively) plumage

H5134.notes: Transliteration: nûwq; Pronunciation: nook; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5135.notes: Transliteration: nûwr; Pronunciation: noor; Derivation: (Aramaic) from an unused root (corresponding to that of H5216 (נִיר)) meaning to shine;

H5136.notes: Transliteration: nûwsh; Pronunciation: noosh; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5137.meaning: to spirt, i.e. besprinkle (especially in expiation)
H5137.notes: Transliteration: nâzâh; Pronunciation: naw-zaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5138.notes: Transliteration: nâzîyd; Pronunciation: naw-zeed'; Derivation: from H2102 (זוּד);

H5139.meaning: separate, i.e. consecrated (as prince, a Nazirite); hence (figuratively from the latter) an unpruned vine (like an unshorn Nazirite)

H5139.notes: Transliteration: nâzîyr; Pronunciation: naw-zeer'; Derivation: or נָזִר; from H5144 (נָזַר);

H5141.notes: Transliteration: nezem; Pronunciation: neh'-zem; Derivation: from an unused root of uncertain meaning;

H5142.notes: Transliteration: nᵉzaq; Pronunciation: nez-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to the root of H5143 (נֵזֶק);

H5143.notes: Transliteration: nêzeq; Pronunciation: nay'zek; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to injure;

H5146.meaning: Noach, the patriarch of the flood

H5147.notes: Transliteration: Nachbîy; Pronunciation: nakh-bee'; Derivation: from H2247 (חָבָה); occult;

H5148.meaning: to guide; by implication, to transport (into exile, or as colonists)

H5156.notes: Transliteration: nᵉchîyr; Pronunciation: nekh-eer'; Derivation: from the same as H5170 (נַחַר);

H5166.meaning: Nechemjah, the name of three Israelites

H5172.meaning: properly, to hiss, i.e. whisper a (magic) spell; generally, to prognosticate

H5174.notes: Transliteration: nᵉchâsh; Pronunciation: nekh-awsh'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5154 (נְחוּשָׁה);

H5188.notes: Transliteration: nᵉṭîyphâh; Pronunciation: net-ee-faw'; Derivation: from H5197 (נָטַף);

H5189.notes: Transliteration: nᵉṭîyshâh; Pronunciation: net-ee-shaw'; Derivation: from H5203 (נָטַשׁ);

H5190.meaning: to lift; by implication, to impose
H5190.notes: Transliteration: nâṭal; Pronunciation: naw-tal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5191.notes: Transliteration: nᵉṭal; Pronunciation: net-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5190 (נָטַל);

H5192.notes: Transliteration: nêṭel; Pronunciation: nay'-tel; Derivation: ; from H5190 (נָטַל);

H5193.meaning: properly, to strike in, i.e. fix; specifically, to plant (literally or figuratively)

H5195.notes: Transliteration: nâṭîyaʻ; Pronunciation: naw-tee'-ah; Derivation: from H5193 (נָטַע);

H5198.meaning: a drop; specifically, an aromatic gum (probably stacte)
H5198.notes: Transliteration: nâṭâph; Pronunciation: naw-tawf'; Derivation: from H5197 (נָטַף);

H5199.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉṭôphâh; Pronunciation: net-o-faw'; Derivation: from H5197 (נָטַף); distillation;

H5200.meaning: a Netophathite, or inhabitant of Netophah

H5201.notes: Transliteration: nâṭar; Pronunciation: naw-tar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5202.notes: Transliteration: nᵉṭar; Pronunciation: net-ar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5201 (נָטַר);

H5203.meaning: properly, to pound, i.e. smite; by implication (as if beating out, and thus expanding) to disperse; also, to thrust off, down, out or upon (inclusively, reject, let alone, permit, remit, etc.)

H5204.notes: Transliteration: nîy; Pronunciation: nee; Derivation: a doubtful word; apparently from H5091 (נָהָה);

H5205.notes: Transliteration: nîyd; Pronunciation: need; Derivation: from H5110 (נוּד);

H5206.notes: Transliteration: nîydâh; Pronunciation: nee-daw'; Derivation: feminine of H5205 (נִיד);

H5207.meaning: properly, restful, i.e. pleasant; abstractly, delight
H5207.notes: Transliteration: nîychôwach; Pronunciation: nee-kho'-akh; Derivation: or נִיחֹחַ; from H5117 (נוּחַ);

H5208.notes: Transliteration: nîychôwach; Pronunciation: nee-kho'-akh; Derivation: (Aramaic) or נִיחֹחַ; (shorter) (Aramaic), corresponding to H5207 (נִיחוֹחַ);

H5209.notes: Transliteration: nîyn; Pronunciation: neen; Derivation: from H5125 (נוּן);

H5210.notes: Transliteration: Nîynᵉvêh; Pronunciation: nee-nev-ay'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H5211.notes: Transliteration: nîyç; Pronunciation: neece; Derivation: from H5127 (נוּס);

H5212.meaning: Nisan, the first month of the Jewish sacred year

H5213.notes: Transliteration: nîytsôwts; Pronunciation: nee-tsotes'; Derivation: from H5340 (נָצַץ);

H5215.meaning: properly, plowing, i.e. (concretely) freshly plowed land

H5219.meaning: properly, a smiting, i.e. (concretely) an aromatic gum (perhaps styrax) (as powdered)

H5221.meaning: to strike (lightly or severely, literally or figuratively)

H5225.notes: Transliteration: Nâkôwn; Pronunciation: naw-kone'; Derivation: from H3559 (כּוּן); prepared;

H5226.meaning: properly, the fore part; used adverbially, opposite

H5228.meaning: straightforward, i.e. (figuratively), equitable, correct, or (abstractly), integrity
H5228.notes: Transliteration: nâkôach; Pronunciation: naw-ko'-akh; Derivation: from the same as H5226 (נֵכַח);

H5229.notes: Transliteration: nᵉkôchâh; Pronunciation: nek-o-khaw'; Derivation: feminine of H5228 (נָכֹחַ);

H5230.notes: Transliteration: nâkal; Pronunciation: naw-kal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5231.notes: Transliteration: nêkel; Pronunciation: nay'-kel; Derivation: from H5230 (נָכַל);

H5232.notes: Transliteration: nᵉkaç; Pronunciation: nek-as'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5233 (נֶכֶס)

H5233.notes: Transliteration: nekeç; Pronunciation: neh'-kes; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to accumulate;

H5234.meaning: properly, to scrutinize, i.e. look intently at; hence (with recognition implied), to acknowledge, be acquainted with, care for, respect, revere, or (with suspicion implied), to disregard, ignore, be strange toward, reject, resign, dissimulate (as if ignorant or disowning)
H5234.kjv: acknowledge, [idiom] could, deliver, discern, dissemble, estrange, feign self to be another, know, take knowledge (notice), perceive, regard, (have) respect, behave (make) self strange(-ly).
H5234.notes: Transliteration: nâkar; Pronunciation: naw-kar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5235.notes: Transliteration: neker; Pronunciation: neh'-ker; Derivation: or נֹכֶר; from H5234 (נָכַר);

H5236.meaning: foreign, or (concretely) a foreigner, or (abstractly) heathendom
H5236.notes: Transliteration: nêkâr; Pronunciation: nay-kawr'; Derivation: ' from H5234 (נָכַר);

H5237.meaning: strange, in a variety of degrees and applications (foreign, non-relative, adulterous, different, wonderful)
H5237.notes: Transliteration: nokrîy; Pronunciation: nok-ree'; Derivation: from H5235 (נֶכֶר) (second form);

H5238.notes: Transliteration: nᵉkôth; Pronunciation: nek-oth'; Derivation: probably for H5219 (נְכֹאת);

H5239.notes: Transliteration: nâlâh; Pronunciation: naw-law'; Derivation: apparently a primitive root;

H5240.notes: Transliteration: nᵉmibzeh; Pronunciation: nem-ib-zeh'; Derivation: from H959 (בָּזָה),

H5241.meaning: Nemuel, the name of two Israelites
H5241.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉmûwʼêl; Pronunciation: nem-oo-ale'; Derivation: apparently for H3223 (יְמוּאֵל);

H5242.meaning: a Nemuelite, or descendant of Nemuel
H5242.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉmûwʼêlîy; Pronunciation: nem-oo-ay-lee'; Derivation: from H5241 (נְמוּאֵל);

H5243.notes: Transliteration: nâmal; Pronunciation: naw-mal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5244.meaning: an ant (probably from its almost bisected form)
H5244.notes: Transliteration: nᵉmâlâh; Pronunciation: nem-aw-law'; Derivation: feminine from H5243 (נָמַל);

H5245.notes: Transliteration: nᵉmar; Pronunciation: nem-ar'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5246 (נָמֵר)

H5247.meaning: Nimrah, a place East of the Jordan
H5247.kjv: Nimrah. See also H1039 (בֵּית נִמְרָה), H5249 (נִמְרִים).
H5247.notes: Transliteration: Nimrâh; Pronunciation: nim-raw'; Derivation: from the same as H5246 (נָמֵר); clear water;

H5248.notes: Transliteration: Nimrôwd; Pronunciation: nim-rode'; Derivation: or נִמְרֹד; probably of foreign origin;

H5249.meaning: Nimrim, a place East of the Jordan
H5249.notes: Transliteration: Nimrîym; Pronunciation: nim-reem'; Derivation: plural of a masculine corresponding to H5247 (נִמְרָה); clear waters;

H5250.notes: Transliteration: Nimshîy; Pronunciation: nim-shee'; Derivation: probably from H4871 (מָשָׁה); extricated;

H5251.meaning: a flag; also a sail; by implication, a flagstaff; generally a signal; figuratively, a token
H5251.notes: Transliteration: nêç; Pronunciation: nace; Derivation: from H5264 (נָסַס);

H5252.meaning: properly, an environment, i.e. circumstance or turn of affairs
H5252.notes: Transliteration: nᵉçibbâh; Pronunciation: nes-ib-baw'; Derivation: feminine participle passive of H5437 (סָבַב);

H5253.kjv: departing away, remove, take (hold), turn away.
H5253.notes: Transliteration: nâçag; Pronunciation: naw-sag'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5254.meaning: to test; by implication, to attempt
H5254.notes: Transliteration: nâçâh; Pronunciation: naw-saw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5255.notes: Transliteration: nâçach; Pronunciation: naw-sakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5256.notes: Transliteration: nᵉçach; Pronunciation: nes-akh'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5255 (נָסַח)

H5257.meaning: properly, something poured out, i.e. a libation; also a molten image; by implication, a prince (as anointed)
H5257.notes: Transliteration: nᵉçîyk; Pronunciation: nes-eek'; Derivation: from H5258 (נָסַךְ);

H5258.meaning: to pour out, especially a libation, or to cast (metal); by analogy, to anoint aking
H5258.kjv: cover, melt, offer, (cause to) pour (out), set (up).
H5258.notes: Transliteration: nâçak; Pronunciation: naw-sak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5259.notes: Transliteration: nâçak; Pronunciation: naw-sak'; Derivation: a primitive root (probably identical with H5258 (נָסַךְ) through the idea of fusion);

H5260.notes: Transliteration: nᵉçak; Pronunciation: nes-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5258 (נָסַךְ);

H5261.notes: Transliteration: nᵉçak; Pronunciation: nes-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5262 (נֶסֶךְ);

H5262.meaning: a libation; also a cast idol
H5262.notes: Transliteration: neçek; Pronunciation: neh'-sek; Derivation: or נֵסֶךְ; from H5258 (נָסַךְ);

H5263.notes: Transliteration: nâçaç; Pronunciation: naw-sas'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5265.meaning: properly, to pull up, especially the tent-pins, i.e. start on ajourney
H5265.kjv: cause to blow, bring, get, (make to) go (away, forth, forward, onward, out), (take) journey, march, remove, set aside (forward), [idiom] still, be on his (go their) way.
H5265.notes: Transliteration: nâçaʻ; Pronunciation: naw-sah'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5266.notes: Transliteration: nâçaq; Pronunciation: naw-sak'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5267.notes: Transliteration: nᵉçaq; Pronunciation: nes-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5266 (נָסַק)

H5268.notes: Transliteration: Niçrôk; Pronunciation: nis-roke'; Derivation: of foreign origin;

H5269.notes: Transliteration: Nêʻâh; Pronunciation: nay-aw'; Derivation: from H5128 (נוּעַ); motion;

H5270.notes: Transliteration: Nôʻâh; Pronunciation: no-aw'; Derivation: from H5128 (נוּעַ); movement;

H5271.meaning: (only in plural collectively or emphatic form) youth, the state (juvenility) or the persons (young people)
H5271.notes: Transliteration: nâʻûwr; Pronunciation: naw-oor'; Derivation: or נָעֻר; and (feminine) נְעֻרָה; properly, passive participle from H5288 (נַעַר) as denominative;

H5272.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉʻîyʼêl; Pronunciation: neh-ee-ale'; Derivation: from H5128 (נוּעַ) and H410 (אֵל); moved of God;

H5273.meaning: delightful (objective or subjective, literal or figurative)
H5273.notes: Transliteration: nâʻîym; Pronunciation: naw-eem'; Derivation: from H5276 (נָעֵם);

H5274.meaning: properly, to fasten up, i.e. with abar or cord; hence, to sandal, i.e. furnish with slippers
H5274.kjv: bolt, inclose, lock, shoe, shut up.
H5274.notes: Transliteration: nâʻal; Pronunciation: naw-al'; Derivation: a primitive root; also denominative from H5275 (נַעַל)

H5275.meaning: properly, a sandal tongue; by extension a sandal or slipper (sometimes as a symbol of occupancy, a refusal to marry, or of something valueless)
H5275.notes: Transliteration: naʻal; Pronunciation: nah'-al; Derivation: or (feminine) נַעֲלָה; from H5274 (נָעַל);

H5276.kjv: pass in beauty, be delight, be pleasant, be sweet.
H5276.notes: Transliteration: nâʻêm; Pronunciation: naw-ame'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5277.notes: Transliteration: Naʻam; Pronunciation: nah'-am; Derivation: from H5276 (נָעֵם); pleasure;

H5278.meaning: agreeableness, i.e. delight, suitableness, splendor or grace
H5278.notes: Transliteration: nôʻam; Pronunciation: no'-am; Derivation: from H5276 (נָעֵם);

H5279.meaning: Naamah, the name of an antediluvian woman, of an Ammonitess, and of a place in Palestine
H5279.notes: Transliteration: Naʻămâh; Pronunciation: nah-am-aw'; Derivation: feminine of H5277 (נַעַם); pleasantness;

H5280.meaning: a Naamanite, or descendant of Naaman (collectively)
H5280.notes: Transliteration: Naʻămîy; Pronunciation: nah-am-ee'; Derivation: patronymic from H5283 (נַעֲמָן);

H5281.notes: Transliteration: Noʻŏmîy; Pronunciation: no-om-ee'; Derivation: from H5278 (נֹעַם); pleasant;

H5282.notes: Transliteration: naʻămân; Pronunciation: nah-am-awn'; Derivation: from H5276 (נָעֵם);

H5283.meaning: Naaman, the name of an Israelite and of a Damascene
H5283.notes: Transliteration: Naʻămân; Pronunciation: nah-am-awn'; Derivation: the same as H5282 (נַעֲמָן);

H5284.meaning: a Naamathite, or inhabitant of Naamah
H5284.notes: Transliteration: Naʻămâthîy; Pronunciation: nah-am-aw-thee'; Derivation: patrial from a place corresponding in name (but not identical) with H5279 (נַעֲמָה);

H5285.meaning: probably a brier; by implication, a thicket of thorny bushes
H5285.notes: Transliteration: naʻătsûwts; Pronunciation: nah-ats-oots'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to prick;

H5286.notes: Transliteration: nâʻar; Pronunciation: naw-ar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5287.kjv: shake (off, out, self), overthrow, toss up and down.

H5288.meaning: (concretely) a boy (as active), from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also (by interch. of sex), a girl (of similar latitude in age)
H5288.kjv: babe, boy, child, damsel (from the margin), lad, servant, young (man).
H5288.notes: Transliteration: naʻar; Pronunciation: nah'-ar; Derivation: from H5287 (נָעַר);

H5289.notes: Transliteration: naʻar; Pronunciation: nah'-ar; Derivation: from H5287 (נָעַר) in its derivative sense of tossing about;

H5290.notes: Transliteration: nôʻar; Pronunciation: no'-ar; Derivation: from H5287 (נָעַר); (compare H5288 (נַעַר))

H5291.notes: Transliteration: naʻărâh; Pronunciation: nah-ar-aw'; Derivation: feminine of H5288 (נַעַר);

H5292.meaning: Naarah, the name of an Israelitess, and of a place in Palestine
H5292.notes: Transliteration: Naʻărâh; Pronunciation: nah-ar-aw'; Derivation: the same as H5291 (נַעֲרָה);

H5293.notes: Transliteration: Naʻăray; Pronunciation: nah-ar-ah'-ee; Derivation: from H5288 (נַעַר); youthful;

H5294.meaning: Nearjah, the name of two Israelites
H5294.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉʻaryâh; Pronunciation: neh-ar-yaw'; Derivation: from H5288 (נַעַר) and H3050 (יָהּ); servant of Jah;

H5295.notes: Transliteration: Naʻărân; Pronunciation: nah-ar-awn'; Derivation: from H5288 (נַעַר); juvenile;

H5296.meaning: something shaken out, i.e. tow (as the refuse of flax)
H5296.notes: Transliteration: nᵉʻôreth; Pronunciation: neh-o'-reth; Derivation: from H5287 (נָעַר);

H5297.meaning: Noph, the capital of Upper Egypt
H5297.notes: Transliteration: Nôph; Pronunciation: nofe; Derivation: a variation of H4644 (מֹף);

H5298.meaning: Nepheg, the name of two Israelites
H5298.notes: Transliteration: Nepheg; Pronunciation: neh'-feg; Derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to spring forth; a sprout;

H5299.notes: Transliteration: nâphâh; Pronunciation: naw-faw'; Derivation: from H5130 (נוּף) in the sense of lifting;

H5300.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉphûwshᵉçîym; Pronunciation: nef-oo-shes-eem'; Derivation: for H5304 (נְפִיסִים);

H5301.meaning: to puff, in various applications (literally, to inflate, blow hard, scatter, kindle, expire; figuratively, to disesteem)
H5301.kjv: blow, breath, give up, cause to lose (life), seething, snuff.
H5301.notes: Transliteration: nâphach; Pronunciation: naw-fakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5302.notes: Transliteration: Nôphach; Pronunciation: no'-fakh; Derivation: from H5301 (נָפַח); a gust;

H5303.meaning: properly, a feller, i.e. a bully or tyrant
H5303.notes: Transliteration: nᵉphîyl; Pronunciation: nef-eel'; Derivation: or נְפִל; from H5307 (נָפַל);

H5304.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉphîyçîym; Pronunciation: nef-ee-seem'; Derivation: plural from an unused root meaning to scatter; expansions;

H5305.meaning: Naphish, a son of Ishmael, and his posterity
H5305.notes: Transliteration: Nâphîysh; Pronunciation: naw-feesh'; Derivation: from H5314 (נָפַשׁ); refreshed;

H5306.meaning: shining; a gem, probably the garnet
H5306.notes: Transliteration: nôphek; Pronunciation: no'-fek; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to glisten;

H5307.meaning: to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)
H5307.kjv: be accepted, cast (down, self, (lots), out), cease, die, divide (by lot), (let) fail, (cause to, let, make, ready to) fall (away, down, -en, -ing), fell(-ing), fugitive, have (inheritance), inferior, be judged (by mistake for H6419 (פָּלַל)), lay (along), (cause to) lie down, light (down), be ([idiom] hast) lost, lying, overthrow, overwhelm, perish, present(-ed, -ing), (make to) rot, slay, smite out, [idiom] surely, throw down.
H5307.notes: Transliteration: nâphal; Pronunciation: naw-fal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5308.meaning: to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)
H5308.notes: Transliteration: nᵉphal; Pronunciation: nef-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5307 (נָפַל)

H5309.notes: Transliteration: nephel; Pronunciation: neh'-fel; Derivation: or נֵפֶל; from H5307 (נָפַל);

H5310.meaning: to dash to pieces, or scatter
H5310.kjv: be beaten in sunder, break (in pieces), broken, dash (in pieces), cause to be discharged, dispersed, be overspread, scatter.
H5310.notes: Transliteration: nâphats; Pronunciation: naw-fats'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5311.notes: Transliteration: nephets; Pronunciation: neh'-fets; Derivation: from H5310 (נָפַץ);

H5312.meaning: to issue; causatively, to bring out
H5312.notes: Transliteration: nᵉphaq; Pronunciation: nef-ak'; Derivation: (Aramaic) a primitive root;

H5313.notes: Transliteration: niphqâʼ; Pronunciation: nif-kaw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H5312 (נְפַק);

H5314.meaning: to breathe; passively, to be breathed upon , i.e. (figuratively) refreshed (as if by a current of air)
H5314.notes: Transliteration: nâphash; Pronunciation: naw-fash'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5315.meaning: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)
H5315.kjv: any, appetite, beast, body, breath, creature, [idiom] dead(-ly), desire, [idiom] (dis-) contented, [idiom] fish, ghost, [phrase] greedy, he, heart(-y), (hath, [idiom] jeopardy of) life ([idiom] in jeopardy), lust, man, me, mind, mortally, one, own, person, pleasure, (her-, him-, my-, thy-) self, them (your) -selves, [phrase] slay, soul, [phrase] tablet, they, thing, ([idiom] she) will, [idiom] would have it.
H5315.notes: Transliteration: nephesh; Pronunciation: neh'-fesh; Derivation: from H5314 (נָפַשׁ);

H5316.notes: Transliteration: nepheth; Pronunciation: neh'-feth; Derivation: for H5299 (נָפָה);

H5317.meaning: a dripping i.e. of honey (from the comb)
H5317.notes: Transliteration: nôpheth; Pronunciation: no'-feth; Derivation: from H5130 (נוּף) in the sense of shaking to pieces;

H5318.notes: Transliteration: Nephtôwach; Pronunciation: nef-to'-akh; Derivation: from H6605 (פָּתַח); opened, i.e. a spring;

H5319.meaning: properly, wrestled; but used (in the plural) transitively, a struggle
H5319.notes: Transliteration: naphtûwl; Pronunciation: naf-tool'; Derivation: from H6617 (פָּתַל);

H5320.notes: Transliteration: Naphtuchîym; Pronunciation: naf-too-kheem; Derivation: plural of foreign origin,

H5321.meaning: Naphtali, a son of Jacob, with the tribe descended from him, and its territory
H5321.notes: Transliteration: Naphtâlîy; Pronunciation: naf-taw-lee'; Derivation: from H6617 (פָּתַל); my wrestling;

H5322.meaning: a flower (from its brilliancy); also a hawk (from it flashing speed)
H5322.notes: Transliteration: nêts; Pronunciation: nayts; Derivation: from H5340 (נָצַץ);

H5323.notes: Transliteration: nâtsâʼ; Pronunciation: naw-tsaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5324.meaning: to station, in various applications (literally or figuratively)
H5324.kjv: appointed, deputy, erect, establish, [idiom] Huzzah (by mistake for a proper name), lay, officer, pillar, present, rear up, set (over, up), settle, sharpen, establish, (make to) stand(-ing, still, up, upright), best state.
H5324.notes: Transliteration: nâtsab; Pronunciation: naw-tsab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5325.notes: Transliteration: nitstsâb; Pronunciation: nits-twawb'; Derivation: passive participle of H5324 (נָצַב);

H5326.notes: Transliteration: nitsbâh; Pronunciation: nits-baw'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5324 (נָצַב);

H5327.meaning: properly, to go forth, i.e. (by implication) to be expelled, and (consequently) desolate; causatively, to lay waste; also (specifically), to quarrel
H5327.notes: Transliteration: nâtsâh; Pronunciation: naw-tsaw'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5328.notes: Transliteration: nitstsâh; Pronunciation: nits-tsaw'; Derivation: feminine of H5322 (נֵץ);

H5329.meaning: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent
H5329.kjv: excel, chief musician (singer), oversee(-r), set forward.
H5329.notes: Transliteration: nâtsach; Pronunciation: naw-tsakh'; Derivation: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)

H5330.notes: Transliteration: nᵉtsach; Pronunciation: nets-akh'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5329 (נָצַח);

H5331.meaning: properly, a goal, i.e. the bright object at adistance travelled towards; hence (figuratively), splendor, or (subjectively) truthfulness, or (objectively) confidence; but usually (adverbially), continually (i.e. to the most distant point of view)
H5331.kjv: alway(-s), constantly, end, ([phrase] n-) ever(more), perpetual, strength, victory.
H5331.notes: Transliteration: netsach; Pronunciation: neh'-tsakh; Derivation: or נֵצַח; from H5329 (נָצַח);

H5332.meaning: juice of the grape (as blood red)
H5332.notes: Transliteration: Nêtsach; Pronunciation: nay'-tsakh; Derivation: probably identical with H5331 (נֶצַח), through the idea of brilliancy of color;

H5333.meaning: something stationary, i.e. a prefect, a military post, a statue
H5333.notes: Transliteration: nᵉtsîyb; Pronunciation: nets-eeb'; Derivation: or נְצִב; from H5324 (נָצַב);

H5334.notes: Transliteration: Nᵉtsîyb; Pronunciation: nets-eeb'; Derivation: the same as H5333 (נְצִיב); station;

H5335.notes: Transliteration: nᵉtsîyach; Pronunciation: nets-ee'-akh; Derivation: from H5329 (נָצַח); conspicuous;

H5336.meaning: properly, conservative; but used passively, delivered
H5336.notes: Transliteration: nâtsîyr; Pronunciation: naw-tsere'; Derivation: from H5341 (נָצַר);

H5337.meaning: to snatch away, whether in a good or a bad sense
H5337.kjv: [idiom] at all, defend, deliver (self), escape, [idiom] without fail, part, pluck, preserve, recover, rescue, rid, save, spoil, strip, [idiom] surely, take (out).
H5337.notes: Transliteration: nâtsal; Pronunciation: naw-tsal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5338.notes: Transliteration: nᵉtsal; Pronunciation: nets-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5337 (נָצַל);

H5339.notes: Transliteration: nitstsân; Pronunciation: nits-tsawn'; Derivation: from H5322 (נֵץ);

H5340.notes: Transliteration: nâtsats; Pronunciation: naw-tsats'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5341.meaning: to guard, in a good sense (to protect, maintain, obey, etc.) or a bad one (to conceal, etc.)
H5341.kjv: besieged, hidden thing, keep(-er, -ing), monument, observe, preserve(-r), subtil, watcher(-man).
H5341.notes: Transliteration: nâtsar; Pronunciation: naw-tsar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5342.notes: Transliteration: nêtser; Pronunciation: nay'-tser; Derivation: from H5341 (נָצַר) in the sense of greenness as a striking color;

H5343.notes: Transliteration: nᵉqêʼ; Pronunciation: nek-ay'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5352 (נָקָה);

H5344.meaning: to puncture, literally (to perforate, with more or less violence) or figuratively (to specify, designate, libel)
H5344.kjv: appoint, blaspheme, bore, curse, express, with holes, name, pierce, strike through.
H5344.notes: Transliteration: nâqab; Pronunciation: naw-kab'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5346.notes: Transliteration: Neqeb; Pronunciation: neh'-keb; Derivation: the same as H5345 (נֶקֶב); dell;

H5347.notes: Transliteration: nᵉqêbâh; Pronunciation: nek-ay-baw'; Derivation: from H5344 (נָקַב);

H5348.notes: Transliteration: nâqôd; Pronunciation: naw-kode'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to mark (by puncturing or branding);

H5349.meaning: a spotter (of sheep or cattle), i.e. the owner or tender (who thus marks them)
H5349.notes: Transliteration: nôqêd; Pronunciation: no-kade'; Derivation: active participle from the same as H5348 (נָקֹד);

H5350.meaning: a crumb (as broken to spots); also a biscuit (as pricked)
H5350.notes: Transliteration: niqqud; Pronunciation: nik-kood'; Derivation: from the same as H5348 (נָקֹד);

H5351.notes: Transliteration: nᵉquddâh; Pronunciation: ned-ood-daw'; Derivation: feminine of H5348 (נָקֹד);

H5352.meaning: to be (or make) clean (literally or figuratively); by implication (in an adverse sense) to be bare, i.e. extirpated
H5352.kjv: acquit [idiom] at all, [idiom] altogether, be blameless, cleanse, (be) clear(-ing), cut off, be desolate, be free, be (hold) guiltless, be (hold) innocent, [idiom] by no means, be quit, be (leave) unpunished, [idiom] utterly, [idiom] wholly.

H5849.notes: Transliteration: ʻâṭar; Pronunciation: aw-tar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5850.notes: Transliteration: ʻăṭârâh; Pronunciation: at-aw-raw'; Derivation: from H5849 (עָטַר);

H5851.notes: Transliteration: ʻĂṭârâh; Pronunciation: at-aw-raw'; Derivation: the same as H5850 (עֲטָרָה);

H5852.meaning: Ataroth, the name (thus simply) of two places in Palestine
H5852.notes: Transliteration: ʻĂṭârôwth; Pronunciation: at-aw-roth'; Derivation: or עֲטָרֹת; plural of H5850 (עֲטָרָה);

H5853.notes: Transliteration: ʻAṭrôwth ʼAddâr; Pronunciation: at-roth' ad-dawr'; Derivation: from the same as H5852 (עֲטָרוֹת) and H146 (אַדָּר); crowns of Addar;

H5854.notes: Transliteration: ʻAṭrôwth bêyth Yôwʼâb; Pronunciation: at-roth' bayth yoawb'; Derivation: from the same as H5852 (עֲטָרוֹת) and H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3097 (יוֹאָב); crowns of the house of Joab;

H5855.notes: Transliteration: ʻAṭrôwth Shôwphân; Pronunciation: at-roth' sho-fawn'

H5856.notes: Transliteration: ʻîy; Pronunciation: ee; Derivation: from H5753 (עָוָה);

H5857.meaning: Ai, Aja or Ajath, a place in Palestine
H5857.notes: Transliteration: ʻAy; Pronunciation: ah'ee; Derivation: or (feminine) עַיָּא; (Nehemiah 11:31), or עַיָּת; (Isaiah 10:28), for H5856 (עִי);

H5858.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊybâl; Pronunciation: ay-bawl'; Derivation: perhaps from an unused root probably meaning to be bald; bare;

H5859.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyôwn; Pronunciation: ee-yone'; Derivation: from H5856 (עִי); ruin;

H5860.meaning: to swoop down upon (literally or figuratively)
H5860.notes: Transliteration: ʻîyṭ; Pronunciation: eet; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5861.meaning: a hawk or other bird of prey
H5861.notes: Transliteration: ʻayiṭ; Pronunciation: ah'-yit; Derivation: from H5860 (עִיט);

H5862.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyṭâm; Pronunciation: ay-tawm'; Derivation: from H5861 (עַיִט); hawk-ground;

H5863.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyêy hâ-ʻĂbârîym; Pronunciation: ee-yay' haw-ab-aw-reem'

H5864.meaning: Ijim, a place in the Desert.
H5864.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyîym; Pronunciation: ee-yeem'; Derivation: plural of H5856 (עִי); ruins;

H5865.meaning: concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always
H5865.notes: Transliteration: ʻêylôwm; Pronunciation: ay-lome'; Derivation: for H5769 (עוֹלָם)

H5866.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎylay; Pronunciation: ee-lah'-ee; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה); elevated;

H5867.meaning: Elam, a son of Shem and his descendants, with their country; also of six Israelites
H5867.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊylâm; Pronunciation: ay-lawm'; Derivation: or עוֹלָם; (Ezra 10:2; Jeremiah 49:36), probably from H5956 (עָלַם); hidden, i.e. distant;

H5868.notes: Transliteration: ʻăyâm; Pronunciation: ah-yawm'; Derivation: of doubtful origin and authenticity;

H5869.meaning: an eye (literally or figuratively); by analogy, a fountain (as the eye of the landscape)
H5869.kjv: affliction, outward appearance, [phrase] before, [phrase] think best, colour, conceit, [phrase] be content, countenance, [phrase] displease, eye((-brow), (-d), -sight), face, [phrase] favour, fountain, furrow (from the margin), [idiom] him, [phrase] humble, knowledge, look, ([phrase] well), [idiom] me, open(-ly), [phrase] (not) please, presence, [phrase] regard, resemblance, sight, [idiom] thee, [idiom] them, [phrase] think, [idiom] us, well, [idiom] you(-rselves).
H5869.notes: Transliteration: ʻayin; Pronunciation: ah'-yin; Derivation: probably a primitive word;

H5870.notes: Transliteration: ʻayin; Pronunciation: ah'-yin; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5869 (עַיִן);

H5871.meaning: Ajin, the name (thus simply) of two places in Palestine
H5871.notes: Transliteration: ʻAyin; Pronunciation: ah'-yin; Derivation: the same as H5869 (עַיִן); fountain;

H5872.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Gedîy; Pronunciation: ane geh'-dee; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and H1423 (גְּדִי); fountain of a kid;

H5873.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Gannîym; Pronunciation: ane gan-neem'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and the plural of H1588 (גַּן); fountain of gardens;

H5874.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn-Dôʼr; Pronunciation: ane-dore'; Derivation: or עֵין דּוֹר; or עֵין־דֹּר; from H5869 (עַיִן) and H1755 (דּוֹר); fountain of dwelling;

H5875.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn haq-Qôwrêʼ; Pronunciation: ane-hak-ko-ray'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and the active participle of H7121 (קָרָא); fountain of One calling;

H5876.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Chaddâh; Pronunciation: ane khad-daw'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and the feminine of a derivative from H2300 (חָדַד); fountain of sharpness;

H5877.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Châtsôwr; Pronunciation: ane khaw-tsore'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and the same as H2674 (חָצוֹר); fountain of a village;

H5878.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Chărôd; Pronunciation: ane khar-ode'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and a derivative of H2729 (חָרַד); fountain of trembling;

H5879.meaning: Enajim or Enam, a place in Palestine
H5879.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊynayim; Pronunciation: ay-nah'-yim; Derivation: or עֵינָם; dual of H5869 (עַיִן); double fountain;

H5880.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Mishpâṭ; Pronunciation: ane mish-pawt'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and H4941 (מִשְׁפָּט); fountain of judgment;

H5881.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊynân; Pronunciation: ay-nawn'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן); having eyes;

H5882.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn ʻEglayim; Pronunciation: ane eg-lah'-yim; Derivation: עַיִן and the dual of H5695 (עֵגֶל); fountain of two calves;

H5883.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Rôgêl; Pronunciation: ane ro-gale'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and the active participle of H7270 (רָגַל); fountain of a traveller;

H5884.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Rimmôwn; Pronunciation: ane rim-mone'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and H7416 (רִמּוֹן); fountain of a pomegranate;

H5885.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Shemesh; Pronunciation: ane sheh'-mesh; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and H8121 (שֶׁמֶשׁ); fountain of the sun;

H5886.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Tannîym; Pronunciation: ane tan-neem'; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and the plural of H8565 (תַּן); fountain of jackals;

H5887.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyn Tappûwach; Pronunciation: ane tap-poo'-akh; Derivation: from H5869 (עַיִן) and H8598 (תַּפּוּחַ); fountain of an apple-tree;

H5888.notes: Transliteration: ʻâyêph; Pronunciation: aw-yafe'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5889.notes: Transliteration: ʻâyêph; Pronunciation: aw-yafe'; Derivation: from H5888 (עָיֵף);

H5890.notes: Transliteration: ʻêyphâh; Pronunciation: ay-faw'; Derivation: feminine from H5774 (עוּף);

H5891.meaning: Ephah, the name of a son of Midian, and of the region settled by him; also of an Israelite and of an Israelitess
H5891.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊyphâh; Pronunciation: ay-faw'; Derivation: the same as H5890 (עֵיפָה);

H5892.meaning: a city (a place guarded by waking or a watch) in the widest sense (even of a mere encampment or post)
H5892.kjv: Ai (from margin), city, court (from margin), town.
H5892.notes: Transliteration: ʻîyr; Pronunciation: eer; Derivation: or (in the plural) עָר; or עָיַר; (Judges 10:4), from H5782 (עוּר)

H5893.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyr; Pronunciation: eer; Derivation: the same as H5892 (עִיר);

H5894.meaning: a watcher, i.e. an angel (as guardian)
H5894.notes: Transliteration: ʻîyr; Pronunciation: eer; Derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5782 (עוּר);

H5895.meaning: properly, a young ass (as just broken to a load); hence an ass-colt
H5895.notes: Transliteration: ʻayir; Pronunciation: ah'-yeer; Derivation: from H5782 (עוּר) in the sense of raising (i.e. bearing a burden);

H5896.meaning: Ira, the name of three Israelites
H5896.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyrâʼ; Pronunciation: ee-raw'; Derivation: from H5782 (עוּר); wakefulness;

H5897.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyrâd; Pronunciation: ee-rawd'; Derivation: from the same as H6166 (עֲרָד); fugitive;

H5898.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyr ham-Melach; Pronunciation: eer ham-meh'-lakh; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר) and H4417 (מֶלַח) with the article of substance interp.; city of (the) salt;

H5899.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyr hat-Tᵉmârîym; Pronunciation: eer hat-tem-aw-reem'; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר) and the plural of H8558 (תָּמָר) with the article interpolated; city of the palmtrees;

H5900.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyrûw; Pronunciation: ee-roo'; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר); a citizen;

H5901.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyrîy; Pronunciation: ee-ree'; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר); urbane;

H5902.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyrâm; Pronunciation: ee-rawm'; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר); city-wise;

H5903.notes: Transliteration: ʻêyrôm; Pronunciation: ay-rome'; Derivation: or עֵרֹם; from H6191 (עָרַם);

H5904.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyr Nâchâsh; Pronunciation: eer naw-khawsh'; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר) and H5175 (נָחָשׁ); city of a serpent;

H5905.notes: Transliteration: ʻÎyr Shemesh; Pronunciation: eer sheh'-mesh; Derivation: from H5892 (עִיר) and H8121 (שֶׁמֶשׁ); city of the sun;

H5906.meaning: the constellation of the Great Bear (perhaps from its migration through the heavens)
H5906.notes: Transliteration: ʻAyish; Pronunciation: ah'-yish; Derivation: or עָשׁ; from H5789 (עוּשׁ);

H5907.meaning: Akbor, the name of an Idumaean and of two Israelites
H5907.notes: Transliteration: ʻAkbôwr; Pronunciation: ak-bore'; Derivation: probably for H5909 (עַכְבָּר);

H5908.notes: Transliteration: ʻakkâbîysh; Pronunciation: ak-kaw-beesh'; Derivation: probably from an unused root in the literal sense of entangling;

H5909.notes: Transliteration: ʻakbâr; Pronunciation: ak-bawr'; Derivation: probably from the same as H5908 (עַכָּבִישׁ) in the secondary sense of attacking;

H5910.meaning: Akko (from its situation on a bay)
H5910.notes: Transliteration: ʻAkkôw; Pronunciation: ak-ko'; Derivation: apparently from an unused root meaning to hem in;

H5911.meaning: Akor, the name of a place in Palestine
H5911.notes: Transliteration: ʻÂkôwr; Pronunciation: aw-kore'; Derivation: from H5916 (עָכַר); troubled;

H5912.notes: Transliteration: ʻÂkân; Pronunciation: aw-kawn'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to trouble; troublesome;

H5913.notes: Transliteration: ʻâkaç; Pronunciation: aw-kas'; Derivation: a primitive root; properly, to tie, specifically, with fetters; but used only as denominative from H5914 (עֶכֶס);

H5914.notes: Transliteration: ʻekeç; Pronunciation: eh'-kes; Derivation: from H5913 (עָכַס);

H5915.notes: Transliteration: ʻAkçâh; Pronunciation: ak-saw'; Derivation: feminine of H5914 (עֶכֶס); anklet;

H5916.meaning: properly, to roil water; figuratively, to disturb or affict
H5916.notes: Transliteration: ʻâkar; Pronunciation: aw-kar'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5917.notes: Transliteration: ʻÂkâr; Pronunciation: aw-kawr'; Derivation: from H5916 (עָכַר); troublesome;

H5918.notes: Transliteration: ʻOkrân; Pronunciation: ok-rawn'; Derivation: from H5916 (עָכַר); muddler;

H5919.notes: Transliteration: ʻakshûwb; Pronunciation: ak-shoob'; Derivation: probably from an unused root meaning to coil;

H5920.meaning: properly, the top; specifically, the highest (i.e. God); also (adverb) aloft, to Jehovah
H5920.notes: Transliteration: ʻal; Pronunciation: al; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5921.meaning: above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
H5921.kjv: above, according to(-ly), after, (as) against, among, and, [idiom] as, at, because of, beside (the rest of), between, beyond the time, [idiom] both and, by (reason of), [idiom] had the charge of, concerning for, in (that), (forth, out) of, (from) (off), (up-) on, over, than, through(-out), to, touching, [idiom] with.

H5922.meaning: above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
H5922.kjv: about, against, concerning, for, (there-) fore, from, in, [idiom] more, of, (there-, up-) on, (in-) to, [phrase] why with.
H5922.notes: Transliteration: ʻal; Pronunciation: al; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5921 (עַל)

H5923.meaning: a yoke (as imposed on the neck), literally or figuratively
H5923.notes: Transliteration: ʻôl; Pronunciation: ole; Derivation: or עוֹל; from H5953 (עָלַל);

H5924.notes: Transliteration: ʻêllâʼ; Pronunciation: ale-law'; Derivation: (Aramaic) from H5922 (עַל);

H5925.notes: Transliteration: ʻUllâʼ; Pronunciation: ool-law'; Derivation: feminine of H5923 (עֹל); burden;

H5926.notes: Transliteration: ʻillêg; Pronunciation: il-layg'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to stutter;

H5927.meaning: to ascend, intransitively (be high) or actively (mount); used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative
H5927.kjv: arise (up), (cause to) ascend up, at once, break (the day) (up), bring (up), (cause to) burn, carry up, cast up, [phrase] shew, climb (up), (cause to, make to) come (up), cut off, dawn, depart, exalt, excel, fall, fetch up, get up, (make to) go (away, up); grow (over) increase, lay, leap, levy, lift (self) up, light, (make) up, [idiom] mention, mount up, offer, make to pay, [phrase] perfect, prefer, put (on), raise, recover, restore, (make to) rise (up), scale, set (up), shoot forth (up), (begin to) spring (up), stir up, take away (up), work.
H5927.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlâh; Pronunciation: aw-law'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5928.notes: Transliteration: ʻălâh; Pronunciation: al-law'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5930 (עֹלָה);

H5929.meaning: a leaf (as coming up on a tree); collectively, foliage
H5929.notes: Transliteration: ʻâleh; Pronunciation: aw-leh'; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5930.meaning: a step or (collectively, stairs, as ascending); usually a holocaust (as going up in smoke)
H5930.kjv: ascent, burnt offering (sacrifice), go up to. See also H5766 (עֶוֶל).
H5930.notes: Transliteration: ʻôlâh; Pronunciation: o-law'; Derivation: or עוֹלָה; feminine active participle of H5927 (עָלָה);

H5931.notes: Transliteration: ʻillâh; Pronunciation: il-law'; Derivation: (Aramaic) feminine from a root corresponding to H5927 (עָלָה);

H5932.notes: Transliteration: ʻalvâh; Pronunciation: al-vaw'; Derivation: for H5766 (עֶוֶל);

H5933.notes: Transliteration: ʻAlvâh; Pronunciation: al-vaw'; Derivation: or עַליָה; the same as H5932 (עַלְוָה);

H5934.meaning: (only in plural as abstract) adolescence; figuratively, vigor
H5934.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlûwm; Pronunciation: aw-loom'; Derivation: passive participle of H5956 (עָלַם) in the denominative sense of H5958 (עֶלֶם);

H5935.notes: Transliteration: ʻAlvân; Pronunciation: al-vawn'; Derivation: or עַלְיָן; from H5927 (עָלָה); lofty;

H5936.notes: Transliteration: ʻălûwqâh; Pronunciation: al-oo-kaw'; Derivation: feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to suck;

H5937.meaning: to jump for joy, i.e. exult
H5937.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlaz; Pronunciation: aw-laz'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5938.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlêz; Pronunciation: aw-laze'; Derivation: from H5937 (עָלַז);

H5939.notes: Transliteration: ʻălâṭâh; Pronunciation: al-aw-taw'; Derivation: feminine from an unused root meaning to cover;

H5940.notes: Transliteration: ʻĕlîy; Pronunciation: el-ee'; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5941.notes: Transliteration: ʻÊlîy; Pronunciation: ay-lee'; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה); lofty;

H5942.notes: Transliteration: ʻillîy; Pronunciation: il-lee'; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5943.notes: Transliteration: ʻillay; Pronunciation: il-lah'-ee; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5942 (עִלִּי);

H5944.meaning: something lofty, i.e. a stair-way; also a second-story room (or even one on the roof); figuratively, the sky
H5944.kjv: ascent, (upper) chamber, going up, loft, parlour.
H5944.notes: Transliteration: ʻălîyâh; Pronunciation: al-ee-yaw'; Derivation: feminine from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5945.meaning: an elevation, i.e. (adj.) lofty (compar.); as title, the Supreme
H5945.notes: Transliteration: ʻelyôwn; Pronunciation: el-yone'; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5946.notes: Transliteration: ʻelyôwn; Pronunciation: el-yone'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5945 (עֶלְיוֹן);

H5947.notes: Transliteration: ʻallîyz; Pronunciation: al-leez'; Derivation: from H5937 (עָלַז);

H5948.meaning: probably a crucible (as working over the metal)
H5948.notes: Transliteration: ʻălîyl; Pronunciation: al-eel'; Derivation: from H5953 (עָלַל) in the sense of completing;

H5949.meaning: an exploit (of God), or a performance (of man, often in a bad sense); by implication, an opportunity
H5949.kjv: act(-ion), deed, doing, invention, occasion, work.
H5949.notes: Transliteration: ʻălîylâh; Pronunciation: al-ee-law'; Derivation: or עֲלִלָה; from H5953 (עָלַל) in the sense of effecting;

H5950.notes: Transliteration: ʻălîylîyâh; Pronunciation: al-ee-lee-yaw'; Derivation: for H5949 (עֲלִילָה);

H5951.notes: Transliteration: ʻălîytsûwth; Pronunciation: al-ee-tsooth'; Derivation: from H5970 (עָלַץ);

H5952.notes: Transliteration: ʻallîyth; Pronunciation: al-leeth'; Derivation: from H5927 (עָלָה);

H5953.meaning: to effect thoroughly; specifically, to glean (also figuratively); by implication (in a bad sense) to overdo, i.e. maltreat, be saucy to, pain, impose (also literal)
H5953.kjv: abuse, affect, [idiom] child, defile, do, glean, mock, practise, thoroughly, work (wonderfully).
H5953.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlal; Pronunciation: aw-lal'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5954.kjv: bring in, come in, go in.
H5954.notes: Transliteration: ʻălal; Pronunciation: al-al'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5953 (עָלַל) (in the sense of thrusting oneself in),

H5955.meaning: only in plural gleanings; by extension gleaning-time
H5955.notes: Transliteration: ʻôlêlâh; Pronunciation: o-lay-law'; Derivation: feminine active participle of H5953 (עָלַל);

H5956.meaning: to veil from sight, i.e. conceal (literally or figuratively)
H5956.kjv: [idiom] any ways, blind, dissembler, hide (self), secret (thing).
H5956.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlam; Pronunciation: aw-lam'; Derivation: a primitive root;

H5957.meaning: remote time, i.e. the future or past indefinitely; often adverb, forever
H5957.notes: Transliteration: ʻâlam; Pronunciation: aw-lam'; Derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5769 (עוֹלָם);

H5958.meaning: properly, something kept out of sight, i.e. a lad
H5958.notes: Transliteration: ʻelem; Pronunciation: eh'-lem; Derivation: from H5956 (עָלַם); (compare H5959 (עַלְמָה))

H5959.notes: Transliteration: ʻalmâh; Pronunciation: al-maw'; Derivation: feminine of H5958 (עֶלֶם);

H5960.notes: Transliteration: ʻAlmôwn; Pronunciation: al-mone'; Derivation: from H5956 (עָלַם); hidden; See also H5963 (עַלְמֹן דִּבְלָתָיְמָה).

H5961.meaning: properly, girls, i.e. the soprano or female voice, perhaps falsetto
H5961.notes: Transliteration: ʻĂlâmôwth; Pronunciation: al-aw-moth'; Derivation: plural of H5959 (עַלְמָה);

H5962.meaning: an Elamite or inhabitant of Elam
H5962.notes: Transliteration: ʻAlmîy; Pronunciation: al-mee'; Derivation: (Aramaic) patrial from a name corresponding to H5867 (עֵילָם) contracted;

H5963.notes: Transliteration: ʻAlmôn Diblâthâyᵉmâh; Pronunciation: al-mone' dib-lawthaw'-yem-aw

H5964.meaning: Alemeth, the name of a place in Palestine and of two Israelites

H8588.notes: Transliteration: taʻănûwg; Pronunciation: tah-an-oog'; Derivation: or תַּעֲנֻג; and (feminine) תַּעֲנֻגָה; from H6026 (עָנַג);

H8592.notes: Transliteration: taʻătsumâh; Pronunciation: tah-ats-oo-maw'; Derivation: from H6105 (עָצַם);

H8593.meaning: a knife or razor (as making bare); also a scabbard (as being bare, i.e. empty)

H8595.notes: Transliteration: taʻtuaʻ; Pronunciation: tah-too'-ah; Derivation: from H8591 (תָּעַע);

H8602.notes: Transliteration: tâphêl; Pronunciation: taw-fale'; Derivation: from an unused root meaning to smear;

H8611.notes: Transliteration: tôpheth; Pronunciation: to'-feth; Derivation: from the base of H8608 (תָּפַף);

H8612.notes: Transliteration: Tôpheth; Pronunciation: to'-feth; Derivation: the same as H8611 (תֹּפֶת);

H8613.notes: Transliteration: Tophteh; Pronunciation: tof-teh'; Derivation: probably a form of H8612 (תֹּפֶת);

H8614.notes: Transliteration: tiphtay; Pronunciation: tif-tah'-ee; Derivation: (Aramaic) perhaps from H8199 (שָׁפַט);

H8646.notes: Transliteration: Terach; Pronunciation: teh'-rakh; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H8647.notes: Transliteration: Tirchănâh; Pronunciation: teer-khan-aw'; Derivation: of uncertain derivation;

H8649.notes: Transliteration: tormâh; Pronunciation: tor-maw'; Derivation: and תַּרְמוּת; or תַּרְמִית; from H7411 (רָמָה);

H8650.meaning: a pole (as a mast or flagstaff)

H8651.meaning: a door; by implication, a palace

H8653.notes: Transliteration: tarʻêlâh; Pronunciation: tar-ay-law'; Derivation: from H7477 (רָעַל);

H8654.notes: Transliteration: Tirʻâthîy; Pronunciation: teer-aw-thee'; Derivation: patrial from an unused name meaning gate;

H8660.notes: Transliteration: Tirshâthâʼ; Pronunciation: teer-shaw-thaw'; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H8661.notes: Transliteration: Tartân; Pronunciation: tar-tawn'; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H8662.meaning: Tartak, a deity of the Avvites
H8662.notes: Transliteration: Tartâq; Pronunciation: tar-tawk'; Derivation: of foreign derivation;

H8663.notes: Transliteration: tᵉshuʼâh; Pronunciation: tesh-oo-aw'; Derivation: from H7722 (שׁוֹא);

H8665.notes: Transliteration: tashbêts; Pronunciation: tash-bates'; Derivation: from H7660 (שָׁבַץ);

H8667.notes: Transliteration: tᵉsûwmeth; Pronunciation: tes-oo-meth'; Derivation: from H7760 (שׂוּם);

H8671.notes: Transliteration: tᵉshîyʻîy; Pronunciation: tesh-ee-ee'; Derivation: ordinal from H8672 (תֵּשַׁע);

H8673.notes: Transliteration: tishʻîym; Pronunciation: tish-eem'; Derivation: multiple from H8672 (תֵּשַׁע);

H8674.notes: Transliteration: Tattᵉnay; Pronunciation: tat-ten-ah'-ee; Derivation: of foreign derivation;
